Ostertorte.name: Easter Pie
ingredients: 2 tbsp olive oil + oil for brushing
500g ricotta (Italian fresh cheese, or quark as substitute)
steps: Place 500g flour with a pinch of salt on a pastry board, mix in 2 tbsp oil and knead with enough water (about 250ml) to form a smooth, supple dough. Knead vigorously for about 10 minutes.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces, shape into small balls and place on a flour-dusted cloth. Cover with a slightly damp cloth and leave to rest for about 1 hour.

For the filling, trim and wash the spinach. Place it still wet in a pot, salt, and bring briefly to the boil. Drain the leaves and leave to cool, then squeeze out firmly. Finely chop the spinach and season with salt, pepper, and marjoram.

Cut the bread roll into small pieces and soak in about 100ml milk. Beat 2 eggs and stir in 2–3 tbsp Parmesan. Stir in the lightly squeezed bread roll along with the ricotta or well-drained quark, then gently fold in the spinach. Season generously.
Roll out one dough ball on a floured surface as thinly as possible, then carefully stretch it even thinner with your hands in all directions (similar to strudel dough).

Oil a springform pan (26cm), lay the dough sheet in so that it overlaps the edge of the pan by about 1cm. Brush lightly with oil. Roll out 5 more dough portions in the same way and place them one by one into the pan. Brush each layer with oil, except the last one.

Spread the prepared filling over the dough, smooth it out, and drizzle with a little oil. Use the back of a spoon to press six evenly spaced hollows into the filling. Place a small knob of butter in each. Carefully crack 6 eggs one at a time and slide them into the hollows without breaking the yolks. Season the eggs with salt and pepper and sprinkle with the remaining cheese. Preheat the oven to 200°C.

Roll out the remaining dough portions, stretch them thin, and lay them one by one into the pan, brushing each with a thin layer of oil and placing small knobs of butter around the edge. Roll the overhanging dough edges inward and press down.

Brush the top surface with oil and carefully prick a few holes to allow steam to escape. Place in the preheated oven and bake for about 75 minutes until golden brown. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Ostertorte.tips: Do not serve straight from the fridge — this pie tastes best slightly warm!

Pikanter Kuchen mit Sardellen.name: Savory Anchovy Flatbread
ingredients: 4 tbsp olive oil + oil for the baking sheet
steps: Mound 500g flour on a work surface and make a well in the centre. Dissolve 30g yeast in 4 tbsp lukewarm milk and add to the well. Work in 1/2 tsp salt, 4 tbsp olive oil, and about 250ml lukewarm water until a smooth dough forms. Cover with a cloth and leave to rise in a warm place for 1 hour.

Topping: Briefly blanch and peel the tomatoes, then chop them. Finely chop 2 onions and sauté in 4 tbsp hot olive oil. Add the tomatoes and simmer uncovered over a gentle heat until all the liquid has evaporated. Rinse 100g anchovy fillets, chop finely, and stir in. Preheat the oven to 175°C.

Oil a large baking sheet, spread the dough over it with your hands and press flat. Spread the tomato mixture on top. Slice the garlic and scatter over the dough along with 100g olives and oregano. Bake in the oven for 45 minutes (gas: mark 2). Scatter fresh basil over the top before serving.

Brotfladen mit Käse.name: Cheese Flatbread
ingredients: 250g fresh goat's cheese (or very fresh blue cheese)
steps: Mix 200g flour with 50ml olive oil and knead on a pastry board. Add enough cold water to form a smooth dough. Shape into a ball, wrap in a plastic bag, and leave to rest for about 1 hour.

Knead the dough vigorously for a few minutes, shape into two balls, and leave to rest on the pastry board for another 5 minutes. Roll out one portion of dough thinly with a rolling pin, then carefully stretch it with your hands in all directions as thinly as possible. Preheat the oven to its highest setting.
Line a well-greased, as large as possible round baking pan with the dough sheet (in Liguria, pans of 50cm diameter or larger are used). Press the edges down.

Dice the goat's cheese and distribute over the dough. Cover with the second dough sheet, stretched equally thin, and press the edges together. Use a fingertip to press coin-sized indentations into the surface at several points. Sprinkle the dough surface with salt and drizzle with the remaining oil. Spread the oil over the dough with the palm of your hand, lightly crushing the pieces of cheese as you do so.
Bake in the very hot oven for about 10 minutes (gas: mark 6) until the surface is nicely browned. Cut into large pieces and serve immediately.
Brotfladen mit Käse.tips: If fresh goat's cheese is not available, blue cheese can be used instead. Ideally, bake in a wood-fired oven.

Mangold-Artischocken-Torte.name: Swiss Chard and Artichoke Pie
ingredients: 12 young, tender artichokes (or canned artichoke hearts as substitute)
5 tbsp olive oil + oil for brushing
steps: Dough: Mound 500g flour on a pastry board, add 1 tsp salt, 4 tbsp olive oil, and 250ml cold water. Knead everything together thoroughly until a smooth, uniform dough forms. Cover and leave to rest.
Filling: Wash the Swiss chard and cut out the stalks. Tear the leaves apart and place them still wet in a hot pot, allowing them to wilt. Squeeze out and chop finely.

Trim the artichokes, removing the tough outer leaves and tips, and pull out the chokes. Slice the artichokes thinly. Finely chop 1 onion. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil and sauté the onion and artichokes (if using canned artichoke hearts, add them only at the very end). Mix in the Swiss chard and sauté briefly.

Melt 50g butter, toast 2 tbsp breadcrumbs in it, and add the buttered breadcrumbs to the artichoke mixture. Add 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp marjoram, salt, and pepper, mix everything thoroughly and adjust seasoning. Preheat the oven to 200°C. Oil a round, shallow pan (26cm diameter).

Halve the dough and roll out two thin circles; line the pan with one of them. Sprinkle with 1 tbsp breadcrumbs. Spread the filling on top to a thickness of about 2cm. Cover with the second dough sheet and press the edges together. If desired, use any leftover dough to form a thin rope and press it around the edge.
Brush the surface of the pie with oil and prick a few holes with a fork. Place in the preheated oven (gas: mark 3) and bake for 55 minutes. Serve warm or cold.

Gefüllte Teigtäschchen.name: Stuffed Pasta Parcels
ingredients: 150g fresh borage (or parsley and basil as substitute)
steps: For the dough, mound 400g flour on a wooden board and make a well in the centre. Crack 4 eggs one at a time into the well and add salt. Mix the flour from the edges into the eggs and knead well until the dough is smooth and elastic. Shape into a ball, cover with a damp cloth, and leave to rest.
Filling: Wash the Swiss chard and borage leaves, then blanch in boiling salted water for about 3 minutes. Rinse under cold water, drain, squeeze out well, and chop finely. Place in a bowl.
Blanch the veal sweetbreads in boiling salted water for 5 minutes, then refresh in cold water. Remove the membranes and ducts and cut into small cubes. Add to the vegetables.
Grate the hard crust of 1 bread roll into breadcrumbs; soak the soft white crumb in 100ml veal stock.

Add the minced veal and forcemeat to the vegetables in the bowl. Thoroughly mix in 2 eggs, the grated breadcrumbs, the squeezed bread crumb, and 3 tbsp Parmesan with a wooden spoon. Season generously with 1/2 tsp marjoram, salt, and pepper.

Halve the dough ball and roll both pieces out thinly on a lightly floured surface (2mm thick). Cut into 5cm-wide strips with a pastry wheel. On half of the strips, place small portions of filling at intervals of about 3cm using a teaspoon. Carefully lay the remaining strips on top and cut out the ravioli, pressing the edges gently together. Spread out on a floured cloth and leave to dry briefly.

Bring 3 litres of salted water to the boil. Add the ravioli to the bubbling water and, once it returns to the boil, leave to cook for 3–4 minutes. Melt 50g butter and stir in 1 tsp fresh marjoram leaves. Drain the ravioli well, drizzle with the marjoram butter, and sprinkle with 50g Parmesan.
Gefüllte Teigtäschchen.tips: If you are making a larger batch of ravioli, it is worth supplementing the listed ingredients with veal brain and spleen.

Nudeln mit Pesto.name: Pasta with Pesto
ingredients: 400g trenette (flat spaghetti from Liguria) or narrow ribbon pasta
steps: Wipe the leaves of 1 large bunch of basil clean — do not wash them. Lightly toast 2 tbsp pine nuts in a dry pan. Roughly chop 3 garlic cloves. Grind all three ingredients in a mortar with a pinch of salt to form a paste (or blend finely in a food processor).
Transfer to a bowl and stir in 125ml olive oil and 50g freshly grated cheese alternately, a tablespoon at a time, until a creamy sauce forms.
Bring 2–3 litres of salted water to the boil in a large pot and cook 400g trenette until al dente. Stir 3–4 tbsp of the hot pasta water into the pesto.
Drain the trenette and toss with three-quarters of the sauce in a warmed bowl. Spoon the remaining pesto over the top before serving.
Nudeln mit Pesto.tips: Trenette are long, thin, flat noodles — the closest thing to spaghetti — known by this name only in Liguria. And pesto? Without question one of the most remarkable combinations of basil, garlic, and olive oil there is!

Gefüllte Teigecken mit Nusssauce.name: Stuffed Pasta Triangles with Walnut Sauce
ingredients: 100g walnut kernels + walnut kernels to garnish
steps: Filling: Wash the herbs. Briefly bring the borage to the boil in a little salted water. Drain, squeeze out. Chop all the herbs very finely.
Press 2 garlic cloves and mix thoroughly with the herbs, 1 egg, 100g ricotta, and 20g Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.

Dough: Knead 300g flour, 125ml white wine, a pinch of salt, and a little water into a firm, elastic dough. Leave to rest covered for 20 minutes. Then roll out on a lightly floured surface (2–3mm thick) and cut into squares of 5–6cm.
Place a portion of filling on each square of dough. Brush the edges with water, fold into triangles, and press the edges gently together.

Sauce: Briefly blanch 100g walnut kernels and remove the thin skin. Soak 4 tbsp bread crumb in water and squeeze out well. Grind with the walnuts and a little salt in a mortar to form a smooth cream (or process in a blender). Pass through a sieve, then stir in 4 tbsp olive oil and 4 tbsp buttermilk a tablespoon at a time.

Bring plenty of salted water to the boil. Cook the pansoòti for about 15 minutes, drain, and toss with the walnut sauce. Serve hot, sprinkle with the remaining Parmesan, and garnish with halved walnut kernels if desired.
Gefüllte Teigecken mit Nusssauce.tips: What makes this dish special is also the dough itself: it is made with wine. Add fresh borage and finely ground walnuts — and you have a wonderfully composed combination of flavours.

Nocken mit Pilzsauce.name: Pasta Twists with Mushroom Sauce
ingredients: 200g fresh porcini mushrooms (or 20g dried, soaked for about 2 hours)
steps: Dough: Knead 350g wheat flour, 50g wheat bran, 1/2 tsp salt, and about 250ml lukewarm water into a smooth dough, cover with a damp cloth, and leave to rest for 20 minutes.
Sauce: Clean the fresh mushrooms, wipe them carefully, and slice thinly. Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed. Chop the flesh. Finely chop 1 onion and 1 garlic clove.

Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a pan. Sauté the onion and garlic, stir in the tomatoes and 2 tbsp tomato paste, season with salt and pepper. Reduce over high heat, then stir in the mushrooms and braise covered over the lowest heat.
Cut off small pieces of dough and roll into pencil-thin cylinders about 5cm long. Wind each one spirally around a thick knitting needle, slide carefully off, and leave to dry briefly.

Bring 2–3 litres of salted water to the boil in a large pot. Add the pasta twists in batches and leave to cook briefly. As soon as they rise to the surface, lift them out with a slotted spoon, drain, and place in a warmed bowl.
Mix in 30g melted butter and half the Parmesan. If necessary, thin the mushroom sauce with a little hot pasta water and pour over the pasta. Serve the remaining Parmesan on the side.
Nocken mit Pilzsauce.tips: Trofie are a gnocchi speciality known by this name only in Genoa. There they are traditionally served with pesto — here offered as a variation with a warm vegetable sauce.

Gemüsesuppe mit Pesto.name: Vegetable Soup with Pesto
ingredients: 4 tbsp olive oil + olive oil for drizzling
steps: Shell fresh beans; if using dried, soak overnight in water. Wash and peel or trim the potatoes, carrots, leeks, celery, zucchini, and onion, then slice them.

Bring 2 litres of water to a boil in a large pot and salt it. Add the beans and prepared vegetables. (If using dried beans, cook them alone for 15 minutes first, then add the remaining vegetables.) Stir in 4 tbsp olive oil, cover the pot, and simmer on the lowest heat for 30 minutes until you have a thick soup.

Briefly blanch 2 tomatoes, peel and deseed them, then coarsely chop the flesh. Wash and chop the borage leaves. After 30 minutes, add both to the vegetables and cook for a further 15 minutes. Shortly before the end of cooking time, bring the soup back to a full boil, add 150g of soup pasta, and cook until al dente.

Mix 2 tbsp pesto with a ladleful of hot broth and stir it into the soup. Serve immediately — drizzle with a little fresh olive oil at the table, add freshly ground pepper, and sprinkle with 4 tbsp grated Parmesan. Garnish with the decorative borage flowers if available.
Gemüsesuppe mit Pesto.tips: If you can't find borlotti beans, simply use pinto beans or speckled kidney beans — recognisable by their red-speckled pattern.

Muschelsuppe.name: Mussel Soup
steps: Thoroughly clean the mussels and scrub them under running water. Discard any that are already open.
Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a large pot and press in 2 cloves of garlic. Coarsely chop 1 bunch of parsley and sauté half of it together with the garlic.
Add the cleaned mussels — still dripping wet — and cook until the shells open (about 5 minutes). Pour in 200 ml white wine and 125 ml broth, season with salt, and finish cooking for 10 minutes.

Knead 1 tbsp butter together with 1 tbsp flour, dissolve the mixture in the broth, and stir until smooth. Taste and adjust the seasoning of the broth. Cut the lemon into eighths and toast the white bread. Divide only the opened mussels among soup bowls, drizzle with broth, and scatter over the remaining parsley. Serve with lemon wedges and toasted white bread.
Muschelsuppe.tips: Variation: At the end, stir in 500g peeled and puréed ripe tomatoes, let the sauce bubble vigorously, and pour it hot over the mussels.

Brühe mit Kräuteromelette.name: Broth with Herb Omelette
ingredients: 1 bunch mixed herbs (basil, chives, parsley, borage, thyme)
steps: Trim and wash the endive, blanch in boiling salted water for 2 minutes, refresh in cold water, and drain well.

Finely chop the herbs and 1 onion. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a pan and sauté the herbs and onion. Squeeze the endive firmly, then cut into thin strips. Add three-quarters of it to the pan along with the onion and herbs and cook for 10 minutes. Season with 1 tsp lemon juice, salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg.
Whisk 3 eggs with 3 tbsp Parmesan. Pour over the vegetables, gently mix, and allow to set over very low heat. Remove from the pan and let cool slightly. Bring 1 litre of beef broth to a boil.
Cut the omelette into thin strips. Place them in the broth along with the remaining endive strips, heat through, and serve immediately.
Brühe mit Kräuteromelette.tips: If you can get hold of plenty of fresh borage, use it in place of the endive in this soup!

Gemischter Fisch-Eintopf (Toskana).name: Mixed Fish Stew (Tuscany)
ingredients: 1.5 kg mixed fish (e.g. cod, halibut, mackerel)
steps: Gut the fish, rinse under running water, and cut into bite-sized pieces. Wash and scrub the mussels vigorously; discard any that are already open.
Finely chop 2 celery stalks, 1/2 onion, and 2 cloves of garlic. Sauté in a casserole. Add water and simmer for 30 minutes to make a stock, then pass through a fine sieve.
Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a large casserole. Sauté the squid, then add some crushed and blanched tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper.
First, place the firm-fleshed fish (mackerel, red mullet, halibut) into the tomato sauce. Pour in the stock and gently cook for 10 minutes.
Finally, add the mussels, seafood, and shrimp and cook for a further 10–15 minutes. Finely chop the parsley, scatter over the dish, and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Toast the white bread slices in the oven and brush with garlic and olive oil. Serve alongside the fish stew.
Gemischter Fisch-Eintopf (Toskana).tips: Wine suggestion: A dry Bianco di Pitigliano or another dry white wine from the region.

Stockfisch-Eintopf (Ligurien).name: Salt Cod Stew (Liguria)
steps: Soak the stockfish in water for 24 hours, changing the water 1–2 times. Rinse the fish well, then remove the skin and bones and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Blanch, peel, and deseed the tomatoes, then chop roughly. Finely chop the celery stalk, 1 carrot, 1 onion, and 2 cloves of garlic.

Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a casserole. Sauté the celery, carrot, onion, and garlic. Stir in the stockfish and briefly fry. Add the tomatoes and 1 litre of broth, season with a little salt and pepper. Cover and braise over low heat for 1 hour.

Peel the potatoes and cut into roughly 1 cm cubes. Add them to the fish after 1 hour and cook for a further 30 minutes. Taste the finished stew and adjust seasoning, then scatter over the parsley leaves, basil, and 3 tbsp pine nuts.

Marinierte Sardinen (Ligurien/Toskana).name: Marinated Sardines (Liguria/Tuscany)
steps: Gut the fresh sardines, wash well, and cut off the heads. Pat the sardines dry, drizzle with the juice of 1/2 lemon, and dust lightly on both sides with 2 tbsp flour. Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a large pan and fry the fish for about 2 minutes on each side.

For the marinade: very finely chop 2 cloves of garlic and 1 small onion. Place in a saucepan with 125 ml white wine, 125 ml white wine vinegar, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp dried rosemary, and 1/2 tsp salt. Bring to a vigorous boil for 2 minutes. Finely chop the parsley and cut the basil into strips. Remove the liquid from the heat and stir in the fresh herbs, then season with pepper.
Slice the remaining 1/2 lemon. Layer the fried sardines and lemon slices in a dish. Pour the herb marinade over them, cover, and leave to marinate briefly.
Marinierte Sardinen (Ligurien/Toskana).tips: Also suitable as a warm or cold starter for 6–8 people.

Geschmorter Tintenfisch (Ligurien).name: Braised Octopus (Liguria)
ingredients: 125 ml (1/8 l) fish broth (or ready-made fish stock)
steps: Soak 20g dried mushrooms in 250 ml white wine. Thoroughly clean and wash the octopus, then remove the skin. Cut the tentacles and head away from the body (mantle), remove the eyes, beak, and innards. Cut the mantle into strips about 1/2 cm wide.
Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed them. Finely chop 2 onions and 3 cloves of garlic.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a wide pan. Sauté the onion and garlic. Drain the mushrooms, reserving the wine liquid. Chop the mushrooms and briefly fry them. Add the tomatoes and reserved wine liquid, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the octopus, cover, and braise gently for about 50 minutes until tender. Gradually add the fish broth, stirring well.
Finely chop the parsley and cut the basil into strips. Taste and adjust the seasoning of the octopus and scatter the fresh herbs over the top.

Meerbarben mit Tomaten (Toskana).name: Red Mullet with Tomatoes (Tuscany)
steps: For the sauce, briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel and deseed them, then coarsely chop. Finely chop the celery stalks and 3 cloves of garlic. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot, sauté the celery and garlic. Add the tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for about 15 minutes. Add a few tablespoons of water if needed.
Wash and pat the red mullet dry. Dust lightly with 2–3 tbsp flour. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a pan and fry the fish for 1 minute on each side.

Pass the tomato sauce through a sieve and return it to the pot. Transfer the briefly fried red mullet from the pan into the tomato sauce. Allow to finish cooking gently for about 5 minutes. Garnish with parsley leaves and adjust seasoning to taste.
Meerbarben mit Tomaten (Toskana).tips: Red mullet — sometimes called the woodcock of the sea — have no gallbladder, which means smaller specimens do not need to be gutted.

Gefüllte Kalbsbrust (Ligurien).name: Stuffed Breast of Veal (Liguria)
ingredients: 2 tbsp pine nuts or chopped pine nuts
steps: Soak 30 g dried mushrooms in 1/4 l white wine. Using a sharp knife, or ask your butcher to cut a pocket toward the bone. Rinse briefly and pat dry.
Blanch the calf's brain or sweetbreads for 5 minutes, remove membranes and vessels, refresh in cold water, and cut carefully into small cubes.
Melt 30 g butter in a pan and gently sauté the meat. Transfer to a bowl and let cool slightly.
Drain the mushrooms and trim the stems. Peel 2 garlic cloves and 1 carrot and dice finely. Add everything to the bowl.
Whisk the veal, 80 g peas or pine nuts, and 1 tsp marjoram together with 3 eggs and mix well. Add 2 tbsp Parmesan and 1 tsp marjoram, season generously with salt and pepper.
Fill the prepared breast of veal with the stuffing and sew the opening firmly closed with kitchen twine, especially important for smaller pieces.
Lower the stuffed breast of veal into simmering broth. Remove from the heat and let the meat finish cooking in the broth for 30 minutes. Cook for a total of about 2 hours, then slice and serve.
Gefüllte Kalbsbrust (Ligurien).tips: Wine suggestion: rosé or a light red wine.

Kalbsragout mit Zucchini.name: Veal Ragout with Zucchini
ingredients: 750 g braising veal (shoulder or breast tip)
steps: Cut the veal into small cubes. Finely chop the celery, 1 carrot, 1 onion, and 2 garlic cloves. Peel the potatoes and cut into small cubes.

Heat 5 tbsp oil in a wide pot. Brown the veal cubes in batches in the hot oil, stirring frequently. Mix in the vegetables and sauté briefly. Pour in 1/8 l wine and 1/8 l broth, sprinkle in 1 tsp rosemary, add the potatoes, and braise covered.
Blanch the tomatoes, peel, deseed, and chop coarsely, then add to the pot. Season generously with salt and pepper and cook for about 20 minutes until the potatoes are nearly tender.
Wash the zucchini and slice into 1/2 cm rounds. Add to the ragout and cook everything for a further 10 minutes until done.
Kalbsragout mit Zucchini.tips: A summery ragout that can be varied with whatever fresh seasonal vegetables are available.

Mariniertes Rindfleisch.name: Marinated Beef
ingredients: 400 g cooked lean beef (leftover boiled beef works well)
steps: Slice 2 bread rolls and arrange on a plate. Drizzle with 4–5 tbsp wine.
Rinse 4 anchovy fillets under cold running water, pat dry, and halve lengthwise.
Slice 400 g cooked, well-chilled beef very thinly and arrange over the bread slices. Distribute the halved anchovy fillets on top and refrigerate until ready to serve.

Very finely chop 2 tbsp capers and stir together with 6 tbsp olive oil, 3 tbsp white wine vinegar, a little salt, and freshly ground pepper to make a smooth sauce. Finely chop 1/2 bunch parsley and mix in. Spread the sauce over the topped bread slices.

Geschmortes Kaninchen.name: Braised Rabbit
steps: Cut the rabbit into 8–10 pieces, rinse under cold running water, and pat thoroughly dry.
Slice 2 garlic cloves. Place in a wide braising pot together with 4 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp butter, 1 tsp rosemary, 3 sage leaves, and 2 bay leaves, and heat.
Add the rabbit pieces to the hot fat and brown well on all sides. Season with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg, pour in 1/8 l wine, bring briefly to a boil, then reduce the heat.

Blanch the tomatoes, peel, and chop coarsely. Add to the rabbit, cover, and braise over low heat for about 45 minutes until done. Add a splash more wine if needed. Scatter with 2 tbsp pine nuts and the leaves from 1/2 bunch parsley before serving.

Kalbfleisch in Weißwein.name: Veal in White Wine
steps: Cut the veal into large cubes. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil slowly in a large pan, add 30 g butter and let it melt. Gently sauté 6 sage leaves and 1 bay leaf.
Increase the heat, add the veal in batches, and brown well, stirring frequently.

Season with salt and pepper, pour in 100 ml white wine, and braise gently for about 10 minutes. Stir in 100 g olives and let them heat through briefly. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper and serve immediately.
Kalbfleisch in Weißwein.tips: Wine suggestion: a full-bodied, dry white wine, such as a Pigato from Liguria.

Gefüllte Zucchiniblüten.name: Stuffed Zucchini Blossoms
steps: Remove any stem from the zucchini blossoms. Dip the blossoms briefly in cold water, shake gently, place in a colander, and leave to drain well.

Cook 2 potatoes in salted water until tender. Cut 200 g zucchini flesh into small pieces, place in a saucepan with a few tablespoons of water and a pinch of salt. Cook covered for about 5 minutes until soft.
Drain the zucchini and purée finely once slightly cooled. Peel the potatoes while still hot, press through a potato ricer, and pile loosely onto the zucchini purée. Leave to cool, then mix together.

Cut the basil into thin strips. Finely chop 1 tbsp marjoram and mix both into the vegetable purée. Press in 2 garlic cloves. Stir in 1 tbsp freshly grated Parmesan, 1 egg, and 2 tbsp olive oil, and mix everything thoroughly. Season carefully with salt and pepper.
Preheat the oven to 250°C. Grease an ovenproof dish with 1 tbsp olive oil. Cut out the stamens from the zucchini blossoms.

Fill the zucchini blossoms with the prepared vegetable mixture and place side by side in the dish. Drizzle with 2 tbsp oil and bake in the oven (gas mark 5) for about 3 minutes until golden. Serve hot.
Gefüllte Zucchiniblüten.tips: For a quick garnish: season finely chopped raw tomato flesh with a little olive oil, salt, and freshly ground pepper. Place a spoonful alongside the stuffed blossoms when plating.

Grüne Bohnen.name: Green Beans
steps: Trim and wash the green beans. Blanch in boiling salted water for 5 minutes, refresh in cold water, and drain well.
Rinse 4 anchovy fillets briefly and pat dry. Peel 2 garlic cloves, wash 1 bunch parsley and pick the leaves. Finely chop all three ingredients together and mix.
Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a wide pan, melt 1 tbsp butter in it. Add the anchovy mixture and sauté over gentle heat, stirring.
Add the drained beans, stir to combine, and cook through. Season generously with salt and pepper and serve.
Grüne Bohnen.tips: Excellent as a side dish with quickly pan-fried meat — for example veal cutlets or steaks, tender lamb chops, grilled rabbit, or poultry.

Artischockenomelett.name: Artichoke Omelette
steps: Remove the tough outer leaves from the artichokes and trim the tips off the remaining leaves. Cut the artichokes into eighths and remove the choke. Place the cleaned artichokes immediately in water with 2 tbsp lemon juice to prevent discoloration.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a pan. Remove the artichokes from the lemon water, pat dry, and season with salt and pepper. Dust with 1 tbsp flour and fry in the hot oil until golden. Remove and drain on paper towels. Clean the pan.

Whisk 4 eggs with 2 tbsp milk, season with salt and pepper. Melt 2 tbsp butter in the pan, add the artichokes, and pour in the egg mixture (or prepare four individual portions one at a time). Fry the underside until golden, shaking the pan occasionally. Slide the omelette onto a plate and flip. Cook through over gentle heat and serve hot.
Artischockenomelett.tips: Omelettes made with fresh vegetables or herbs are also popular served as a light starter or snack.

Ausgebackene Schwarzwurzeln.name: Deep-Fried Salsify
steps: Thoroughly wash the salsify under running water and scrape off the black skin using a small knife or vegetable peeler. Cut the peeled roots into 4 cm pieces and immediately place in cold water with the juice of 1/2 lemon. Cook in boiling salted water for 10–15 minutes until tender but still firm.

Separate 2 eggs. Whisk the yolks with 100g flour, 30g freshly grated Parmesan, 2 tbsp white wine, and 50ml cold water; season with salt. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and gently fold into the batter. Heat a generous amount of oil in a saucepan or deep-fryer.

Drain the salsify, let it dry thoroughly, and pat dry with kitchen paper. Dip the pieces in the batter and deep-fry in the hot oil until crispy. Drain on kitchen paper and serve as a side dish with meat.
Ausgebackene Schwarzwurzeln.tips: The most important thing when deep-frying: use fresh oil and make sure it is hot enough. How can you tell? Simply dip the handle of a wooden spoon into the oil — as soon as small bubbles rise around it, you're ready to go.

Schwarzwurzel-Ragout.name: Salsify Ragout
steps: Thoroughly scrub 750g salsify under running water. Peel as thinly as possible and immediately place in cold water with the juice of 1/2 lemon.
Peel 1 onion, wash 1/2 bunch of parsley, and finely chop both. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a wide saucepan, add the onion and parsley, and sauté briefly.

Drain the salsify and cut into thumb-length pieces. Add to the pot, season with salt, and sauté for 5 minutes. Dust with 1 tsp flour, let it take on a little color, then deglaze with just under 250ml broth. Simmer gently for 15–20 minutes.
Whisk 2 egg yolks with the juice of 1/2 lemon, about 2 tbsp broth, and salt. Stir into the sauce — do not allow to boil again. Sprinkle with the remaining parsley leaves.
Schwarzwurzel-Ragout.tips: Peeling salsify always takes a little time. Try this alternative method: wash and scrub the salsify thoroughly, then blanch unpeeled in boiling salted water for 10 minutes. Rinse under cold water and peel off the skin with a knife. This will reduce the cooking time accordingly.

Mandelkringel.name: Almond Rings
ingredients: 5–6 tbsp orange blossom water (or 2 tbsp orange liqueur + 3–4 tbsp water)
steps: Finely grind 300g blanched almonds, place in a bowl, and thoroughly work in 200g sugar with a wooden spoon. Knead in 5–6 tbsp orange blossom water until a smooth, pliable dough forms.
Preheat the oven to 150°C. Roll out small portions of the dough to finger thickness. Shape into even rings and arrange on lightly greased baking sheets.

Slide the baking sheets one at a time onto the top shelf of the preheated oven (gas: mark 1) and dry out rather than bake for 15–20 minutes. Remove from the oven and glaze with a little orange syrup while still hot. Place in decorative paper cups and leave to cool.
Mandelkringel.tips: Instead of orange syrup, you can stir a little orange marmalade with a squeeze of lemon juice until smooth, warm it slightly, and use it as a glaze for the almond rings. Storage tip: keep the almond rings in a well-sealed tin — they will soften slightly and improve over time.

Gebackene süße Creme.name: Fried Sweet Cream
steps: Separate 4 eggs. In a round-bottomed saucepan, whisk 4 egg yolks together with 4 whole eggs, the finely grated zest of 1 lemon, and 100g sugar. Gradually add 1 litre of milk and 150g flour in alternating additions, stirring thoroughly until a smooth, lump-free mixture forms.

Leave the mixture to rest for 30 minutes. Then, stirring constantly, bring it slowly to the boil (a double boiler or bain-marie works best). Bring to a brief boil, remove from the heat, and leave to set. Pour the mixture into a cold-rinsed shallow dish or a rimmed baking tray, spread evenly to a depth of 2 cm, and leave to cool completely.
Cut the set cream into diamonds or rectangles of about 5 cm. Beat 4 egg whites lightly with a fork, dip each piece in the egg white, then coat in breadcrumbs.

Heat a generous amount of oil in a saucepan until very hot (temperature test: small bubbles rise around a wooden skewer held in the oil). Carefully lower the breaded pieces into the oil and fry until golden brown. Drain on kitchen paper, arrange on a warmed serving plate, dust with cinnamon and sugar, and serve immediately.
Gebackene süße Creme.tips: This dessert pairs wonderfully with a fruity accompaniment: for example, puréed raspberries seasoned with a little lemon juice and vanilla sugar.

Süße Teigtaschen.name: Sweet Pastry Pockets
steps: Drain 200g ricotta or quark well. Wash 1 orange in hot water and dry it. Finely grate the zest. Cut 150g candied fruit into very small pieces or chop in a food processor, then mix with the ricotta and orange zest.

Roll 300g puff pastry out thinly on a lightly floured surface (layer thawed sheets on top of each other first). Dot the ricotta filling over one half of the pastry at 5 cm intervals using a teaspoon. Loosely fold the other half of the pastry over the top. Use a glass to cut out round pockets around each mound of filling, then press the edges firmly to seal.

Heat a generous amount of oil in a saucepan until very hot (temperature test: small bubbles rise around a wooden skewer held in the oil). Fry the ravioli in batches until golden brown. Drain on kitchen paper, dust lightly with powdered sugar, and serve immediately.
Süße Teigtaschen.tips: Ravioli dolci are a traditional carnival pastry, made with various fillings and different types of dough. For an even more authentic result, try a crispy shortcrust pastry: quickly knead together 200g flour, 125g butter, 1 egg yolk, and a pinch of salt into a smooth dough. Rest in the refrigerator for 30 minutes, roll out thinly, and continue as described above.

Safranreis auf Mailänder Art.name: Milanese Saffron Risotto
steps: Briefly soak the marrow bone in ice-cold water, then push out the marrow, pat dry with kitchen paper, and dice finely. Pour 2 tbsp boiling water over the saffron and leave to steep. Finely dice 1 onion and heat 1 litre of broth.
In a large heavy-bottomed pot, melt 2 tbsp butter with the diced marrow until foaming. Add the diced onion and sauté over medium heat, stirring, until translucent.
Add 350g rice and stir-fry until the grains look pale and translucent, taking care not to let them brown. Pour in a generous ladleful (about 1 cup) of hot broth, stirring continuously.
As soon as the liquid has almost evaporated, add more broth and continue to cook at a gentle simmer, stirring. There should always be just enough broth in the pot to barely cover the rice.
After about 15 minutes, add the dissolved saffron. Continue cooking for another 7 minutes, stirring and adding broth as needed.
Taste the rice: when the grains are cooked through but still have a bite, stir in the remaining 3 tbsp butter and 75g grated Parmesan. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

The risotto should now be moist and creamy enough to flow slowly when the pot is tilted. Remove from the heat, cover with a lid, and leave to rest for 1–2 minutes. Serve immediately, offering extra Parmesan at the table if desired.
Safranreis auf Mailänder Art.tips: For an especially rich result, stir in a spoonful of cream at the end. Always add only a little broth at a time, as adding too much at once will interrupt the cooking process.

Reis mit Erbsen.name: Rice with Peas
ingredients: 450g young peas (freshly podded from about 1kg pods, or frozen)
just under 1 litre hot beef broth
steps: Cut 80g bacon and 1 onion into small cubes and finely chop 1/2 bunch of parsley. Melt 2 tbsp butter in a saucepan. Add the bacon, onion, and parsley and sauté briefly, stirring.
Add the peas and pour in 250ml hot broth. Simmer for 5–15 minutes depending on the type of peas (young, tender peas need less time). Pour in the remaining hot broth and bring to the boil.

Stir in 250g rice and cook, stirring, until the rice has absorbed the liquid and is tender (about 20 minutes). Season with salt and pepper. Stir 1 tbsp butter and 50g grated Parmesan into the finished rice. Chop the remaining 1/2 bunch of parsley and scatter over the top. Serve the remaining Parmesan on the side.
Reis mit Erbsen.tips: Wine suggestion: a not-too-dry white wine, such as a Colli Euganei bianco. In the original version, this dish is served more as a liquid soup.

Schwarzer Tintenfischreis.name: Black Squid Ink Risotto
ingredients: 750g squid with ink sacs (available from a fishmonger)
about 750ml hot fish broth (or ready-made fish stock)
steps: Clean and wash the squid, carefully placing the intact ink sacs in a bowl. Cut the squid bodies and tentacles into thin strips. Finely chop 2 garlic cloves and mix with 3 tbsp olive oil and the juice of 1 lemon. Add the squid, stir to coat, and marinate for 20 minutes.

Finely chop 1 onion. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan. Sauté the onion until softened. Drain the squid strips, add to the pot, and sauté, stirring. Pour in the marinade and 250ml white wine. Open the ink sacs and add the ink to the pot. Simmer for 20 minutes, adding a little fish broth from time to time.
Stir in 250g rice and gradually add the remaining fish broth, cooking the rice while stirring constantly until tender. Season with salt and pepper. Finely chop the parsley and scatter over the top.
Schwarzer Tintenfischreis.tips: Serve garnished with lemon wedges if desired.

Reis mit Spargel.name: Asparagus Risotto
steps: Wash the asparagus and cut off the woody ends. Cut off the tender tips and set aside. Roughly chop the remaining stalks and cook in just under 1 litre of salted water for 25 minutes. Remove the cooked asparagus pieces, blend finely with an immersion blender or press through a sieve, then return to the broth.

Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot. Finely chop 1 onion and sauté, stirring. Briefly fry the asparagus tips, then add 250g rice and stir until translucent. Deglaze with 125ml white wine, then gradually add the hot asparagus broth (about 750ml), letting the rice absorb it while stirring constantly.
Stir 1 tbsp butter and 50g freshly grated Parmesan into the finished risotto, and season with salt and pepper.
Reis mit Spargel.tips: Not entirely traditional, but a delightful complement to the asparagus: sauté fresh prawns in olive oil, season with a hint of garlic and pepper, and arrange decoratively on top of the asparagus risotto.

Reis mit Geflügelleber.name: Risotto with Chicken Livers
steps: Soak 20g dried porcini mushrooms in 125ml white wine for about 2 hours.

Finely dice 50g bacon and 1 onion. Place in a pot with 2 tbsp butter and heat gently. Drain the mushrooms, reserving the soaking liquid. Finely chop the mushrooms and briefly fry. Add 250g rice and stir until translucent.
Gradually pour in 1 litre of hot broth, letting the rice absorb it while stirring constantly. Season with salt and pepper, then stir in 2 tbsp freshly grated Parmesan.

Meanwhile, cut the chicken livers into small pieces. Melt 1 tbsp butter in a small pan, add the livers and cook for 3 minutes. Season lightly with salt and pepper, pour in the mushroom soaking liquid and bring to a brief boil. Arrange the livers on top of the finished risotto and sprinkle with the remaining Parmesan.
Reis mit Geflügelleber.tips: For an even more luxurious version: use fresh porcini mushrooms!

Grüner Nudelauflauf.name: Green Lasagne Bake
ingredients: 200g plain flour, plus extra for rolling
1 stick celery (or a small piece of celeriac)
3 tbsp butter for greasing the dish and dotting on top
steps: For the pasta dough, clean and wash the spinach, then place it still dripping wet in a pot and wilt over high heat. Squeeze out very firmly and pass twice through a meat grinder using the fine disc.
Mix 100g semolina with 200g flour, then knead in 1 tsp salt, the spinach, and 2 eggs. Cover the dough with a cloth and set aside to rest.
For the ragù, wash the pork and beef and sear in a pot over high heat while still dripping wet. Finely dice 50g bacon, 1 onion, 1 carrot, and the celery stick.

Heat 2 tbsp butter in a casserole dish, fry the diced bacon and vegetables. Add the minced meat and fry, stirring. Stir in 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 tsp oregano, and 750ml broth, season lightly with salt and pepper. Cover and simmer gently for about 20 minutes.

Béchamel sauce: Melt 2 tbsp butter in a saucepan and stir in 2 tbsp flour. Remove from the heat and after 1 minute stir in 500ml milk. Return to the hob, bring to a boil while stirring, then simmer gently for 10 minutes. Season with salt and a pinch of freshly grated nutmeg.

Bring 2 litres of salted water to the boil in a large pot. Divide the pasta dough into thirds and roll each piece out to 3mm thick on a floured surface. Cook each sheet individually in the boiling water for 3 minutes, refresh in cold water, and drain on a cloth.

Clean the chicken livers, cut into cubes, and add to the meat ragù. Season with salt and pepper, then remove the pot from the heat. Grease a large rectangular baking dish with 1 tbsp butter. Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Layer alternately: a pasta sheet, then meat ragù, béchamel sauce, and grated Parmesan.
Finish with a layer of béchamel sauce, dot with 2 tbsp butter in small pieces, and bake in the oven (Gas Mark 3) for 30 minutes until golden brown.
Grüner Nudelauflauf.tips: You can of course save a lot of effort by using ready-made green lasagne sheets and mixed minced meat. But then it's no longer the real thing.

Bandnudeln mit Schinken.name: Tagliatelle with Parma Ham
ingredients: 150g Parma ham (or cooked ham if preferred)
300g plain flour, plus extra for rolling
steps: Pasta dough: Knead 300g flour, 3 eggs, and a pinch of salt into a smooth dough. Cover with a damp cloth and leave to rest for 30 minutes.
Roll the dough out very thinly on a floured surface and cut into strips about 5mm wide and 10cm long. Bring 3 litres of salted water to the boil in a large pot.

Cut the ham into small cubes, separating the fatty parts from the lean. Melt 50g butter in a large pan. Add the fatty pieces of ham and fry lightly. Finely chop 1 onion, add to the pan, and sauté until translucent. Then stir in the lean ham cubes and fry gently, stirring.

Meanwhile, cook the pasta in the boiling salted water for about 10 minutes until al dente, drain well, and immediately add to the pan. Toss thoroughly, season with salt and pepper. Shave 50g fresh Parmesan and serve alongside.
Bandnudeln mit Schinken.tips: A speciality of the city of Parma — which you can of course also prepare using ready-made tagliatelle!

Spinat-Käse-Nocken.name: Spinach and Cheese Dumplings
ingredients: 150g ricotta (Italian fresh cheese; well-drained low-fat quark can be used as a substitute)
steps: Clean and wash the spinach, place it still dripping wet in a pot, heat and wilt. Allow to cool slightly, squeeze out the leaves firmly, and chop finely.
Roughly chop 1 onion and sauté in 30g melted butter until translucent. Stir in the chopped spinach, remove from the heat, and leave to cool.
Beat the ricotta until creamy, then fold in the cooled spinach and half the grated Parmesan. Stir in 2 eggs and 1 egg yolk, and season generously with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg.

Gradually work in 200g flour and stir into a smooth dough, then taste and adjust the seasoning. Bring 2 litres of salted water to the boil in a large pot. Using two tablespoons, shape dumplings from the dough and drop them into the boiling water. Reduce the temperature and let the dumplings poach until they float to the surface. Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 175°C.

Lift the dumplings out with a slotted spoon, drain, and place in an ovenproof dish. Melt the remaining butter and drizzle over the dumplings, then bake in the hot oven (Gas Mark 2) for 5 minutes. Sprinkle with the remaining Parmesan and serve immediately.

Makkaroni mit Bolognese Fleischsauce.name: Macaroni with Bolognese Meat Sauce
steps: Finely dice 1 carrot, 1 celery stick, 1 onion, and 80g bacon. Place in a pot with 2 tbsp butter, heat, and gently fry everything together.

Add the ground beef and fry, stirring, until browned. Dust with 1 tsp flour, then deglaze with 125ml beef broth. Season with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg, add 1 clove and 1 bay leaf. Cover and braise gently for about 20 minutes.
Bring 2 litres of salted water to the boil in a large pot. Cook the macaroni until al dente.
Cut the chicken liver into small pieces, add to the sauce, and cook for a further 3–4 minutes. Season the sauce with salt, pepper, and 1 pinch of cayenne pepper, then remove the clove and bay leaf.
Drain the cooked macaroni, divide among warmed plates, and spoon the meat sauce over the top. Serve with 50g freshly grated Parmesan.
Makkaroni mit Bolognese Fleischsauce.tips: The authentic recipe for Ragù Bolognese does not use pre-minced beef — instead, fresh meat is thinly sliced with a sharp knife and gradually chopped finer and finer. Variation: peel, deseed, and finely chop 300g ripe tomatoes and braise them in the sauce.

Tortellini – Gefüllte Nudelkringel.name: Tortellini – Filled Pasta Rings
ingredients: 500g plain flour, plus extra for rolling
steps: Pasta dough: Knead 500g flour with 250ml water, 2 eggs, 1 tsp salt, and 2 tbsp olive oil into a smooth dough. If the dough is too dry, add water drop by drop until it is soft and pliable but not sticky. Dust with flour, cover with a cloth, and leave to rest at room temperature for 20 minutes.

Filling: Poach the chicken breast fillet in 250ml chicken broth at a gentle simmer for 15 minutes. Remove and drain. Finely chop with a large knife or in a food processor, then mix in 1 tbsp crème fraîche.
Transfer to a bowl and mix thoroughly with 5 tbsp freshly grated Parmesan, 2 egg yolks, the grated zest of ½ lemon, salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg.
Break off a quarter of the dough and roll it out paper-thin on a floured surface.
Cut out circles of 5cm diameter. Place ½ tsp of the chicken filling in the centre of each circle.
Moisten the edges of the circles, fold each one in half, and press the edges firmly together.

Shape into rings by gently pulling each piece apart in the middle, wrapping around your index finger, and pressing the overlapping tips together. Place the finished tortellini on a flour-dusted cloth. Repeat with the remaining dough.

Bring 3–4 litres of broth to the boil and add the tortellini. Stir gently to prevent them from sticking together. Simmer gently for about 8 minutes, then lift out with a slotted spoon into a colander, rinse with hot water, and drain. Serve with Ragù Bolognese or with hot melted butter and Parmesan.
Tortellini – Gefüllte Nudelkringel.tips: Tortellini can be filled with minced pork, ham, sausage, cheese, or eggs, and served in a rich broth. Wine recommendation: Lambrusco or Gutturnio dei Colli Piacentini.

Geschmorte Kalbshaxe.name: Braised Veal Shin
ingredients: 6–8 veal shin slices (cut crosswise through the bone, about 3kg total)
500ml beef broth, plus extra for basting
½ tsp each thyme and oregano
steps: Finely dice the carrots, celery, 3 onions, and 3 garlic cloves. Melt 4 tbsp butter in a roasting pan over medium heat. Once the fat is clear, fry the diced vegetables until lightly browned. Remove the pan from the heat.

Tie the veal shin slices with kitchen twine to hold their shape. Season with salt and pepper, dust with flour, and shake off any excess. Fry in a pan in 6 tbsp olive oil over medium heat until lightly browned on both sides. Remove and place on top of the sautéed vegetables.
Pour off the oil from the pan. Deglaze the pan with 250ml white wine, stirring to dissolve the sediment, and reduce to 4–6 tbsp of liquid. Preheat the oven to 175°C.
Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water and peel. Halve and remove the seeds. Roughly chop the flesh. Coarsely chop the parsley, including the stalks.
Add 750ml beef broth to the pan juices, then add the chopped parsley, ½ tsp each of thyme and oregano, 2 bay leaves, and the chopped tomatoes. Bring to a boil and season with salt and pepper.
Pour the sauce over the meat. Bring to a boil on the hob. Place the lid on and transfer to the oven (Gas Mark 2). Braise for 2–3 hours, basting the shanks with a little broth every 30 minutes.

For the gremolata: finely grate the zest of 2 lemons. Finely chop the parsley. Very finely dice 5 garlic cloves. Mix everything together. Transfer the finished braised meat to a serving dish, spoon the vegetables and sauce over the top, and sprinkle with the gremolata.
Geschmorte Kalbshaxe.tips: Essential equipment: a heavy, wide casserole or shallow braising pot with a lid. Wine recommendation: a young, fresh red wine, such as Bardolino.

Schmorbraten in Rotwein.name: Braised Beef in Red Wine
steps: Using a sharp knife, score the meat at regular intervals and stud it with small pieces of garlic (from 2 cloves).
Heat 3 tbsp oil in a heavy casserole dish and melt 50g butter in it. Add the meat and brown on all sides.

Meanwhile, prepare the vegetables: trim 1 celery stalk and 1 kohlrabi, setting the greens aside for later. Peel 1 carrot and 1 onion. Finely chop everything, add to the casserole and sauté. Season with salt and pepper, add 2 cloves. Pour in ⅜ l red wine and let it reduce slightly.
Blanch and peel the tomatoes. Finely chop the flesh and add it to the casserole with the meat.
Gradually pour in about ¼ l beef broth, cover and braise gently for 3 hours.
To serve, pass the sauce through a sieve, season with salt, pepper and freshly grated nutmeg, and heat through quickly. Cut the kohlrabi leaves into fine strips and scatter over the sliced meat.
Schmorbraten in Rotwein.tips: The Milanese love to serve generous portions. Polenta makes a great side dish — either as a soft mash or sliced and briefly pan-fried. Wine suggestion: a full-bodied red, such as Bardolino Classico Superiore.

Kalbsröllchen.name: Veal Rolls
ingredients: 50g streaky bacon, cut into thin strips
steps: Pound the escalopes very thin. Remove the skins from the sausages, crumble the meat into a bowl.

For the filling, finely chop the chicken livers. Peel and crush 2 cloves of garlic and finely chop the parsley. Add everything to the sausage meat along with 2 tbsp Parmesan and 2 egg yolks. Mix thoroughly and season with freshly ground pepper.
Spread the filling evenly over the escalopes and roll them up. Place 1 sage leaf on each roll, wrap with bacon strips and secure with toothpicks.
Dust the rolls lightly with flour. Heat 50g butter in a pan, brown the rolls on all sides, turning frequently. Season with salt and pour in ⅛ l wine, letting it reduce by about half.
Add ⅛ l broth, cover and braise over low heat for 20 minutes. After 10 minutes, place an additional sage leaf on each roll (or add a few sage leaves to the sauce).
Remove the rolls and keep warm. Bring the sauce to a vigorous boil, adjust seasoning and serve with the meat.
Kalbsröllchen.tips: Very thinly pounded slices of meat are filled with a savory stuffing and gently braised in wine. Potatoes and vegetables make a great side dish. Wine suggestion: a dry, full-bodied white, such as Riesling (Oltrepò Pavese).

Kalbsleber mit Zwiebeln.name: Calf's Liver with Onions
ingredients: 100ml good beef broth (or ready-made veal stock from a jar)
steps: Slice the calf's liver (remove the membrane if necessary) into thin strips. Cut 350g onions into thin rings and finely chop half of the parsley.
Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a pan and melt 2 tbsp butter in it. Add the onion rings and chopped parsley, and sauté over low heat for 10 minutes until the onions are translucent.

Pour in 100ml broth and increase the heat. Add the liver slices and cook, turning frequently, for about 4 minutes. Roughly chop the remaining parsley. Season the liver with salt and freshly ground pepper and scatter over the parsley.
Kalbsleber mit Zwiebeln.tips: Freshly made mashed potatoes, white bread or soft polenta make great accompaniments. Wine suggestion: a full-bodied white, such as a Soave Superiore.

Kalbsschnitzel mit Zitronensauce.name: Veal Escalopes with Lemon Sauce
steps: Cut the veal escalopes in half crosswise and pound to about 0.5 cm thin. Finely grate the zest of the unwaxed lemon and squeeze its juice. Whisk the lemon juice vigorously with 4 tbsp olive oil, season with a little pepper and stir in the lemon zest.
Pour the marinade over the escalopes, cover and leave to marinate in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, turning once halfway through.

Brush a pan with 2 tbsp olive oil and heat it. Remove the escalopes from the marinade and let them drain well. Place in the hot pan and fry for about 2 minutes on each side. Remove and set aside, covered.
Pour the lemon and oil marinade into the pan. Squeeze the second lemon, add the juice and bring everything briefly to a boil. Stir in 1 tbsp butter and let it melt, then season with salt and pepper.
Return the escalopes to the sauce and heat through thoroughly. Arrange on warmed plates, spoon the sauce over the top and serve.
Kalbsschnitzel mit Zitronensauce.tips: When plating, these little escalopes look beautiful garnished with paper-thin lemon slices.

Kalbsbraten in Milch.name: Veal Roast in Milk
steps: Lightly season the veal with salt and pepper. Press 2 cloves of garlic and rub the meat with it.
Melt 80g butter in a braising pot. Add the veal and brown on all sides. Heat 1 l milk in a separate small saucepan.
Pour the hot milk over the veal, add 1 bay leaf and 1 sprig of thyme. Cover and braise over low heat for about 1.5 hours, basting the meat with the milk regularly.

Once the meat is tender and cooked through, remove it and keep warm, covered. Discard the bay leaf and thyme from the sauce, pour in 250g cream and simmer vigorously until a smooth, creamy sauce forms.
Pass the sauce through a sieve and season with salt and pepper. Slice the veal thinly, arrange on a warmed serving platter and serve with the sauce.
Kalbsbraten in Milch.tips: Delicate, briefly steamed vegetables such as broccoli, carrots, white turnips or kohlrabi make a lovely side dish.

Gebratener Radicchio.name: Pan-Fried Radicchio
steps: Trim the radicchio and remove the outer leaves. Quarter the heads lengthwise and cut out the cores. Rinse briefly and drain well.

Cut the bacon into thin strips and cut the 4 onions into eighths. Finely chop 2 cloves of garlic. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a large pan and sauté the bacon and onions, stirring, for 3 minutes. Stir in the garlic.

Add the well-drained radicchio to the pan and fry over low heat for 5 minutes, turning once or twice. Season with salt, freshly ground pepper and 2 tbsp olive oil. Serve hot as a side dish with roasted or grilled meat.
Gebratener Radicchio.tips: A special specialty from Veneto is Radicchio di Treviso — long, smooth leaves instead of a round head. Preparation alla griglia: brush with olive oil, grill, then season with salt and pepper.

Überbackener Spargel.name: Asparagus au Gratin
steps: Thinly peel the lower parts of the asparagus spears and trim off the woody ends completely. Wash the asparagus and tie into four bundles.

Stand the bundles upright in a pot and add enough hot water so that the tips are not submerged. Add salt, close the lid tightly and cook the asparagus for 10–15 minutes depending on thickness, leaving it slightly firm.
Preheat the oven to 225°C. Melt 80g butter without browning it. Drain the asparagus and let it dry. Lightly grease a large ovenproof dish with 1 tbsp of the melted butter.

Arrange the asparagus spears in overlapping layers on the dish, keeping the tips as exposed as possible. Drizzle with the melted butter and sprinkle evenly with 50g freshly grated Parmesan. Bake in the preheated oven for 3 minutes until golden. Season with freshly ground pepper and serve immediately.
Überbackener Spargel.tips: A variation from the gourmet city of Parma: par-cook asparagus spears for 10–15 minutes, wrap in Parma ham and briefly bake with Parmesan and small knobs of butter.

Marinierte Auberginen.name: Marinated Aubergines
steps: Wash the aubergines and cut into 0.5 cm thick slices, removing the stem ends. Salt the slices, stack them in layers on a large plate, cover with a second plate and place a weight on top. Leave to stand for at least 30 minutes.

Rinse the aubergines and pat them dry thoroughly. Heat 1–2 tbsp lard in a large pan. Fry the aubergine slices in batches for about 2 minutes on each side. Add more lard as needed. Remove the fried slices from the pan and drain well on paper towels.

Very finely chop ½ bunch each of parsley and basil, 6–8 sage leaves and 3 cloves of garlic, then mix together. Layer the aubergine slices in a bowl, seasoning each layer with salt and pepper, drizzling with a little wine vinegar and scattering over the herb and garlic mixture. Cover and leave to marinate in the refrigerator overnight. Serve as a side dish with boiled or roasted meat.
Marinierte Auberginen.tips: Always use fresh ingredients and drain the fried aubergines on paper towels immediately after cooking.

Kürbis-Mandel-Eier.name: Pumpkin and Almond Baked Eggs
steps: Cut the peeled, ripe pumpkin flesh into small pieces. Place in a saucepan, season lightly with salt, and add 2–3 tablespoons of water. Cover and simmer until soft. Purée directly in the pan with an immersion blender or press through a fine sieve. Allow to cool slightly. Preheat the oven to 200°C.

Mix 50g of the ground almonds and half of the butter into the pumpkin purée. Whisk the 6 eggs together, then stir in 50g of the freshly grated Parmesan until smooth. Gradually fold in the pumpkin purée, stirring as you go. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

Grease an ovenproof dish with 1 tablespoon of butter. Pour in the pumpkin and egg mixture, sprinkle with the remaining almonds and Parmesan, and dot the top with the remaining butter. Place in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) and bake until set, about 30 minutes. Serve immediately.
Kürbis-Mandel-Eier.tips: Fresh pumpkin can also be sliced thinly and fried until crispy in aromatic olive oil — drain well, then sprinkle with salt, pepper, and a pinch of chilli.

Frühlingsgemüse.name: Spring Vegetables
steps: Trim the broccoli and break into florets. Peel the stalks and slice thinly. Trim and wash the celery, then cut into 3 cm pieces. Trim the asparagus, cut off the woody ends, and cut the spears into 3 cm pieces. Trim and wash the sugar snap peas and spring onions. Separate the white bulbs from the green tops, leaving about 2 cm of green stalk attached to each bulb. Slice the remaining green tops into thin rings. Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel them, and cut into thin wedges.

Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil. Stir in the juice of 1 lemon. Blanch the vegetables one after another: asparagus pieces (5 min.), celery pieces (4 min.), broccoli florets (3 min.), and sugar snap peas (2 min.). Refresh in cold water and drain. Reserve the cooking liquid for later.

Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a frying pan until foamy. Add the white spring onion bulbs and broccoli stalks and sauté, stirring, for 3 minutes. Gradually stir in the blanched vegetables and add a few tablespoons of the reserved cooking liquid. Stir in the tomato wedges.

Continue to cook until the vegetables are tender but still crisp and al dente. Season with 2 tablespoons of balsamic vinegar, a little salt, freshly ground pepper, and a pinch of freshly grated nutmeg. Scatter over the spring onion rings, 2 tablespoons of freshly grated Parmesan, and fresh basil leaves, and serve immediately.

Gemüseauflauf.name: Vegetable Bake
steps: Wash the potatoes, place in salted water, and cook until tender. In a second pot, bring plenty of salted water to the boil for the spinach. Trim and wash the spinach, then blanch in the boiling water for 2 minutes. Drain well.
Strip the parsley leaves from the stalks and finely purée together with the spinach. Season to taste with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg.

Preheat the oven to 175°C. Drain the potatoes, peel while still hot, and press through a potato ricer. Whisk the 4 eggs with the 4 tablespoons of cream and stir into the potatoes. Thoroughly mix in the spinach purée and about three-quarters of the grated Parmesan. Season well once more.

Grease an ovenproof dish with 1 tablespoon of butter. Fill with the potato mixture, place in the oven (gas mark 2), and bake for 20 minutes. Sprinkle with the remaining Parmesan, dot with 2 tablespoons of butter in small pieces, and bake until golden and crispy. Serve hot.
Gemüseauflauf.tips: Fresh peas or carrots feature in other variations of this vegetable bake. Instead of potatoes, a mixture of pasta and béchamel sauce can be used.

Eisgekühlte Schichttorte.name: Chilled Layered Cake
ingredients: 3 eggs, at room temperature (for the filling)
2 small cups strong cooled espresso (for the filling)
steps: Prepare the cake layers the day before if possible. Preheat the oven to 150°C. Finely grind 250g of blanched almonds. Mix with 150g of icing sugar. Whisk the 2 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in.

Divide the dough into 3 portions. Roll each portion out thinly between sheets of cling film to the size of a springform tin (24 cm diameter). Bake the 3 layers one after another in the preheated oven (gas mark 1) on the middle shelf for 10–15 minutes. Allow to cool completely.

For the filling, separate the 3 eggs. Whisk the 3 egg whites to stiff peaks. Beat 200g butter, the 3 egg yolks, and 60g icing sugar together until light and fluffy. Fold in the whisked egg whites one tablespoon at a time.
Mix the 2 cl of brandy with the 2 small cups of cold espresso. Soak one of the cooled cake layers with about one-third of the espresso and brandy mixture.

Spread one-third of the cream mixture over the soaked layer and smooth it out. Finely grate 50g of chocolate and scatter half of it over the cream. Carefully place another cake layer on top, soak with the espresso and brandy, then add another layer of cream.

Cover the cake and leave to set in the coldest part of the fridge for 4–6 hours, or for a shorter time in the freezer. Just before serving, coarsely grate 100g of chocolate and decorate the top of the cake with it, along with 3 tablespoons of flaked almonds if desired. Serve ice cold.

Mascarponecreme.name: Mascarpone Cream
ingredients: 500g mascarpone (Italian full-fat cream cheese; alternatively 300g thick cream cheese mixed with 200g crème fraîche)
steps: Place the 4 egg yolks and 4 tablespoons of sugar in a bowl and beat with a hand mixer on the highest setting until pale and fluffy.
Gradually fold in the 500g of mascarpone. Flavour with the finely grated zest of 1 lemon.

Have a shallow dish ready. Mix the 2 small cups of cold espresso with the 4 cl of brandy. Briefly dip half of the ladyfinger biscuits into the mixture one by one. Use them to line the base of the dish.
Spread half of the mascarpone cream over the biscuits and smooth the surface. Dip the remaining biscuits briefly in the espresso, layer them on top, and spread over the rest of the cream.
Cover and refrigerate for 5 hours. Just before serving, dust generously with unsweetened cocoa powder.
Mascarponecreme.tips: "Crema di mascarpone" is also an old Lombard recipe. Another sweet trail leads more towards the cuisine of Tuscany.

Kastanienpüree mit Sahne.name: Chestnut Purée with Whipped Cream
steps: Preheat the oven to 250°C. Score the chestnuts with a sharp knife, place on a baking tray, and roast in the oven (gas mark 5) for about 20 minutes until the shells split open. Remove and peel while still hot.

Place the chestnuts in a saucepan, pour in the 500ml of milk, and add a split vanilla pod. Simmer gently for about 45 minutes until soft (the milk will be almost completely absorbed). Remove the vanilla pod and purée the chestnuts.

Stir in 100g of icing sugar, 50g of cocoa powder, and 2 cl of rum until smooth. Divide among dessert bowls — for a particularly attractive presentation, press through a potato ricer or spätzle press to create a spaghetti-like mound. Whip the 200ml of cream to stiff peaks and use to garnish the chestnut purée.
Kastanienpüree mit Sahne.tips: If fresh chestnuts are not in season, use tinned, pre-peeled chestnuts (about 400g). They do not need to simmer as long in the milk until they break down — check for doneness after 30 minutes.

Gestürzte Sahnecreme.name: Turned-Out Cream Dessert
ingredients: caramel sauce or fresh fruit, to garnish
steps: Soak the 2 sheets of gelatine in cold water. Pour the 500ml of cream into a saucepan. Split the vanilla pod lengthways, scrape out the seeds, and add both the seeds and the pod to the cream along with the 50g of sugar. Heat gently and let it simmer very gently for about 15 minutes.

Remove the pan from the heat and take out the vanilla pod. Squeeze the soaked gelatine sheets lightly and add them to the hot cream, stirring until completely dissolved. Pour the cream into 4–6 small moulds that have been rinsed with cold water. Leave to set in the fridge for 3–4 hours.
Turn the chilled cream desserts out onto dessert plates, drizzle with caramel sauce, or garnish with fresh fruit.
Gestürzte Sahnecreme.tips: For those who find plain cream too subtle, a small glass of Marsala can be stirred in for extra flavour. Wine suggestion: Marsala or a fruity, sweet Prosecco (Amabile).

Marinierte Erdbeeren.name: Marinated Strawberries
ingredients: 500g small, flavourful strawberries (wild strawberries are ideal)
2 tbsp balsamic vinegar (Italian condiment made from grape must)
steps: Briefly rinse the strawberries, drain well, and remove the hulls. Place the fruit in a bowl.

Sprinkle the strawberries with 1–2 tablespoons of sugar and drizzle over the 2 tablespoons of balsamic vinegar. Toss gently, cover, and leave to macerate at room temperature for at least 1 hour. Refrigerate briefly just before serving if desired.
Marinierte Erdbeeren.tips: Balsamic vinegar is anything but an ordinary vinegar. Just a few drops are enough to bring a salad or sauce to life, to perfectly complement noble ingredients like beef fillet or delicate fish, and even to add a subtle elegance to desserts.

Gemüsefondue mit heißer Sardellensauce.name: Vegetable Fondue with Hot Anchovy Sauce
steps: Rinse the anchovy fillets under cold running water, pat dry, and finely chop. Peel the garlic and finely chop.
Melt 50g butter in a pan, add the garlic, and gently sauté over low heat without letting it brown. Add the anchovy fillets and let them slowly dissolve.

Prepare the vegetables: divide the cauliflower into florets, slice the fennel into strips, cut the celery into 3cm pieces, deseed the bell peppers and cut into strips, peel the carrots and cut into batons, clean the mushrooms and halve them.
Remove the pan with the hot sauce from the heat and gradually stir in the olive oil. Keep warm over gentle heat until a creamy sauce forms.
To serve, set a fondue burner or warming stand on the table and arrange the vegetables decoratively. Each guest dips the vegetables into the hot anchovy sauce. Serve with fresh white bread.
Gemüsefondue mit heißer Sardellensauce.tips: Bagna cauda can also be enriched with truffle or a fine nut oil. Grissini (breadsticks) make a great accompaniment.

Schinken mit Feigen.name: Ham with Figs
ingredients: 150g thinly sliced San Daniele ham (Parma ham works as a substitute)
coarsely ground black pepper from the mill
steps: Place the fresh figs in a bowl of ice water and chill thoroughly (about 15 minutes).
Carefully separate the delicate slices of ham and arrange them on a serving platter. Drain the figs, quarter them or score them crosswise, and place them alongside the ham.
At the table, guests help themselves to ham and figs. Provide a pepper mill for the ham. Figs that are scored crosswise are especially easy to peel. Serve with fresh, crusty white bread on the side.
Schinken mit Feigen.tips: Variation: serve spicy Italian salami instead of ham with the figs. Or arrange the ham slices with a beautiful ripe cantaloupe melon.

Marinierte, rohe Rinderlende.name: Marinated Raw Beef Tenderloin
ingredients: 200g well-aged beef tenderloin (center cut, trimmed of sinew and fat)
150g small brown cremini mushrooms (or fresh porcini)
8 tbsp cold-pressed, aromatic olive oil, plus extra olive oil for serving
coarsely ground black pepper from the mill
steps: Wrap the beef tenderloin in plastic wrap, place it in the freezer, and let it partially freeze (about 1 hour).

Brush four large plates each with 1 tbsp of olive oil. Slice the beef tenderloin paper-thin using a very sharp, large knife or an electric slicer. Peel the slices off the wrap, and if needed, gently flatten them even thinner with the flat side of a meat mallet. Lay them flat side by side on the plates and drizzle with the remaining oil.

Clean and wipe 150g of cremini mushrooms. Slice thinly and scatter over the meat. Shave 50g of Parmesan into thin slices and sprinkle over the meat. Season with coarsely ground pepper and garnish with basil leaves. Halve the lemons and serve alongside the carpaccio with salt and fresh olive oil. Season further at the table according to personal taste.

Kalbfleisch mit Thunfischsauce.name: Veal with Tuna Sauce
ingredients: 1 can of tuna in water, drained (150g)
steps: Place the veal in a pot and pour in 750ml white wine. Roughly chop the celery, carrot, and onion, and add to the marinade along with 1 bay leaf and 2 cloves. Cover and leave to marinate for 24 hours, turning several times.
Add just enough water to barely cover the meat and bring to a boil. Stir in 1 tsp salt. Simmer uncovered over low heat for just under 1 hour. Allow to cool in the cooking broth.

Drain the tuna. Rinse the 3 anchovy fillets and pat dry with kitchen paper, then finely chop. In a blender, purée the tuna, anchovies, 2 egg yolks, 2 tbsp capers, the juice of ½ lemon, and 2 tbsp wine vinegar until smooth. Gradually pour in a few tablespoons of the veal broth and about 200ml olive oil, blending to create a smooth, creamy sauce. Season with salt and pepper.
Slice the veal as thinly as possible and arrange on a serving platter. Cover evenly with the tuna sauce, then cover the platter and refrigerate for 3–4 hours to allow the flavors to meld.
To serve, thinly slice 1½ lemons and use them to garnish the veal. Scatter 1 tbsp of capers over the top.
Kalbfleisch mit Thunfischsauce.tips: Important: serve well chilled, with plenty of white bread. The sauce should be smooth, nicely creamy, and not too thick. Thin it with a little veal broth if needed.

Marinierte Paprikaschoten.name: Marinated Bell Peppers
ingredients: 4 large bell peppers (a mix of red, green, and yellow)
steps: Wash the bell peppers and halve them, removing the seeds and membranes. Place the halves skin-side up in a very hot oven or under the broiler. Roast until the skin blisters and chars. Remove, cover with a damp cloth, and leave to cool. Carefully peel off the skin.
Collect any juices from the peeled peppers if possible, and mix with the juice of 1 lemon. Peel 2 garlic cloves, slice thinly, and stir in.

Cut the pepper halves into strips about 3cm wide. Arrange on a platter, season with salt and coarsely ground pepper, and drizzle evenly with the prepared dressing and 6 tbsp olive oil. Cover and leave to marinate until the flavors develop.
Marinierte Paprikaschoten.tips: Peeling is even easier in the microwave: prepare the peppers as above, place on a plate, and cook on full power for about 3 minutes. The skin can then be removed very quickly and easily.

Süß-saure Möhren.name: Sweet and Sour Carrots
ingredients: 1 fresh herb bouquet of rosemary, parsley, mint, and bay leaf
steps: Clean the carrots and cut into 1cm thick rounds or slightly wider diagonal slices. Place in a pot with 125ml each of water, wine, and vinegar, and bring to a heat.
Peel 2 garlic cloves, halve them, and stir in along with 1 tbsp sugar and 4 tbsp oil. Add the herb bouquet, cover with a lid, and gently cook the carrots for 10–15 minutes.

Let cool slightly, then remove the garlic and herbs. Transfer the carrots separately to a bowl. Strain the cooking liquid through a fine sieve and drizzle over the carrots. Cover and refrigerate for 1–2 days to marinate.
To serve, sprinkle with fresh basil cut into thin strips.
Süß-saure Möhren.tips: Pickled vegetables are not only particularly delicious — they are also fantastically easy to prepare in advance. Using this recipe, you can pickle all kinds of other vegetables to your heart's content, such as zucchini batons, small onions, or baby turnips.

Bohnen mit Wurst.name: Beans with Sausage
ingredients: 200g lean boiling pork sausage (or cotechino-style cooked sausage)
½ tsp fresh marjoram (or ¼ tsp dried)
steps: Soak 100g dried beans overnight in water, then cook the next day in the soaking water until tender (about 30 minutes).
Meanwhile, peel 2 onions and cut into eighths. Melt 30g butter in a pan until foamy, stir in the onions, and sauté until translucent. Sprinkle in ½ tsp marjoram, season with salt and pepper.

Cut the sausage into thin slices, mix into the onions, and briefly fry together. Drain the cooked beans, let them drain, and fold them in. Drizzle with 2 tbsp oil and a little red wine vinegar if desired, adjust seasoning with salt and pepper, and serve warm.

Sellerie mit Käse.name: Celery with Cheese
steps: Clean and wash the celery, reserving the leafy tops. Cut the individual stalks into 3–4 long pieces and let them drain.
Mash the Gorgonzola with a fork and stir in 4 tbsp cream. Gradually fold in 100g Mascarpone, stirring to a smooth cream.
Mound the cheese cream onto a flat dish and grind pepper over it. Arrange the celery sticks alongside and garnish with the celery leaves.
Sellerie mit Käse.tips: Celery is a favorite vegetable in Italian cuisine. The largest growing region is Piedmont, closely followed by Lazio and Apulia.

Polenta-Auflauf.name: Polenta Bake
steps: Bring 1 litre of salted water to a boil in a heavy pot. Slowly pour in 250g of coarse cornmeal, stirring constantly with a long, narrow wooden spoon. Caution: the mixture can splatter! Remove from the heat briefly if needed.

Reduce the heat and let the polenta simmer for 30 minutes, stirring as vigorously and frequently as possible. A crust will form on the sides of the pot and the polenta will begin to pull away from the bottom and walls. If it becomes too thick, add a little boiling water one spoonful at a time. Turn the finished polenta out onto a large wooden board or a cloth dusted with semolina, spread it to about 5 cm thick, and leave to cool.

Finely chop 1 onion. Heat 2 tbsp of butter in a pan and sauté the onion until translucent. Add the ground beef and veal sausage meat in small portions and fry until crumbly. Season with salt and pepper, then stir in 2 tbsp of tomato paste and 2 tbsp of water. Simmer for 5 minutes. Preheat the oven to 150°C.
Melt the remaining butter. Grease a large ovenproof dish with 2 tbsp of the melted butter. Cut the cooled, firm polenta into 1 cm thick slices — this is easiest done with a length of sturdy string.

Lay a layer of polenta slices in the greased dish. Drizzle with a little melted butter. Set aside a few tablespoons of freshly grated Parmesan to scatter over at serving time. Sprinkle with 2–3 tbsp of Parmesan and cover with the meat ragù. Add another layer of polenta slices, drizzle with melted butter, and add more Parmesan and meat ragù. Continue in this way until all the ingredients are used up. Finish with a layer of polenta and spread the remaining butter on top.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 1) for about 30 minutes. Scatter over the remaining Parmesan and serve the polenta bake piping hot in the dish.

Polenta mit Sardellen.name: Polenta with Anchovies
steps: Bring 1 litre of salted water to a boil in a large pot and slowly pour in 250g of coarse cornmeal. Let it simmer for 40 minutes, stirring as frequently as possible.
Preheat the oven to 225°C. Melt 100g of butter without letting it brown. Grease an ovenproof dish with butter.
Once the polenta has reached the right consistency and is pulling away from the bottom and sides of the pot, transfer it to the greased baking dish and smooth the surface.

Rinse and dry 10 anchovy fillets, chop them up, and distribute evenly over the polenta. Drizzle with half the melted butter and sprinkle with half the Parmesan. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 4) for 10 minutes. Serve with the remaining melted butter and Parmesan on the side.
Polenta mit Sardellen.tips: Traditionally, this speciality from Trentino is made with buckwheat flour, which gives a more robust flavour but is less visually appealing. It also works wonderfully as a side dish with pan-fried pork ribs or simply alongside a fresh salad.

Speckknödel-Suppe.name: Bacon Dumpling Soup
ingredients: 250g day-old white bread or bread rolls
steps: Cut 250g of white bread into small cubes and place in a bowl. Whisk 3 eggs with 1/4 litre of milk, pour over the bread, and leave to soak for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cut the bacon and 1 onion into very small cubes and finely chop the parsley. Render the bacon in a small pan, then stir in the onion and half the parsley and fry together for 2 minutes.

Cut the salami into small cubes. Stir into the bacon mixture along with the remaining parsley. Combine with the soaked bread and mix in 6 tbsp of flour. Season the mixture with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg.

Bring 1.5 litres of beef broth to a boil. With dampened hands, shape about 10 evenly sized small dumplings and poach them gently in the broth until cooked through. Cut the chives into fine rings, scatter over the finished soup, and serve.

Spinatnocken.name: Spinach Dumplings
ingredients: 4–5 tbsp plain flour (plus more if needed)
steps: Cut 250g of white bread into small cubes. Pour 150ml of milk over them and mix well. Cover with a plate, place a weight on top, and leave to soak for at least 2 hours.

Clean and wash the leaf spinach, removing any tough stems. Bring a generous amount of salted water to a boil and blanch the spinach leaves for 2 minutes. Refresh in cold water, drain well, and leave to cool. Then squeeze out as much moisture as possible and chop as finely as you can.
Thoroughly knead the chopped spinach together with the soaked bread, 2 eggs, and 4–5 tbsp of flour. Season with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg.

Bring 2 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Use two tablespoons to shape a test dumpling from the bread mixture and drop it into the boiling water. Let it cook for about 5 minutes. Adjust the consistency of the remaining mixture with extra flour or milk as needed. Shape the remaining dumplings and cook them the same way.

Remove with a slotted spoon, drain well, and arrange on a warmed serving plate. Melt 50g of butter in a pan and toss the sage leaves in it. Add the dumplings to the pan and coat them thoroughly in the butter. Sprinkle with 50g of grated cheese and serve hot.
Spinatnocken.tips: Ideal as a side dish with a saucy main course such as braised meat or a ragù.

Kartoffelklößchen.name: Potato Gnocchi
steps: For the dough, wash the potatoes and cook them in salted water until tender. Peel and press them while still hot through a potato ricer onto a lightly floured work surface. While the potatoes are cooking, prepare the tomato sauce.

Lightly salt the mashed potato, then gradually knead in enough flour to form a smooth, uniform dough. The exact amount of flour will depend on the potato variety — the dough should no longer stick to your fingers.
