For the sauce, briefly blanch the tomatoes in boiling water, refresh in cold water, and peel. Deseed and finely chop the flesh. Finely chop 1 onion and sauté in 1 tbsp of melted butter. Stir in the tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer slowly into a thick sauce.

To shape the gnocchi, take egg-sized portions of dough and roll each one on a floured surface into a finger-thick rope. Cut into 2–3 cm pieces. Press each piece against the inside of a grater or the back of a fork with your thumb or index finger. Place the finished gnocchi on the lightly floured work surface. Bring 2 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot.

Add the gnocchi to the boiling water in batches and poach at a gentle simmer for about 4 minutes. Stir carefully from time to time to prevent them from sticking together. As soon as the gnocchi float to the surface, remove them with a slotted spoon and drain well.
Gently fry the sage leaves in 1 tbsp of hot butter. Toss the gnocchi in the sage butter and serve alongside the tomato sauce. Sprinkle with freshly grated Parmesan.
Kartoffelklößchen.tips: Wine suggestion: a soft, light red wine such as a Barbaresco or a Barbera.

Graupensuppe.name: Pearl Barley Soup
steps: Cut 80g of bacon into fine strips. Clean and wash the leek and 2 celery stalks, then cut into strips. Slice 2 carrots and dice 2 potatoes. Chop 1 onion and 2 cloves of garlic.

Render the bacon strips in a large pot, then add 2 tbsp of oil. Sauté the vegetables, stirring, for 5 minutes. Add 200g of pearl barley and 1 bay leaf, pour in just under 2 litres of broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and let the soup simmer gently for about 1 hour until the barley is tender. Remove the bay leaf and season with salt and pepper. Stir in the parsley leaves and serve.
Graupensuppe.tips: Pearl barley is hulled and polished barley — it may also be labelled as 'pot barley' or 'pearled barley' in shops.

Käsefondue.name: Cheese Fondue
ingredients: 300g Fontina cheese (alternatively Provolone or Italian mountain cheese with at least 40% fat content)
steps: Cut the Fontina into small cubes, place in a bowl, and pour over 100ml of milk. Cover and leave to soak in a cool place for several hours or overnight.
For the cooking process, use a double boiler (or ideally a simmering pot). Place a stainless steel bowl with a rounded base over a warm water bath, add 1 tbsp of butter, and let it melt.
Add the cheese cubes together with the milk and melt slowly over a low heat, stirring constantly and vigorously with a whisk.

Once the cheese has melted and begins to pull into strings, increase the temperature of the water bath. Add 3 egg yolks one at a time, stirring each in firmly with a wooden spoon. Continue stirring until the mixture is smooth, creamy, and no longer stringy.
Divide the cheese cream into individual bowls, grind pepper over the top. Toast the bread slices and serve alongside.

Wirsing-Käse-Suppe.name: Savoy Cabbage and Cheese Soup
steps: Clean the savoy cabbage, removing the outer leaves and the core. Blanch the remaining leaves in boiling salted water for 8 minutes. Refresh in cold water, drain well, and cut into centimetre-wide strips.
Cut the bacon slices into strips and fry on both sides in a pan, then remove. Grease a large ovenproof dish with the rendered bacon fat. Preheat the oven to 175°C.

Lightly toast 8 slices of bread. Place 4 of them on the bottom of the dish and cover with some of the bacon and cabbage strips. Lay a few cheese slices on top. Layer in the remaining bacon and cabbage alternately. Finish with the remaining bread and cheese slices, and dot with 2 tbsp of butter.

Pour in enough broth to just cover the ingredients (about 1 litre). Place the dish in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) and bake for 45 minutes. Season with salt and pepper if needed and serve piping hot.
Wirsing-Käse-Suppe.tips: For the ultimate indulgence, crown this dish with a thin layer of freshly shaved truffles — ideally from Piedmont.

Gemischtes gekochtes Fleisch mit grüner.name: Mixed Boiled Meats with Green Sauce
ingredients: 1 fresh pork sausage seasoned with garlic (300g)
steps: Barely cover the veal tongue with water and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer gently for about 1½ hours until tender.

At the same time, bring 3 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot, add ½ tsp peppercorns and the beef. Reduce the heat and let the beef simmer gently for about 30 minutes. Then add the poularde.
Clean and roughly chop the celery stalks, carrots, leek, and 2 onions. Add them to the large pot along with the veal. Continue to simmer everything gently for about 1 hour.

For the salsa verde: pluck the parsley leaves and blend them finely together with 6 anchovy fillets, 1 garlic clove, 1–2 tbsp capers, and 2 egg yolks in a blender (or pound with a mortar and pestle). Gradually drizzle in 5 tbsp olive oil, stirring constantly, until a smooth, creamy sauce forms. Season with salt and vinegar.

Pierce the pork sausage several times with a fork and heat it slowly, covered with water. Once the veal tongue is cooked, refresh it in cold water, make an incision at the tip with a kitchen knife, and peel off the skin. Warm it through again before serving. Slice all the meats thinly, cut the poularde and sausage into portions, and arrange everything on a well-warmed platter. Serve with salsa verde and the cooked vegetables.
Gemischtes gekochtes Fleisch mit grüner.tips: Boiled salted potatoes also make a great side dish. Pickled mustard fruits (Mostarda di Cremona) are traditionally served alongside the meats.

Lammragout.name: Lamb Ragout
steps: Cut the lamb into 2–3cm cubes. Cut the bacon into fine strips. Finely chop 1 onion and 2 garlic cloves. Heat 5 tbsp oil in a braising pot. Add the bacon first, then the onion and garlic, and sauté while stirring.

Add the lamb cubes in batches and brown well on all sides. Season with salt and pepper, dust with 1 tbsp flour, and allow to colour slightly. Deglaze with 125ml beef stock, stir well, and braise covered over a gentle heat for about 1 hour.

After about 30 minutes of cooking time, pour in 125ml red wine and add a pinch of cinnamon. Stir in 2 tbsp tomato paste and continue to braise until done. In the final minutes, remove the lid if needed to allow the sauce to reduce and thicken. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Lammragout.tips: Boiled salted potatoes or polenta — either as a soft porridge or sliced and briefly pan-fried as described in the recipe on page 102 — make excellent accompaniments to the lamb ragout.

Kaninchen mit Paprika.name: Rabbit with Bell Peppers
ingredients: 1 sprig fresh rosemary (or 1 tsp dried rosemary)
steps: Rinse the rabbit briefly and pat dry. Cut into 8–10 pieces. Rub all over with salt, pepper, and a halved garlic clove.

Dice 80g fatty bacon into small cubes and render in a large braising pot. Remove the bacon pieces and set aside. Add 2 tbsp olive oil and heat. Add the rabbit pieces and brown well on all sides. Add the rosemary sprig and pour in 250ml beef stock. Cover and braise for about 45 minutes.
Wash the bell peppers, halve them, and remove the white ribs and seeds. Cut the pepper halves into 1cm-wide strips. Briefly rinse 5 anchovy fillets, pat dry with kitchen paper, and chop finely.

Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan. Sauté the pepper strips, then press in 2–3 garlic cloves through a garlic press. Add 4 tbsp white wine vinegar, bring to a vigorous boil, and reduce by half. Stir in the anchovies.

Add the pepper mixture to the rabbit and braise everything together for a further 15 minutes. Remove the rosemary sprig and season the sauce with salt and pepper. Scatter the bacon pieces over the top and serve the rabbit hot.
Kaninchen mit Paprika.tips: A light white wine can be used instead of beef stock for braising. Wine suggestion: a mild red wine, such as Donnaz.

Kalbszunge mit pikanter Sauce.name: Veal Tongue with Piquant Sauce
steps: Soak 20g dried mushrooms in 125ml water for 2 hours. Rinse the veal tongue under cold water, place in a pot with 1 litre of water and 250ml white wine, and bring to a heat. Clean and roughly chop 1 carrot, 2 celery stalks, and the leek. Peel and halve 1 onion. Wash ½ lemon and slice it. Add all these ingredients to the pot together with 1 clove, 1 bay leaf, and ½ tsp peppercorns. Add ½ tsp salt. Half-cover and cook just below simmering point for about 1¾ hours.

After 30 minutes of cooking time, prepare the sauce: finely chop 1 onion, 3 garlic cloves, 1 celery stalk, and ½ bunch of parsley. Melt 2 tbsp butter in a pot and gently sweat the mixture for 10 minutes. Drain the soaked mushrooms, reserving the soaking liquid. Strain the liquid through a fine sieve and set aside. Finely chop the mushrooms, add them to the pot with the vegetables, and sauté.
Pour in 250ml white wine and the mushroom soaking liquid, season with salt and pepper. Simmer vigorously for 20 minutes. If needed, add a little of the tongue cooking liquid.

After 1¾ hours, remove the veal tongue and refresh in cold water. Use a sharp knife to score and peel off the skin. Cut the tongue on the diagonal into ½cm-thick slices. Place in the tomato sauce and finish cooking over a gentle heat for about 10 minutes. Adjust the seasoning, roughly chop the remaining parsley, and scatter over the top.
Briefly blanch the tomatoes in boiling water, refresh in cold water, and peel. Roughly chop the flesh, pass through a sieve, and add to the pot.
Kalbszunge mit pikanter Sauce.tips: Wine suggestion: a full-bodied white wine, such as a Pinot Grigio from Trentino.

Geflügelragout mit Tomaten und Rührei.name: Chicken Ragout with Tomatoes and Scrambled Eggs
steps: Cut the poularde into 6–8 pieces with a sharp knife. Rub with salt and pepper. Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a large frying pan or braising pot and brown the chicken pieces all over for about 10 minutes. Remove only the breast pieces and set aside.

Meanwhile, blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed them. Roughly chop and add to the chicken. Stir in 125ml white wine and 250ml chicken stock. Press in 2 garlic cloves through a garlic press. Cover and braise for 45 minutes.

Clean the mushrooms and halve the larger ones if necessary. Peel 200g pearl onions. Heat 1 tbsp butter in a frying pan, add the mushrooms and onions, and sauté gently for about 10 minutes while stirring. Season with 2 tbsp lemon juice, salt, and pepper.

Bring the remaining lemon juice and 125ml water to a boil in a small pot. Simmer the 4 prawns for 6 minutes until they turn pink (alternatively, quickly sauté in 2 tbsp olive oil with a garlic clove). Heat through once more just before serving.

Remove the cooked chicken pieces from the pot. Reduce the sauce until thickened and adjust seasoning. Return all the chicken pieces, including the breast, to the pot and heat everything through thoroughly. Stir in the mushrooms and onions.
Melt 1 tbsp butter in the mushroom pan. Crack the 4 eggs in one at a time and lightly scramble with a fork, cooking over a gentle heat until just set. Season with salt and pepper.
Chop the parsley and toast the bread slices. Arrange the chicken ragout with the prawns, scrambled eggs, and toast, and serve scattered with basil and parsley.
Geflügelragout mit Tomaten und Rührei.tips: Feel free to add more vegetables to the chicken, such as celery and spring onions. The prawns can also be sautéed in olive oil and garlic instead of poached in water.

Rotkohl (Südtirol).name: Red Cabbage (South Tyrol Style)
steps: Remove the outer leaves from the red cabbage, quarter it lengthways, and cut out the core. Slice the quarters crosswise into fine strips, wash, and drain well.

Cut 50g bacon into very small cubes. Place in a pot with 2 tbsp butter and render over a gentle heat. Remove the crispy bacon bits and keep warm. Finely chop 1 onion, add to the rendered fat, and sweat until translucent.

Stir in the shredded red cabbage, season generously with salt and pepper, and add a pinch of sugar. Pour in 200ml red wine, cover, and cook for 30 minutes. Scatter the crispy bacon bits over the top before serving.
Rotkohl (Südtirol).tips: For the adventurous cook: braise the red cabbage in olive oil, or stir in 2 tbsp redcurrant jelly at the very end. Pairs wonderfully as a side dish with game and game birds.

Gefüllte Zwiebeln (Piemont).name: Stuffed Onions (Piedmont Style)
ingredients: 50g freshly grated mountain cheese (such as Bergkäse) or Parmesan
steps: Peel the onions and pre-cook in boiling salted water for 15 minutes. Drain and leave to cool slightly. Cut in half crosswise and hollow out about one-third of the centre. Finely chop roughly half of the scooped-out onion flesh and set the rest aside for another use.

Melt 1 tbsp butter in a frying pan and sauté the ground beef and finely chopped onion. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper, add 2 cl grappa, and reduce vigorously. Remove from the heat and leave to cool. Preheat the oven to 200°C.

Beat the egg and mix into the ground beef together with 50g freshly grated cheese. Finely slice the parsley and stir half of it into the mixture. Season the filling generously with salt and pepper. Spoon into the hollowed-out onions.

Grease an ovenproof dish with 1 tsp butter. Place the onions inside. Melt 2 tbsp butter in the frying pan and toast 3 tbsp breadcrumbs until golden brown. Scatter the breadcrumbs and a few small sage leaves over the onions and drizzle with 2 cl grappa. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 45 minutes. Scatter the remaining parsley over the top before serving.
Gefüllte Zwiebeln (Piemont).tips: The splash of grappa adds a distinctive character — but it can be replaced with wine or a robust beef stock. A fun variation: fill with cooked pumpkin purée, eggs, and finely crumbled amaretti biscuits.

Frischer Pilzsalat (Aostatal).name: Fresh Mushroom Salad (Aosta Valley Style)
ingredients: 300g fresh mixed mushrooms (e.g. porcini, button mushrooms, and a few chanterelles)
steps: Clean the fresh mushrooms and slice them thinly. Drizzle with the juice of ½ lemon (briefly sauté chanterelles in a little butter).

Whisk 1 egg yolk with salt, pepper, and the remaining lemon juice. Press in 1 garlic clove. Gradually drizzle in 100ml olive oil while stirring constantly until a smooth dressing forms. Finely chop the parsley and stir in. Taste and adjust the seasoning, then drizzle the dressing over the mushrooms.
Frischer Pilzsalat (Aostatal).tips: Variation: make a dressing from lemon juice, olive oil, chopped anchovy fillet, and a finely crumbled hard-boiled egg yolk. Alternatively, slice fresh wild mushrooms, sauté gently in a little butter or walnut oil, season lightly, and serve lukewarm as a salad.

Apfelstrudel (Südtirol).name: Apple Strudel (South Tyrol)
ingredients: fat for greasing the baking sheet
steps: For the dough, mound 250g flour on a pastry board and make a well in the centre. Add 2 tbsp melted butter, 1 egg and a pinch of salt. Mix the ingredients in from the edges and knead into a dough, gradually adding about 100ml lukewarm water. Knead vigorously until the dough is smooth and elastic. Shape into a ball, brush with oil and place in a plastic bag. Leave to rest for at least 20 minutes.

For the filling, soak 100g raisins in a little water. Quarter and peel the apples, removing the cores. Slice the apple quarters into thin pieces and place in a bowl. Mix in the finely grated zest of 1 lemon, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 3 tbsp pine nuts, 70g sugar and ½ tsp ground cinnamon. Leave to macerate well. Just before filling the strudel, drain the soaked raisins and mix them in with the apples too. Preheat the oven to 200°C and grease a baking sheet.

To roll out the dough, dust a large kitchen cloth with flour. First roll the ball of dough out thinly with a rolling pin. Then slide both hands (backs upward) under the sheet of dough and carefully stretch it out in all directions until it is paper-thin.

Drizzle the dough surface thinly with melted butter and scatter over 2–3 tbsp breadcrumbs. Spread the apple mixture evenly on top, leaving a 2 cm border all around. Brush the edges with melted butter and fold them inward over the filling.

Lift the kitchen cloth along one of the long sides and roll the strudel up in one confident motion. Use the cloth to lift it onto the baking sheet. Brush the surface generously with melted butter. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 1 hour. Leave to cool slightly and serve dusted with icing sugar.
Apfelstrudel (Südtirol).tips: As well as tart apples, other slightly acidic fruits also work well as a filling — for example pears or apricots. Pour over 200ml cream and brush the dough with it from time to time during baking.

Weinschaum mit Beeren (Piemont).name: Zabaione with Berries (Piedmont)
ingredients: 4 tbsp dry Marsala (fortified wine from Sicily)
steps: Wash the berries, drain and remove the stems; halve any strawberries if needed. Mix together in a bowl, drizzle with the juice of ½ lemon and refrigerate.

Fill a large saucepan with water and bring to a gentle simmer. Set a smaller, round stainless-steel bowl over the bain-marie. Add 4 egg yolks, 4 tbsp sugar and the finely grated zest of ½ lemon, and whisk thoroughly. Gradually drizzle in 4 tbsp Marsala.
Whisk over gentle heat until a light, foamy cream forms. Remove from the heat, divide among 4 dishes and garnish with the berries.
Weinschaum mit Beeren (Piemont).tips: Even easier: prepare the cream in a double boiler. Variations: use white wine, sparkling wine or brandy instead. Can be made ahead: cool the zabaione in a cold water bath, fold in 200g whipped cream and keep well chilled until ready to serve.

Mandelplätzchen (Piemont).name: Almond Cookies (Piedmont)
steps: Grind the almonds finely and mix with 150g sugar and 1 tsp bitter almond extract.
Beat 4 egg whites to stiff peaks, gradually adding the remaining 50g sugar. Fold the almond mixture into the egg whites a spoonful at a time, stirring to a smooth paste.

Grease a baking sheet and dust with 1 tbsp flour. Fill the almond mixture into a piping bag fitted with a plain nozzle and pipe walnut-sized mounds onto the sheets. Leave to stand for about 2 hours (or drop them from a teaspoon).
Preheat the oven to 150°C. Dry the almond cookies out rather than bake them for about 1 hour (gas mark 1). Dust with icing sugar.
Mandelplätzchen (Piemont).tips: Amaretti are used in a wide variety of modern recipes for desserts, but also as a flavouring in savoury vegetable dishes. Serve as a biscuit to dip into Vin Santo (a classic dessert wine), or simply alongside an espresso after dinner.

Ertrunkene Kapuziner.name: Drowned Capuchins
ingredients: oil for frying and for the dish
steps: Heat ¼ litre red wine with 50g vanilla sugar, 1 cinnamon stick and 2 cloves. Cut the zest of ½ lemon in a spiral strip and add it.
Cut the white bread into 2 cm thick slices. Beat 4 eggs and stir in 3 tbsp ground almonds. Heat a generous amount of oil in a large frying pan.
Dip the bread slices in the egg mixture on both sides and fry in the hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on kitchen paper.
Preheat the oven to 175°C. Grease an ovenproof dish with 2 tbsp oil. Strain the hot red wine through a fine sieve back into the saucepan and return to the heat. Briefly steep 100g raisins in it.

Layer the fried bread slices in the dish, sprinkling each slice lightly with sugar, drizzling over 1–2 tbsp red wine and scattering with raisins. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 10 minutes. Serve hot.

Fritierte Teigscherben.name: Fried Pastry Shards
ingredients: 4 cl plum brandy, rum or grappa
about 1 kg clarified butter for deep-frying
steps: Beat 2 eggs with 60g vanilla sugar until very light and fluffy. Finely grate the zest of 1 lemon and stir in together with 4 cl spirits and a pinch of salt.
Melt 50g butter. Mound 350g flour on the work surface and make a well in the centre. Add the melted, slightly cooled butter and the foamy egg cream to the centre and knead into a smooth dough.

Heat 1 kg clarified butter in a saucepan. Dust the work surface or pastry board with flour. Roll the dough out as thinly as possible. Cut into irregular rectangles or diamonds with a pastry wheel (sides about 4–5 cm). Make a cross-shaped cut in the centre of each piece.
Deep-fry the crostoli in the hot fat until golden brown. Drain very thoroughly on kitchen paper, dust with icing sugar. Serve warm or cold.
Fritierte Teigscherben.tips: Wine suggestion: a delicately fresh sparkling wine, for example Trento Classico.

Geröstetes Weißbrot mit Hühnerlebercreme.name: Toasted White Bread with Chicken Liver Pâté and Olive Paste
ingredients: ½ unwaxed lemon (juice and finely grated zest)
1 tsp each fresh rosemary and thyme (or ½ tsp each dried)
steps: For the chicken liver pâté: trim the chicken livers and cut into small pieces. Place in a small bowl with 1 bay leaf and ½ tsp juniper berries, pour over ⅛ litre red wine. Leave to marinate overnight.

Very finely chop the onion, 1 celery stalk and 1 carrot. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan and melt 1 tbsp butter in it. Sauté the vegetables. Remove the chicken livers from the marinade and pat dry; strain the marinade through a sieve.

Add the livers to the vegetables and briefly sear. Stir in 2 tbsp tomato paste and the strained marinade. Simmer gently for 10 minutes, then season with salt, pepper and the juice and zest of ½ lemon. Finely chop the parsley and stir in. Leave to cool slightly, then blend finely in a food processor with 1 tbsp capers. Stir in 1 tbsp softened butter and adjust the seasoning.

For the olive paste: rinse the 2 anchovy fillets, pat dry and chop finely. Deseed the chilli. Blend finely with the olives, all the herbs, 4 cloves of garlic and 1 tbsp capers. Gradually pour in about 100 ml olive oil until a smooth, creamy paste forms. Season with 1 tsp lemon juice, salt and freshly ground pepper.
Toast the bread slices in a toaster or in the oven. Spread each slice with one of the pastes.
Geröstetes Weißbrot mit Hühnerlebercreme.tips: If possible, use authentic Tuscan country bread (hearty crust, dense crumb, lightly seasoned or completely unsalted). Cut the bread slices on the smaller side. Another Tuscan variation: a hearty mixture of fried spleen, onions, anchovies and capers.

Gekühlter Brotsalat.name: Chilled Bread Salad
ingredients: 1 small yellow and 1 small red bell pepper
steps: The day before, remove the crusts from the bread and slice; leave to dry out. The next day, briefly bring ⅛ litre water to the boil with 6 tbsp red wine vinegar and 1 bay leaf, leave to cool, then pour the liquid over the bread slices. Leave to soak for 30 minutes.
Blanch the tomatoes, peel and deseed them. Cut into thin strips. Dice the bell peppers very finely. Cut 2 spring onions into fine rings. Finely chop ½ bunch parsley.

Squeeze out the bread and tear into small pieces, removing the bay leaf. Mix with the tomatoes, peppers, spring onions and 2 tbsp capers. Press 3 cloves of garlic and whisk together with 8 tbsp olive oil, then stir in the chopped parsley, salt and freshly ground pepper. Drizzle the dressing over the salad and mix thoroughly; refrigerate for 1 hour. Before serving, adjust seasoning with salt, pepper and vinegar. Scatter over the remaining herb leaves.
Gekühlter Brotsalat.tips: Before serving, adjust seasoning with salt, pepper and vinegar. Scatter over the remaining herb leaves.

Pilzsuppe mit Brot.name: Mushroom Soup with Bread
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh thyme leaves (or 1 tsp dried)
steps: Clean the mushrooms and slice thinly. Blanch the tomatoes, peel and deseed them; finely chop the flesh. Cut 2 celery stalks and 1 onion into small dice.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan. Sauté the onion and celery in it. Press in 1 clove of garlic. Pour in 1 litre hot beef stock and stir in the chopped tomatoes. Season with ½ tbsp thyme, salt and pepper. Simmer gently in the open pot for 20 minutes.

Stir the sliced mushrooms into the soup and cook for a further 10 minutes over gentle heat. Cut the chives into fine rings. Toast 8 slices of white bread and divide among 4 soup bowls. Taste and adjust the seasoning of the soup, then ladle it over the bread slices. Serve scattered with thyme, chives and 50g freshly grated Parmesan.
Pilzsuppe mit Brot.tips: A hearty version of what was once a very humble meal — in the old days this soup contained nothing more than bread, oil, cheese and water!

Nudelauflauf mit Fleischsauce.name: Pasta Bake with Meat Sauce
ingredients: 3 tbsp butter, cut into small pieces
Butter or oil for greasing the dish
steps: For the pasta dough, mound 350g flour with 125g semolina on a work surface and make a well in the center. Add 4 eggs, 1/2 tsp salt, and 2 tbsp wine into the well and knead into a smooth dough. Cover with a cloth and set aside.

For the meat sauce, soak 20g dried mushrooms in water. Blanch the calf's brain or sweetbreads in boiling salted water for 5 minutes, then rinse under cold water. Remove any membranes and veins. Set aside for later.

Dice the bacon, 1 carrot, and 1 onion. Fry the bacon in 1 tbsp butter, then briefly sauté the vegetables. Brown the ground lamb in batches. Pour in 1/4 l wine, bring to a boil, and let it reduce by half. Stir in 1/4 l broth, 2 tbsp tomato paste, salt, and pepper. Cover and simmer gently for 30 minutes.

Roll out the pasta dough thinly on a lightly floured surface. Prepare a baking dish and cut the pasta sheets to fit. Lay them on a kitchen towel and let them dry briefly. Bring 3 l of salted water to a boil in a large pot, cook the pasta sheets in batches for 3 minutes, rinse under cold water, and drain on the kitchen towel.

Cut the chicken livers and calf's brain (or sweetbreads) into small cubes. Drain the mushrooms and chop them. Add both to the meat sauce. Pour in the mushroom soaking liquid and 100 ml milk, season with 1 pinch of cinnamon. Cook over low heat for 10–15 minutes, then adjust seasoning.

Béchamel sauce: Melt 2 tbsp butter in a saucepan, whisk in 2 tbsp flour and cook briefly. Remove from heat and pour in 1/2 l milk. Return to the heat and bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Simmer gently for 10 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg.

Grease the baking dish and line with a layer of pasta sheets. Spread some meat sauce and béchamel sauce on top, then sprinkle with Parmesan. Continue layering all the ingredients in this way. Finish the top with a generous layer of Parmesan and dot with 3 tbsp of butter pieces. Cover well and refrigerate for several hours. Preheat the oven to 200°C. Bake the dish for about 30 minutes until a nice golden crust has formed. Cut into portions and serve hot.
Nudelauflauf mit Fleischsauce.tips: A specialty from the Marche region. The pasta dough is flavored with dessert wine, and the meat sauce is rounded off with a hint of cinnamon. To save time, you can use store-bought lasagne sheets instead.

Breite Bandnudeln mit Hasensauce.name: Pappardelle with Hare Sauce
steps: For the sauce, cut the hare into pieces and place in a bowl. Pour over 1/2 l red wine. Roughly chop 3 celery stalks, 2 onions, and 3 garlic cloves, and add to the marinade along with 1 tsp fennel seeds, 1 bay leaf, 2 cloves, and 6 peppercorns. Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for about 24 hours, turning the meat several times.

Remove the meat from the marinade and pat dry thoroughly with paper towels. Strain the marinade liquid, removing the cloves and peppercorns. Let the vegetables drain well in the strainer and reserve the liquid.

Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a braising pot. Add the hare pieces in batches and brown on all sides. Add the vegetables and sauté, stirring. Pour in the strained marinade liquid. Season generously with thyme leaves, salt, and pepper. Cover and braise for about 2 hours.

Blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed them, then finely chop the flesh. Remove the hare pieces from the pot, strip the cooked meat from the bones, and cut into small pieces. Remove the bay leaf and purée the sauce in the pot.
Stir in the tomatoes, 2 tbsp tomato paste, and the hare pieces. Finely chop 1/2 bunch of parsley and mix in. Continue to simmer the sauce over low heat.

Meanwhile, bring 4 l of salted water to a boil and cook the pappardelle for 8–10 minutes until al dente. Drain, toss with 2 tbsp olive oil. Adjust the seasoning of the sauce, then combine with the pasta. Scatter over parsley leaves and serve.
Breite Bandnudeln mit Hasensauce.tips: If you have a pasta machine, you can of course make the pappardelle yourself: work 400g flour with 4 eggs, 1 tbsp olive oil, and 1/2 tsp salt into a smooth dough, let it rest for 20 minutes, roll out thinly, and cut into 2 cm wide strips.

Weiße und grüne Bandnudeln mit Sahne-Pil.name: White and Green Ribbon Pasta with Creamy Mushroom Sauce
ingredients: 300g fresh mushrooms (button mushrooms, oyster mushrooms, chanterelles, or porcini)
steps: For the green pasta dough, clean and wash the fresh spinach. Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and blanch the spinach for 2 minutes. Drain into a colander, plunge into cold water, and let drain well. Squeeze out firmly and purée in a blender.

For both pasta doughs, mound 400g flour on the work surface and make a well in the center. Add 4 eggs, 1 tbsp olive oil, and 1/2 tsp salt into the well, gradually mix with the flour, and knead into a smooth, elastic dough. Take 1/3 of the dough and knead in the puréed spinach. Cover both portions of dough with a kitchen towel and let rest for 20 minutes.
Roll out both types of dough thinly on a lightly floured surface and let them dry slightly.
For the sauce, cut the cured ham into thin strips. Clean the mushrooms, slice them thinly, and drizzle with 1 tbsp lemon juice.
Melt 1/2 tbsp butter in a wide saucepan, press in 1 garlic clove, and sauté gently. Pour in 200g cream and 150 ml broth, and reduce over higher heat until creamy.
Loosely roll up the pasta sheets and cut into 1/2 cm wide strips with a sharp knife. Place on a floured cloth. Bring 4 l of salted water to a boil in a large pot.

Melt 1 tbsp butter in a frying pan. Add the mushrooms in batches and sauté. Finely chop the parsley, mix with the ham strips into the mushrooms, and cook together over low heat. Season carefully with salt and pepper.
Cook the white and green ribbon pasta for 7–8 minutes until al dente. Drain well and toss loosely together in a warmed serving bowl.

Season the cream sauce with salt, pepper, and lemon juice, and pour over the pasta. Top with the mushroom and ham mixture and scatter over basil leaves. Grind over some pepper, and serve with freshly grated Parmesan.
Weiße und grüne Bandnudeln mit Sahne-Pil.tips: This mixture of yellow and green pasta strips is called "straw and hay".

Reissuppe mit Linsen.name: Rice Soup with Lentils
steps: Cover 100g lentils with water in a pot and soak overnight. Bring to a boil in the soaking water and cook for about 15 minutes.

Cut the bacon into very fine dice. Finely chop 1/2 bunch of parsley and 1 onion. Crush 6 peppercorns and 2 garlic cloves in a mortar. Mix everything with the bacon. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pot, add the bacon mixture, and sauté gently.

Drain the canned tomatoes, roughly chop them, and add to the pot along with the tomato liquid and 1 l beef broth. Bring to a boil, stir in 150g rice, and let it cook through. Season with salt and pepper.
Stir the cooked lentils along with their cooking water into the rice soup. Simmer gently for another 2 minutes and season to taste. Chop the remaining parsley, sprinkle over the soup, and serve.

Eiercremesuppe.name: Egg Cream Soup
ingredients: 2 cl Marsala or Vin Santo (Italian dessert wines)
steps: Place 4 egg yolks in a bowl and gradually whisk in 1/2 l chicken broth and 2 cl Marsala, beating vigorously. Season with 1/4 tsp ground cinnamon and 1 pinch of salt. Strain through a fine sieve into a saucepan (best to continue using a double boiler or bain-marie from here).
Heat the mixture slowly, stirring constantly. Add 50g butter in small pieces and stir in vigorously. Do not allow it to boil, or the egg cream will curdle.
Once the soup is piping hot and nicely creamy, season to taste with 1 pinch of sugar and freshly grated nutmeg. Pour into warmed cups or bowls and serve immediately.
Eiercremesuppe.tips: A quiet star among the specialties of the Chianti region. The evocative name of this soup comes from its delicate broom-yellow color.

Zwiebelsuppe.name: Onion Soup
steps: Roughly chop 2 carrots, 2 celery stalks, and 1 onion. Place in a pot with the pork spare ribs. Add 1 1/2 l water, season with salt, and bring to a boil. Cook for 15 minutes.
Slice the remaining onions thinly and briefly soak in a bowl of cold water. Dice the bacon and garlic sausage.
Heat 5 tbsp oil. Gently fry the bacon and sausage for a few minutes, stirring. Drain the onions very well, add in batches, and sauté until translucent.

Strain the rib broth and add to the pot. Simmer over low heat for about 1 hour. Strip the meat from the bones and cut into small pieces. Stir into the soup and heat through. Season with salt and pepper.
Toast the bread slices, rub with garlic, and serve alongside the onion soup.
Zwiebelsuppe.tips: This could theoretically also serve as the basis for a ribollita — which is really the name for any soup that tastes even better reheated.

Aufgewärmte Gemüsesuppe.name: Reheated Vegetable Soup
ingredients: 1 ham bone (order from the butcher if needed)
steps: Cover the beans with water and soak for 12 hours. Then bring to a boil in the soaking water and cook over low heat for 1 1/2 hours.

Finely dice the bacon, 1 leek, 2 carrots, 2 celery stalks, 1 onion, and 2 garlic cloves. Heat 3 tbsp oil in a large pot, fry the bacon and vegetables. Add 2 l beef broth, 1 thyme sprig, and the ham bone. Cover and cook over the lowest heat for 30 minutes.

Clean the savoy cabbage leaves and cut into rough strips. Add to the pot and cook for another 30 minutes. Purée half of the cooked beans. Stir in the purée along with the whole beans and simmer uncovered for a further 15 minutes.

Remove the bone and season the soup. Layer into a large pot with slices of bread. Refrigerate overnight, then bring to a boil again before serving. Drizzle with fresh olive oil and season with thyme, salt, and pepper.

Seezunge mit Spinat.name: Sole with Spinach
ingredients: 1 tsp fresh thyme leaves or 1/2 tsp dried (for sauce)
steps: Either have the sole prepared and filleted by the fishmonger, or fillet them yourself. To do so, lay the fish dark-side up on the work surface. Cut off the side fins with kitchen scissors.
Make a crosswise cut through the skin at the tail end and pull it off with a sharp tug toward the head. Turn the fish over, score and peel off the lighter skin on the other side in the same way.

Using a sharp knife, cut along both sides of the central bone and along the outer edges. Lift the fillets away from the head and bones to get 8 sole fillets. Season lightly with salt and pepper and drizzle with the juice of 1/2 lemon.
Sort through the spinach, wash thoroughly, and drain. Place in a pot with 2 tbsp white wine, salt, and pepper, and heat. Press in 2 garlic cloves. Cover the pot and let the spinach wilt.

For the sauce, finely chop 1 small onion. Sauté in 1 tbsp butter until translucent. Stir in 1 tsp thyme leaves. Pour in 1/4 l milk, season with salt and pepper. Bring briefly to a boil, then let infuse for about 5 minutes. Preheat the oven to 200°C.

Melt 1 tbsp butter in a saucepan, whisk in 1 tbsp flour, and cook briefly, stirring. Pour in the thyme-infused milk and 200g cream, stirring until creamy and thickened. Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in 2 egg yolks and 3 tbsp freshly grated Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.

Grease an ovenproof dish with 1/2 tbsp butter. Lay in 4 sole fillets, spread the spinach on top, then place the remaining 4 fillets over the spinach. Pour the sauce over everything and dot with the remaining butter. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for 12 minutes. Divide onto warmed plates, sprinkle with freshly grated Parmesan if desired, and serve.
Seezunge mit Spinat.tips: Wine suggestion: Galestro, a white wine from the Chianti area.

Gebackene Meerbarben.name: Baked Red Mullet
steps: Scale, clean, and wash the red mullet; cut off the heads. Slice open lengthwise and carefully remove the backbone. Season the inside of the fish with salt and pepper and drizzle with 1 tsp lemon juice each. Place 2 sage leaves inside each fish, then fold them back together. Lay them side by side in a shallow dish.
Whisk 4 tbsp olive oil with 2 tbsp lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Pour the marinade over the red mullet, cover, and let marinate in the refrigerator for 2 hours. Turn the fish once during this time.
Grease an ovenproof dish with 1 tbsp olive oil and set aside. Preheat the oven to 200°C. Cut the ham into lengthwise strips about 2 cm wide.

Remove the fish from the marinade and let them drain. Coat all over with 2–3 tbsp breadcrumbs. Wrap with the ham strips and place side by side in the dish. Scatter the remaining sage leaves on top and drizzle with the marinade.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for 15 minutes. Scatter parsley leaves over the top. Serve with lemon wedges.

Kräuterforellen in Weißwein.name: Herb Trout in White Wine
steps: Gut the trout and cut off the fins. Rinse thoroughly under running water. Pat dry with kitchen paper, then season inside and out with salt and pepper. Dust lightly all over with 2 tbsp flour.
Very finely chop the parsley, lemon balm, onion, and garlic cloves and mix together. Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a large pan. Sauté the herb mixture over low heat, stirring.

Place the trout in the pan and fry for 2 minutes on each side. Add the ¼ l white wine a tablespoon at a time, allowing it to reduce slightly before adding more. Cook the trout in this way for 15–20 minutes. Arrange on a platter and drizzle with the sauce.
Kräuterforellen in Weißwein.tips: When serving all four fish dishes, provide fresh white bread on the side — perfect for mopping up the delicious sauces. Wine suggestion: a dry white wine, such as Vernaccia di San Gimignano.

Fritierter Kabeljau (Marken/Umbrien).name: Fried Cod (Marche/Umbria)
ingredients: 3 tbsp olive oil, plus oil for frying
steps: Cut the cod fillet into 2 cm cubes. Season with salt and pepper, drizzle with 2 tbsp lemon juice, cover, and let marinate in the refrigerator.
Blanch, peel, and deseed the tomatoes. Chop the flesh into small pieces. Finely chop the celery, parsley, onion, and garlic cloves and mix together.
Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a wide saucepan. Sauté the onion mixture, then stir in the chopped tomatoes. Simmer uncovered over low heat to reduce. Season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp lemon juice.
Lightly dust the cod cubes all over with 1 tbsp flour, then coat in 3 tbsp breadcrumbs. Heat a generous amount of oil in a pan and fry the fish cubes until crispy. Drain on kitchen paper.
Serve with the tomato sauce and lemon wedges.
Fritierter Kabeljau (Marken/Umbrien).tips: This recipe is often made with stockfish (dried cod) — the version with fresh cod is less work and tastes even juicier.

Aal in Tomatensauce (Umbrien).name: Eel in Tomato Sauce (Umbria)
steps: Have the fishmonger skin the eel and cut it into pieces about 5 cm long. Rinse briefly and pat dry with kitchen paper.

Finely chop 1 onion and 3 garlic cloves. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan and sauté the onion and garlic. Drain the canned tomatoes, reserving the juice, and roughly chop the tomatoes. Finely chop the rosemary.
Add the eel pieces to the pan and brown well on all sides. Season with salt, pepper, and rosemary. Pour in 3 tbsp white wine vinegar and let it evaporate.
Stir in the ⅛ l white wine and the tomatoes. Let the eel simmer gently in the uncovered pot for about 30 minutes, gradually adding the reserved tomato juice.
Roughly chop the parsley. Once the eel is cooked through, season the tomato sauce generously with salt and pepper. Scatter the parsley over the top and serve.

Geschmortes Kaninchen mit Fenchel (Marke.name: Braised Rabbit with Fennel (Marche)
ingredients: 1 rabbit, ready to cook (1.2 kg), preferably with the liver
1 tsp fresh rosemary (or ½ tsp dried)
steps: Rinse and wash the rabbit thoroughly, then pat dry. Rub inside and out with salt and pepper. Set the liver aside for the stuffing. Dice the bread roll and soak in ⅛ l white wine.

Trim the fennel, cut off the stalks, and set the tender fronds aside. Bring ¾ l salted water to the boil. Cook the fennel bulbs and stalks for about 15 minutes, then remove both. Strain and reserve the broth. Let the bulbs cool. Cut ½ lemon into slices and peel 2 garlic cloves; add to the boiling water along with 6 peppercorns. Finely chop the fennel bulbs and stalks together with half of the fennel fronds.

Cut the ham and salami into tiny cubes. Chop the rabbit liver into small pieces. Finely chop 2 garlic cloves and 1 tsp rosemary. Gently squeeze out the soaked bread roll. Mix everything thoroughly with the chopped fennel. Season with salt and pepper.

Preheat the oven to 175°C. Fill the rabbit with the prepared stuffing. Sew the opening closed with kitchen twine. Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a roasting pan with a lid and gently brown the rabbit on all sides.
Pour in half of the reserved fennel broth, cover, and place in the oven (gas mark 2). Braise for about 1 hour, turning occasionally and basting with the fennel broth, adding more if needed.

10 minutes before the end of the cooking time, slice the fennel bulbs. Finely dice the bacon and render it in a pan; remove the crispy cracklings. Gently sauté the fennel slices, add a few tablespoons of fennel broth, and season with salt and pepper.

Remove the rabbit from the roasting pan and keep warm in the oven. Pour ¼ l wine and 125g cream into the pan juices and reduce vigorously on the stovetop. Carve the rabbit into slices and arrange with the fennel. Chop the remaining fennel fronds and scatter over the top along with the bacon cracklings.
Geschmortes Kaninchen mit Fenchel (Marke.tips: Wine suggestion: a fresh, light white wine, such as a Verdicchio dei Castelli di Jesi.

Leberspießchen (Toskana).name: Liver Skewers (Tuscany)
steps: Blanch the caul fat for 2 minutes, then place in a bowl of cold water. Carefully spread out on a cloth and pat dry with kitchen paper. Cut into squares of about 6 cm.

Cut the pork liver into small, flat square pieces (as many as there are pieces of caul fat). Finely grind 1 tbsp fennel seeds, 8 crumbled sage leaves, 1 garlic clove, salt, and 6 peppercorns in a mortar. Mix in 4 tbsp breadcrumbs.

Sprinkle the liver pieces with the spice mixture and wrap each one individually in caul fat. Spread the 8 small slices of white bread with lard and halve them. Thread the liver parcels onto skewers, alternating with bread and bay leaves. Season with salt and pepper.
Lightly oil a grill pan and cook the liver skewers over medium heat for 10 minutes, turning to brown on all sides.

Schweinebraten mit Kräutern (Toskana).name: Herb-Crusted Roast Pork (Tuscany)
steps: Very finely chop 1 tsp fresh rosemary needles and 3 garlic cloves. Place both in a small bowl. Grate the zest of 1 lemon and add to the bowl along with 1 tsp fennel seeds, 1 pinch ground cloves, salt, and pepper. Mix everything thoroughly.

Pierce the meat all over in several places about 1 cm deep with a sharp knife. Fill each small hole with a knife-tip's worth of the spice mixture. Season the roast with salt and pepper, distribute the remaining rosemary sprigs over it, and tie it all together firmly like a rolled roast.
Place the pork roast on the oven rack (or skewer it on a rotisserie for grilling). Slide a drip pan underneath the rack to catch the juices as the meat cooks.
Roast in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 2 hours, turning frequently and basting with the dripping juices. (On a charcoal rotisserie grill, it also takes about 2 hours.)

Kutteltopf mit Gemüse (Toskana).name: Tripe with Vegetables (Tuscany)
ingredients: 1 kg ripe tomatoes (or 800g canned peeled tomatoes)
1 fresh rosemary sprig (or ½ tsp dried)
¼ l dry white wine (or beef stock)
steps: Rinse the pre-cooked tripe thoroughly under running water and pat well dry. Cut into very fine strips using a sharp knife.
Very finely dice 2 carrots, 4 celery stalks, and 4 spring onions. Finely chop ½ bunch parsley, the rosemary, and 3 garlic cloves.
Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a pot and melt 2 tbsp butter in it. Sauté the vegetables. Stir in the parsley, rosemary, and garlic, and season with salt and pepper.

Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan. Add the tripe in batches and sear over high heat. Transfer to the pot with the vegetables, add a little more oil to the pan, sear the next batch, and add that to the pot as well. Pour in ¼ l wine, season with salt and pepper, and let simmer gently.

Blanch, peel, and deseed the tomatoes. Chop the flesh and stir into the tripe along with 2 tbsp tomato paste and ¼ tsp cayenne pepper. Half-cover the pot and braise for about 1 hour. Shortly before the end of cooking, preheat the oven to 225°C.

Crush ½ tsp peppercorns in a mortar and chop the remaining parsley. Adjust the seasoning of the tripe, then transfer to an ovenproof earthenware dish and sprinkle with pepper, parsley, and 50g Parmesan. Briefly gratinate in the preheated oven (gas mark 4). Serve very hot with plenty of white bread.
Kutteltopf mit Gemüse (Toskana).tips: A delicious accompaniment: braise chopped vegetables and potatoes in the drip pan alongside the roast. Wine suggestion: a not-too-heavy red wine, such as a young Chianti Classico or a Vino Nobile di Montepulciano.

Hasenpfeffer.name: Braised Rabbit Hunter's Style
ingredients: 1 young rabbit (approx. 1.2 kg), oven-ready, cut into portions
¼ l meat stock (preferably ready-made game stock)
steps: Thoroughly wash and dry the rabbit pieces, carefully removing any sinew.

Roughly chop 1 carrot and 2 stalks of celery, halve 2 garlic cloves. Tie 1 sprig of rosemary together with 2 bay leaves. Place in a bowl with the meat and pour over ½ l of red wine. Cover and marinate in a cool place for 24 hours.
Remove the meat, pat dry, and dust with 2 tbsp flour. Strain the marinade and set aside the wine and vegetables separately. Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a casserole dish.

Brown the rabbit pieces well on all sides, season with salt and pepper, then reduce the heat. Add the vegetables, garlic, and herbs and sauté briefly. Pour in the red wine and ¼ l of stock. Cover and braise the meat over the lowest heat for about 2½ hours until tender.
Remove the rabbit pieces and the herb bouquet. Blend the sauce until smooth and adjust seasoning. Serve the meat in the sauce.

Wachteln mit Reis.name: Quail with Rice
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh thyme (or ½ tbsp dried)
steps: Briefly rinse and dry the quails, remove the giblets. Carefully remove any remaining pin feathers with tweezers. Season the quails lightly with salt and pepper inside and out.
Fry the bacon slices until crispy in a casserole dish. Remove and keep warm in a small pan at the edge of the stove. Melt 1 tbsp butter in the casserole dish.
Slowly brown the quails on all sides. Add the thyme leaves and 2 bay leaves, pour in ⅛ l of white wine and ¼ l of stock. Cover and braise over low heat for about 1 hour.

Bring 1 l of salted water to a boil in a pot, add 300 g of rice and cook until tender. Drain and let the rice dry, then stir in 2 tbsp butter and 4 tbsp Parmesan. Arrange on a warmed serving platter with the quails and bacon slices. Adjust the seasoning of the sauce and serve alongside.

Huhn mit Kapern und Oliven.name: Chicken with Capers and Olives
ingredients: 1 tsp fresh rosemary (or ½ tsp dried)
⅛ l dry white wine (or chicken stock)
steps: Cut the chicken into 6 pieces. Using a mortar, crush 1 tsp rosemary, 2 garlic cloves, and 6 peppercorns. Mix in 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and the finely grated zest of 1 lemon. Rub the chicken pieces all over with the mixture, cover, and leave to marinate for 30 minutes.
Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a large frying pan. Fry the chicken pieces until crispy and golden all over.
Stir together 3 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp tomato paste, and ⅛ l of white wine, and pour over the chicken. Braise for 15–20 minutes.

Rinse and dry 2 anchovy fillets, then finely chop them. Halve the olives. Stir into the sauce together with 2 tbsp capers and cook for a further 5 minutes. Finely chop the parsley. Season the sauce with salt and pepper and scatter the parsley over the top.
Huhn mit Kapern und Oliven.tips: Wine suggestion: a fresh white wine, such as Orvieto.

Mariniertes Teufelshähnchen.name: Marinated Devil's Chicken
steps: Cut the chicken open along the breast side with poultry shears, press it flat, and place on a sheet of aluminum foil.

Whisk together the juice of 1 lemon, 100 ml olive oil, salt, and pepper. Deseed 3–4 chili peppers and chop them very finely (wash your hands immediately afterwards!). Cut 10 sage leaves into thin strips and stir into the marinade along with the chili.
Pour the marinade over both sides of the chicken and rub it in well. Wrap in the foil and leave to marinate for 2 hours. Then preheat the oven to 200°C.

Place the chicken in the foil on a baking tray. Open the foil at the top and fold it back. Roast the chicken in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 1½ hours until crispy. Garnish with lemon wedges.
Mariniertes Teufelshähnchen.tips: For those who prefer less heat: simply use less chili.

Gebratener Fasan mit Salbei.name: Roast Pheasant with Sage
ingredients: 1 young pheasant (approx. 900 g), preferably with the liver
3 large, thin slices of fatty bacon
1 tbsp fresh rosemary (or ½ tbsp dried)
⅛ l game stock (from a jar) or chicken stock
steps: Soak 10 g of dried mushrooms in 2 cl of brandy. Rinse the pheasant inside and out under cold running water, pat dry thoroughly. Remove the liver and set aside.

In a mortar, finely crush 2 garlic cloves with ½ tsp juniper berries and a pinch of salt. Cut the cured ham into small cubes and chop finely; reserve the brandy soaking liquid. Finely chop 1 tbsp of rosemary needles. Mix together the mushrooms, chopped ham, crushed juniper berries and garlic, rosemary, and sage leaves.
Rub the inside of the pheasant with pepper and fill with the prepared stuffing. Sew shut with kitchen twine. Lightly season the outside with salt and pepper. Preheat the oven to 220°C.
Cover the breast of the pheasant with the bacon slices and secure with kitchen twine.

Heat 3 tbsp oil in a roasting pan. Add the pheasant and brown well on all sides. Pour over the reserved mushroom soaking liquid and let it evaporate. Then transfer the roasting pan to the preheated oven (gas mark 4). Roast the pheasant for 20 minutes, turning once halfway through.

Remove the roasting pan from the oven and take off the bacon slices. Place the pheasant breast-side up back in the pan and pour in ⅛ l of game stock. Roast for a further 20 minutes, basting frequently with the cooking juices.

Remove the pheasant, cover, and keep warm. Place the roasting pan on the stovetop, pour in the cream, and reduce briefly. Cut the liver into small pieces, stir into the sauce, and cook briefly. Blend the sauce until smooth with a hand blender. Season with salt and pepper.
Gebratener Fasan mit Salbei.tips: Wine suggestion: a full-bodied red wine, such as Brunello di Montalcino.

Zucchini-Omelett.name: Zucchini Frittata
ingredients: ½ tsp fresh rosemary (or ¼ tsp dried)
steps: Wash the zucchini and trim the ends. Cut the zucchini into thin rounds, then into fine matchsticks. Very finely chop 1 onion and 2 garlic cloves.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan. Add the zucchini matchsticks along with the onion, garlic, and ½ tsp rosemary, and sauté gently for 5 minutes, stirring. Season with salt and pepper. Finely chop the chives.

Whisk 5 eggs with 5 tbsp milk, salt, and pepper. Stir in half of the chives. Pour the egg mixture over the zucchini and spread evenly. Cook over gentle heat until just set. Flip the frittata and cook the other side as well. Scatter over the remaining chives and serve lukewarm.
Zucchini-Omelett.tips: The easiest way to flip it: slide the frittata out of the pan onto a large plate or pan lid, then confidently flip it back into the pan with the uncooked side facing down.

Käse-Omelett.name: Cheese Frittata
steps: For the sauce, finely chop 1 onion and 1 garlic clove. Drain the canned tomatoes, reserving the juice, and roughly chop the tomatoes.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a small saucepan. Sauté the onion and garlic, then stir in the tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper and simmer over low heat, gradually adding the reserved tomato juice.
For the frittata, whisk 5 eggs with 5 tbsp cream. Stir in 25 g of freshly grated Parmesan and season with salt and pepper.

Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan and melt 1 tbsp butter in it. Add 10 sage leaves and fry gently. Pour the egg mixture into the pan and spread evenly. Cook over gentle heat until just set. Flip and cook the other side until golden brown as well.
Cut the frittata into strips and serve with the tomato sauce. Scatter over the remaining Parmesan.

Pilz-Omelett.name: Mushroom Frittata
steps: Clean the mushrooms and slice them thinly. Very finely chop the parsley.
Whisk 5 eggs with salt and pepper. Stir in ¾ of the mushrooms and ¾ of the parsley.
Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan and melt 1 tbsp butter in it. Pour in the egg mixture and cook over gentle heat until just set.
Season with salt and pepper. Arrange the remaining mushrooms in the center of the frittata, fold it in half, and scatter over the remaining parsley.
Pilz-Omelett.tips: You can apply this technique to other frittata variations too — instead of flipping in the pan, simply fold the frittata in half and serve with a soft, creamy center. In late autumn in Umbria, a true delicacy: frittata topped with paper-thin shaved truffles.

Speck-Omelett.name: Bacon Omelette
steps: Cut the bacon into thin strips. Add to a pan with 1 tbsp olive oil and cook, stirring, until the fat has rendered and the bacon pieces are crispy.
Beat 5 eggs with a little salt (go easy, as the bacon can be quite salty) and pepper. Pour into the pan and spread evenly.
Cook over gentle heat until set, then flip and cook until golden brown on both sides. Serve scattered with fresh basil leaves.
Speck-Omelett.tips: Frittata is usually cut into strips and served warm — also great as a bite-sized appetizer before a meal.

Weiße Bohnen mit Salbei.name: White Beans with Sage
steps: Soak the beans overnight. The next day, top up the soaking water to 2 litres, add 1/2 tsp salt, and simmer the beans covered on the lowest heat for at least 1.5 hours.
After 1 hour, blanch the tomatoes, peel and deseed them. Chop the flesh into small pieces. Cut 10 sage leaves into fine strips and mince 2 garlic cloves.
Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a large pot. Gently sauté the garlic and half the sage strips.
Drain the beans and add them to the pot along with the tomatoes. Season generously with salt and pepper. Cook covered for about 20 minutes.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a pan and briefly sauté the remaining sage. Taste the beans for seasoning, then scatter over the sage.
Weiße Bohnen mit Salbei.tips: Tuscany is famous for its beans — and its bean recipes are hard to beat. One charming tradition involves using a round-bellied wine bottle as a cooking vessel.

Überbackener Spinat.name: Baked Spinach Gratin
steps: Clean and wash the spinach. Place the wet leaves directly into a large pot and wilt over high heat. Drain thoroughly in a colander.

Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a pan. Add the well-drained spinach and press in 1 garlic clove. Season with salt and pepper, stir in 1 tbsp butter and let it melt over gentle heat. Remove from the heat. Preheat the oven to 200°C.

For the sauce, melt 1 tbsp butter in a saucepan, stir in 1 tbsp flour and cook briefly. Gradually add 250ml milk, stirring constantly until the sauce is smooth and creamy. Remove from the heat, stir in 25g grated Parmesan, and season with a pinch of freshly grated nutmeg, salt, pepper, and 1–2 tbsp lemon juice.

Transfer the spinach to an ovenproof dish, spread the sauce over the top, and sprinkle with 25g freshly grated Parmesan. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 5 minutes until golden and bubbling.

Gebratene Knoblauchpilze.name: Sautéed Garlic Mushrooms
ingredients: 500g fresh mixed mushrooms (button mushrooms, chestnut mushrooms, oyster mushrooms, chanterelles)
1 sprig fresh mint (or flat-leaf parsley as an alternative)
steps: Clean the mushrooms thoroughly and slice very thinly. Drizzle with 1 tbsp lemon juice. Slice 3–4 garlic cloves thinly.
Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a pan. Add the mushrooms in batches, sauté briefly, then remove and set aside. Preheat the oven to 200°C.

Melt 1 tsp butter in the pan. Stir in the garlic and sauté gently. Add the remaining lemon juice, stir in 2 tbsp tomato paste, and season with salt and pepper. Return all the mushrooms to the pan, mix thoroughly, and adjust seasoning.

Transfer the mushrooms to a decorative ovenproof terracotta dish and bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for 10 minutes. Scatter with mint leaves and bring to the table sizzling hot in the clay dish.
Gebratene Knoblauchpilze.tips: To intensify the flavour, sauté 1/2 bunch of finely chopped flat-leaf parsley together with the garlic slices.

Überbackener Fenchel.name: Baked Fennel Gratin
steps: Halve the fennel bulbs lengthways and remove the outer layers and tough stalks. Reserve the fennel fronds.
Bring 1 litre of salted water to the boil with the juice of 1/2 lemon. Cook the fennel halves for 20 minutes. Drain and reserve the cooking liquid.

Preheat the oven to 200°C. Grease an ovenproof dish with 2 tbsp olive oil. Drain the canned tomatoes, reserving the juice for another use. Roughly chop the tomatoes and place them in the dish. Nestle the fennel halves on top. Drizzle with about 125ml of the fennel cooking liquid. Season everything generously with salt and pepper.

Very finely chop the parsley, fennel fronds, onion, and garlic. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a pan and gently sauté the onion and garlic. Stir in 4 tbsp breadcrumbs and toast until golden. Remove from the heat. Mix in the herbs and 3 tbsp Parmesan, then spread over the fennel. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for 20 minutes.

Fritierte Blumenkohlröschen.name: Fried Cauliflower Florets
steps: In a bowl, mix 100g flour with salt and pepper and make a well in the centre. Add 1 egg, 125ml white wine, and 2 cl anise liqueur, then gradually whisk into a smooth batter. Beat vigorously, then cover and leave to rest for 30 minutes.
Clean and wash the cauliflower and break it into florets. Bring 2 litres of salted water to the boil in a large pot. Add the florets and blanch for 2 minutes. Refresh in cold water and drain well.

Heat plenty of frying oil in a large pot. Dip the cauliflower florets into the batter using a fork and fry in the hot oil until crispy and golden. Drain on kitchen paper, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and serve immediately.
Fritierte Blumenkohlröschen.tips: The crispy florets look great arranged on fresh, green salad leaves. This recipe works equally well with other vegetables — blanch firmer varieties briefly beforehand.

Gefüllte Zucchini.name: Stuffed Zucchini
ingredients: 1/2 bread roll from the day before
steps: Wash the zucchini and trim off the blossom ends and stems. Parboil in boiling salted water for 5 minutes. Refresh in cold water and leave to cool.
Remove the crust from 1/2 bread roll, cut the crumb into small cubes and soak in just enough cold water to cover. Blanch the tomatoes, peel and deseed them. Finely chop the flesh and the onion.

Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot large enough to hold 16 zucchini halves. Sauté the onion, then stir in the tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper and press in 1 garlic clove. Let the sauce simmer and reduce.

Halve the zucchini lengthways, scoop out some of the flesh and finely chop it. Heat 1 tbsp butter in a pan. Fry the ground beef, stirring, until crumbly. Transfer to a bowl and leave to cool slightly.

Gemüserohkost.name: Raw Vegetable Platter
ingredients: a few leaves of romaine lettuce
steps: Remove the tough outer leaves from the fennel bulbs and trim the stalks generously. Quarter or cut the fennel hearts into eighths lengthways.
Halve the bell peppers, remove the white ribs and seeds. Wash the halves and cut lengthways into 1 cm wide strips.
Wash the zucchini and trim the ends. Cut into 1/2 cm thick rounds or batons. Wash the beef tomatoes, cut into thin wedges and remove the blossom ends.
Separate the radicchio and romaine lettuce into leaves and wash. Drain both well or spin dry. Trim and wash 4 spring onions and halve the stalks.

Arrange the vegetables and salad leaves decoratively on a large platter. For the dressing, whisk 6 tbsp wine vinegar vigorously with salt and pepper, then beat in 12 tbsp olive oil. Pour into small bowls and place around the table. Each guest picks their favourite vegetables, dips them briefly into the dressing, and enjoys them with crusty white bread.
Gemüserohkost.tips: Works as a starter, a light course between dishes, or a small summer meal. Any leftover dressing can be stored in a sealed jar in the fridge and used for the next salad.

Eisgekühlte Kuppeltorte.name: Chilled Dome Cake (Zuccotto)
steps: Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper. Preheat the oven to 175°C. For the sponge, beat 5 egg yolks, 120g powdered sugar, and 1 tbsp vanilla sugar with a whisk until pale and fluffy. Beat 5 egg whites to stiff peaks and gently fold in. Sift in 50g cornstarch and 50g flour and fold into the egg mixture. Spread the batter about 1 cm thick onto the baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for 15 minutes. Turn out onto a board, peel off the paper, and let the sponge cool completely.

Line a deep, half-round bowl with buttered parchment paper or plastic wrap. Cut a small circle from the sponge (the size of the bowl's base, about 10 cm) and place it in the bottom of the bowl. Cut the remaining sponge into strips and use them to line the sides of the bowl all the way to the top. Mix 2 cl brandy with 2 cl Amaretto and brush over the sponge with a pastry brush.

Filling: Coarsely grate half of the chocolate; melt the rest with 100 ml cream in a double boiler. Coarsely chop the almonds and hazelnuts and lightly toast them. Beat the meringue with 50g powdered sugar to stiff peaks. Fold in the grated chocolate, almonds, and hazelnuts.

Divide the cream into 2 portions. Stir the crumbled meringue into one half; mix the second half with the cooled melted chocolate. Spoon the meringue cream into the mold, spreading it evenly over the sponge all the way up the sides. Refrigerate for 20 minutes. Fill with the chocolate cream and smooth the surface. Cover the top with the remaining sponge strips. Refrigerate for at least 6 hours, or overnight.
To make the garnish stencils: cut a circle (20–25 cm) from paper. Mark the center, then cut out pointed triangles radiating inward from the outside edge.

Melt the chocolate glaze. Unmold the well-chilled cake and peel off the parchment paper. Coat with the glaze and let it set. Place the stencil on top. Dust the exposed areas with powdered sugar to create a star-burst pattern. Cut into portions and serve immediately.
Eisgekühlte Kuppeltorte.tips: Instead of Amaretto, a mixture of Maraschino, rum, and orange liqueur is also popular for soaking the sponge. Small cubes of candied fruit can also be added to the cream filling.

Reiskrapfen.name: Rice Fritters
ingredients: 2 cl Vin Santo (Italian dessert wine) or rum
steps: Soak 50g raisins in 2 cl wine or rum. Bring 3/4 l milk to a boil with a pinch of salt, sprinkle in 250g rice, and cook over low heat, stirring frequently, until the rice has absorbed the liquid.

Remove the rice from the heat and let it cool. Wash and dry 1 orange and 1 lemon, then finely grate the zest. Stir the zest into the rice along with 2 egg yolks, 2 tbsp butter, 50g sugar, and the raisins with their soaking liquid.
Heat plenty of oil in a pot. Beat 2 egg whites to stiff peaks, mix with 20g sugar and 1 tbsp flour, and gently fold into the rice mixture.

Using a tablespoon, scoop the mixture into portions and fry in the hot oil until golden brown on all sides, turning frequently with a fork. Drain on paper towels and keep warm until all the fritters are done. Pile onto a large platter, dust with powdered sugar, and serve hot.

Gewürzkuchen.name: Spiced Fruit and Nut Cake (Panforte di Siena)
ingredients: edible wafer paper for lining the pan
powdered sugar and cinnamon for dusting
steps: Toast the almonds, hazelnuts, and walnuts in a dry pan, stirring, for a few minutes. Let cool, then coarsely chop and transfer to a bowl.
Cut the dried figs and candied fruit into small cubes and mix with the nuts. Stir in 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon, and a pinch each of ground cloves, coriander, ginger, and freshly grated nutmeg.

Combine 150g powdered sugar and 100g honey in a heatproof bowl and set over a double boiler. Stir constantly over low heat until the mixture melts and begins to form threads. Remove from the heat and let cool slightly, stirring.

Preheat the oven to 150°C. Stir the honey mixture into the prepared nut mixture, then mix in 1–2 tbsp flour. Grease a shallow pan (square or round, depending on your wafer paper) with butter and line with wafer paper. Spread the dough evenly to a depth of 2 cm.

Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 1) for about 30 minutes. Let cool, then dust lightly with a mixture of powdered sugar and cinnamon. Cut into small diamond shapes or strips if desired, and store in an airtight container.
Gewürzkuchen.tips: No visitor leaves Siena without tucking a sweet treat into their bag for the journey home.

Mandelschnitten.name: Almond Biscotti
steps: Toast the slivered almonds in a dry pan, stirring, for a few minutes. Set aside.

Preheat the oven to 175°C and grease a baking sheet with butter. In a large bowl, thoroughly beat 3 egg yolks with 250g sugar and 3 tbsp vanilla sugar. Beat 3 egg whites with a pinch of salt to stiff peaks, then fold into the yolk mixture in batches.

Mix in the finely grated zest of 1 lemon and the toasted slivered almonds. Combine 400g flour with 1/2 packet baking powder and sift. Knead everything together into a smooth dough, adding more flour if needed.

Shape into rolls about 2 cm thick and place on the greased baking sheet. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 35 minutes, until the surface is lightly browned. Remove from the oven and immediately cut into 1 cm wide diagonal slices with a sharp knife. Let cool and store in an airtight container.
Mandelschnitten.tips: Traditionally meant to be dipped in the delicious dessert wine Vin Santo before nibbling. If you don't have any on hand, try them with an espresso!

Knusprige Brotfladen.name: Crispy Flatbread (Pane Carasau)
steps: Mix 250g flour with 250g semolina and place in a bowl. Crumble 20g yeast into a cup and stir together with 1 pinch of sugar and 2 tbsp lukewarm water until smooth. Add the yeast mixture to the center of the flour blend and stir in a little flour. Cover with a cloth and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Then knead with 1/4 l lukewarm water and 1 tsp salt into a smooth dough. Return to the bowl, cover again, and let rise for another 1 hour.

Preheat the oven to 250°C. Grease 2 flat, round baking pans (25–30 cm diameter). Knead the dough vigorously once more. Divide into 8 small round portions and roll out on a lightly floured surface as thin as possible — just a millimeter thick — into circles the size of the pans.
Place the rolled-out flatbreads on the greased pans. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 5), one at a time, for about 5 minutes, until bubbles form but the dough has not yet taken on any color.
Remove the freshly baked flatbreads, immediately stack them flat on top of each other. Cover with a flat plate or board and weigh down with something heavy. Let the stacked flatbreads cool slightly.

Place each flatbread back in the oven and bake for 8–10 minutes until crispy. Let cool and store wrapped in paper. To serve (as a side), drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, coarsely ground pepper, and rosemary.
Knusprige Brotfladen.tips: Carta da musica — Sardinians take this flatbread out to the fields with them. Today it is served in restaurants as a snack. For Pane Frattau: place 4 flatbreads on large plates, drizzle with 1/4 l hot broth, heat and season 500g tomato purée, melt 2 tbsp butter, fry 4 fried eggs, spread the flatbreads with the tomato purée, top each with a fried egg, season with salt and pepper, sprinkle with 50g grated cheese, and garnish with parsley and basil leaves.

Pikantes Brot aus der Pfanne.name: Savory Pan-Fried Bread (Pandorato)
steps: Remove the crusts from the white loaf. Make cuts at 1 cm intervals, cutting the first slice almost all the way through but leaving it attached at the bottom, then cutting the second slice all the way through. This yields 6 connected double-slice sandwiches.

Cut the mozzarella into 6 slices. Place a slice of cheese inside each bread pocket. Fill half of the bread pockets with 1–2 slices of ham as well. Rinse the 6 anchovy fillets and pat dry with paper towels. Distribute them among the bread pockets without ham.

Press the filled bread pockets firmly together and dip them in 1/8 l milk. Dust all over with 2 tbsp flour and place on a deep plate. In a bowl, beat 2 eggs vigorously, season with salt and pepper. Pour the egg mixture over the bread and let soak for 15 minutes. Turn the bread over once.

Heat plenty of oil in a frying pan. Fry the bread pockets over medium heat until golden brown on both sides. Drain well on paper towels. Cut diagonally in half, arrange two different-filled halves together on each plate, and garnish with basil leaves.

Kartoffelpizza mit Schafkäse.name: Potato Flatbread with Sheep's Cheese (Focaccia Sarda)
steps: Wash 300g potatoes and cook in salted water until tender. Peel while still hot and press through a potato ricer onto a pastry board. Mix in 250g flour and 1/8 l milk, then press a well into the center.

Dissolve 20g yeast with 1 pinch of sugar in 2 tbsp lukewarm milk and stir until smooth. Pour into the well in the dough and dust with a little flour. Cover with a cloth and let rise in a warm place for 30 minutes. Then knead with 1/2 tsp salt (and additional flour and milk if needed) into a smooth dough, and let rise for another 30 minutes.

Meanwhile, briefly blanch the tomatoes, then peel and deseed them and finely chop the flesh. Finely chop 1 onion and 2 garlic cloves. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan, sauté the onion and garlic, stir in the tomatoes, and season with salt and pepper. Simmer over low heat until a thick sauce forms.

Preheat the oven to 200°C. Brush a baking sheet with 2 tbsp oil and dust lightly with flour. Roll out the dough and place on the baking sheet. Spread with the tomato sauce and top with crumbled sheep's cheese. Bake in the preheated oven until done.

Gefüllte Reiskroketten.name: Stuffed Rice Croquettes (Supplì alla Romana)
ingredients: oil or clarified butter for frying
steps: For the filling: soak 20g dried mushrooms in 1/8 l water for 2 hours.

In a pot, combine 1/2 l beef broth with 400g crushed tomatoes and 3 tbsp butter and bring to a boil. Stir in 400g rice and cook over low heat, stirring frequently to prevent sticking, until the rice has absorbed all the liquid. Transfer the rice to a bowl and gradually mix in 2 eggs and 50g grated Parmesan. Let cool.

For the filling: finely dice 1 onion and the ham. Melt 1 tbsp butter in a frying pan. Sauté the onion, ham, and ground beef, stirring. Drain the mushrooms, reserving the soaking liquid; finely chop the mushrooms and add to the pan. Stir 2 tbsp tomato paste into the mushroom soaking liquid until smooth and mix in. Simmer over low heat until the sauce thickens.
Cut the mozzarella or Provatura into very small cubes. Season the finished sauce with salt and pepper.

Using a spoon, scoop egg-sized portions of the rice mixture and flatten in your hand. Place 1 tsp of sauce and a few cheese cubes in the center, then shape the rice around the filling into a ball. Roll in breadcrumbs and place on a plate. Form about 30 rice balls this way.

Heat plenty of oil or clarified butter in a pot. Fry the rice balls in batches until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and serve immediately. Optionally, serve with any leftover meat sauce or a spicy tomato sauce on the side.
Gefüllte Reiskroketten.tips: An appetizer for 10–12 people, or a main course for 6 when served with a large salad.

Spaghetti mit Speck und Eiern.name: Spaghetti with Bacon and Eggs
steps: Cut the bacon into small cubes. Fry slowly in a large pan with 2 tbsp oil over low heat.
Bring 4 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Add 400g spaghetti and cook for 8–10 minutes until al dente.
Vigorously whisk 3 eggs with 3 tbsp cream, salt, and pepper until light and creamy. Stir in 50g grated cheese.
Remove the crispy bacon cubes from the pan and keep warm. Finely chop 2 garlic cloves and gently sauté in the bacon fat.

Drain the spaghetti, shake off excess water, and immediately add to the pan. Toss thoroughly in the bacon fat, then remove the pan from the heat. Pour in the egg mixture and quickly combine with the pasta, taking care that the eggs do not begin to scramble. The sauce should remain beautifully creamy.
Scatter the crispy bacon cubes on top, season generously with freshly ground pepper. Serve the remaining 50g grated cheese on the side.

Nudeln mit scharfer Specksauce.name: Pasta with Spicy Bacon Sauce
ingredients: 150g smoked pork cheek (or smoked streaky bacon)
1/2 peperoncino (or chilli), or cayenne pepper as a substitute
steps: Cut the pork cheek or bacon into 1 cm cubes. Fry all over in a pan with 2 tbsp oil over low heat. Chop 1 onion, stir in, and sauté until translucent.
Meanwhile, bring 4 litres of salted water to a boil in a pot. Add 400g bucatini and cook for about 10 minutes until al dente.
Blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed, then dice finely. Stir into the pan and heat through. Season boldly with salt and finely chopped peperoncino (or cayenne pepper).

Drain the bucatini and shake off excess water. Add to the pan and toss thoroughly with the sauce. Fold in half of the grated cheese, then season again firmly with salt and pepper. Transfer the pasta to a warmed bowl. Serve the remaining cheese on the side.
Nudeln mit scharfer Specksauce.tips: The town of Amatrice, located in the border region between Lazio and Abruzzo, gave this fiery, hearty dish its name.

Bandnudeln mit Hühnerlebersauce.name: Ribbon Pasta with Chicken Liver Sauce
ingredients: 400g green fettuccine (8 nests of narrow green ribbon pasta)
steps: First, soak 20g dried mushrooms in water. Then prepare the base sauce: dice the bacon finely and render in a wide pot with 2 tbsp olive oil. Add the chicken giblets to the hot bacon fat and brown all over.

Finely dice 1 carrot and 1 onion, add to the pot and briefly sauté. Press 1 garlic clove through a garlic press into the pot and season with salt and pepper. Add 2 cloves and 1 bay leaf, then pour in 250ml wine. Simmer for at least 30 minutes. Pass through a fine sieve, return the sauce to the pot, and stir in 2 tbsp tomato paste.
Trim, wash, and finely chop 2 spring onions. Briefly blanch 2 tomatoes, peel, and deseed. Cut the flesh into small cubes. Cut 50g cured ham into thin strips.

Melt 1 tbsp butter in a pan. Gently fry the spring onions and ham over low heat. Drain the mushrooms, chop them, and add to the pan along with their soaking liquid. Pour in the prepared base sauce and simmer vigorously for 10 minutes. Stir in the diced tomatoes and season with salt and pepper.
Meanwhile, bring 4 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Add the 8 fettuccine nests and cook for just under 10 minutes until al dente.
Cut the chicken livers into small cubes. Melt 1 tbsp butter in a pan and fry the liver pieces, stirring, for 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper, then stir into the tomato sauce.
Drain the fettuccine and shake off excess water. Divide among warmed plates, spoon the sauce over the top, and serve with grated cheese.
Bandnudeln mit Hühnerlebersauce.tips: You can of course make the fettuccine fresh: prepare a dough following the recipe on page 139. Cut into long, narrow ribbon strips. Allow to dry briefly.

Nudeln mit Chili-Sauce.name: Pasta with Chilli Sauce
steps: Cut the bacon into thin strips. Blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed; finely chop the flesh and pass through a sieve. Chop 1 onion and slice 2 garlic cloves.
Bring 4 litres of salted water to a boil in a pot. Partially cook 400g penne for 5 minutes.

Meanwhile, melt 2 tbsp butter in a large pan. Add the bacon and onion and fry gently over low heat, stirring. Stir in the garlic, sieved tomatoes, and 2 chillies, season with salt and pepper, and cook over low heat.

Drain the pasta, shake off excess water, and stir into the sauce. Reserve a few tablespoons of the hot pasta water and use to loosen the sauce if needed. Continue to simmer gently until the pasta is cooked al dente.
Finely chop the parsley, remove the chillies, and adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve with 50g freshly grated cheese.

Nudeltaschen mit Schafkäse.name: Pasta Parcels with Sheep's Cheese
ingredients: 200g fresh, soft sheep's cheese (or 100g aged sheep's cheese + 100g cream cheese)
steps: Knead 200g flour, 200g durum wheat semolina, 3 eggs, and 1 pinch of salt into a smooth pasta dough (adding a little water if needed). Shape into a ball, cover with a damp cloth, and rest for 30 minutes.

For the filling, clean and wash the spinach. Place it wet into a hot pot and allow it to wilt. Let cool slightly, then squeeze out firmly and chop finely. Briefly sauté with 2 tbsp butter in a pan and season with salt, pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg.
Crumble the fresh sheep's cheese finely (or blend aged sheep's cheese with 100g cream cheese until smooth), then mix thoroughly with the spinach in a bowl. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Roll the dough out thinly, shape the pasta parcels, and fill them with the filling.
Blanch the tomatoes, peel, deseed, and dice finely. Heat in a pan with 2 tbsp olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Cook the culingionis in salted water until al dente, then drain. Serve with the tomato sauce and grated Pecorino.

Safrannocken.name: Saffron Dumplings
ingredients: 100g all-purpose flour, plus extra for rolling and dusting
steps: Dissolve the saffron threads in 125ml lukewarm water. Mix 300g durum wheat semolina with 100g flour. Knead in 1 pinch of salt, the saffron water, and enough additional water (about 3–4 tbsp) to form a smooth pasta dough. Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 30 minutes.

Take small portions of the dough and roll on a floured surface into ropes about 1/2 cm thick. Cut into 1 cm pieces and toss lightly in flour. Press each piece flat with your thumb and press against a coarse-mesh sieve. Curl the long sides inward until they just meet. Place the dumplings on a floured cloth, cover, and leave to dry for at least 24 hours.
For the sauce, blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed, then dice finely. Slice the garlic sausage and 3 garlic cloves, and roughly dice 1 onion.
Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a pot. Gently fry the onion, garlic sausage, and garlic. Stir in the diced tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer over low heat.
