Meanwhile, bring 4 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Add the saffron dumplings and cook for 13–15 minutes until al dente. Drain and shake off excess water. Divide among warmed plates and spoon the sauce over the top. Scatter 50g grated cheese on top, add a grinding of pepper, and garnish with fresh basil.
Safrannocken.tips: A similar recipe, ravioli with ricotta filling, is made in Lazio.

Broccolisuppe.name: Broccoli Soup
steps: Trim the broccoli, cut into florets, wash, and drain. Slice the stalks. Cut the bacon into thin strips. Briefly blanch the tomato, peel, and deseed; roughly chop the flesh. Thinly slice 2 garlic cloves.

Bring 1.5 litres of beef stock to a boil in a pot. In a larger pot, gently heat the bacon strips with 1 tbsp lard and fry. Briefly sauté the garlic. Stir in the broccoli florets and stalks and sauté for 2 minutes. Add the tomato and season with salt and pepper.

Pour in the boiling stock and bring to a rapid boil. Break 150g spaghetti into 3 cm pieces, scatter into the soup, and cook together. Once the pasta and broccoli are al dente, season the soup assertively with salt, pepper, and 1–2 tsp lemon juice. Finely chop the parsley and sprinkle in. Serve with 50g freshly grated Parmesan.

Eierflöckchensuppe.name: Egg Drop Soup
ingredients: 1 litre rich beef stock (preferably homemade, well chilled)
steps: In a bowl, beat 4 eggs with a whisk until very frothy. Vigorously whisk in 4 tbsp freshly grated Parmesan, 125ml cold beef stock, a little freshly grated nutmeg, and 1 pinch of salt.

Bring the remaining stock to a boil in a pot. Briefly remove from the heat and gradually pour in the egg mixture in a steady stream while stirring constantly. Return the pot to the heat and simmer for another 5 minutes over low heat, stirring vigorously with the whisk, until small egg flakes form.
Immediately ladle into warmed soup bowls and serve at once, while the egg flakes are still tender.
Eierflöckchensuppe.tips: Crispy toasted bread slices go wonderfully alongside. A classic recipe that comes together in no time. In Rome, you will find this soup on the menu in every restaurant.

Kichererbsensuppe.name: Chickpea Soup
steps: Soak 300g chickpeas overnight in 3/4 l of water. The next day, add 1 l of beef broth and bring to a heat, then let the chickpeas simmer for about 2 hours.
After 1.5 hours, prepare the remaining ingredients: cut the bacon, 4 celery stalks, and 2 carrots into fine strips. Chop 1 onion and 2 cloves of garlic.

In a pan, heat the bacon strips with 3 tbsp of olive oil and gently fry. Stir in the onion, garlic, carrots, and celery, and sauté over low heat for 5 minutes. Stir 2 tbsp of tomato paste with a few tablespoons of the chickpea broth until smooth, then mix in.

Once the chickpeas are tender, add the contents of the pan to the pot and mix everything thoroughly. Season generously with salt and pepper. Garnish with parsley leaves. Toast 8 slices of bread and serve alongside the soup.
Kichererbsensuppe.tips: For a quick version, use canned chickpeas.

Bohneneintopf mit Fenchel.name: Bean Stew with Fennel
ingredients: 1 slice of streaky bacon with rind (100g)
steps: Soak 300g beans the day before in 1 l of water. Bring to a boil in the soaking water and let simmer for 1 hour. Drain the beans, reserving the cooking liquid and keeping it warm.

Prick the bratwurst sausages with a fork. Cut 1 onion into strips. Heat 4 tbsp of oil in a pot, then gently fry the sausages and onion. Press in 2 cloves of garlic. Add the bacon and drained beans. Pour in the hot bean cooking liquid. Simmer over low heat for 30 minutes.
Cut the savoy cabbage leaves into fine strips. Halve the fennel bulbs, cut off the stalks, and slice the fennel hearts into thin strips.

Add 3/4 l of hot beef broth, the savoy cabbage, and fennel to the pot. Simmer gently for another 30 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Chop the parsley and stir in. Serve the bean stew sprinkled with 50g of freshly grated cheese.

Gefüllte Tintenfische.name: Stuffed Squid
ingredients: 2 tsp fresh rosemary or 1 tsp dried
steps: Thoroughly wash the uncleaned squid under cold running water. Then peel off the thin skin from the pouch-like squid body — the outside should look nice and white.
Pull the tentacles and innards out of the body. Use a sharp knife to cut off and discard the innards and head. Set the tentacles aside for the stuffing.

Turn the pouch inside out and wash the inside thoroughly as well, removing any remaining skin if necessary. Pat everything dry with paper towels. Turn the body pouch back the right way, drizzle the inside with the juice of 1/2 lemon, and set aside.

For the stuffing, finely chop the tentacles and, if needed, part of the pouches (about 200g of squid in total) with a sharp knife and place in a bowl. Finely chop 1 tsp of rosemary needles and the parsley, then mix in.
Briefly rinse 3–4 anchovy fillets and pat dry with paper towels. Finely chop them and add to the bowl. Press in 2 cloves of garlic. Add 3 tbsp breadcrumbs, the grated zest of 1/2 lemon, and 1 egg.
Mix everything thoroughly and season with salt and pepper (go easy on the salt, as the anchovy fillets can be quite salty). Fill the prepared squid pouches with the mixture.

Sew the opening of each stuffed squid pouch closed with white kitchen twine. Arrange the squid side by side in a greased ovenproof dish or on a grill rack. Brush generously with olive oil, season with salt and pepper, and sprinkle with a little rosemary. Place in a preheated oven (gas mark 3) or under a hot broiler. Bake for about 40 minutes (broiler: 20 minutes), turning at least once. Serve very hot, garnished with fresh herbs and lemon wedges if desired.
Gefüllte Tintenfische.tips: Wine suggestion: a dry white wine, such as Vernaccia di Oristano. Thawed frozen squid can also be used for this recipe.

Thunfisch mit Erbsen.name: Tuna with Peas
steps: Shell the fresh peas. Finely dice 1 onion. Finely chop 1/2 bunch of parsley. Melt 3 tbsp of butter in a wide pot. Sauté the onion and chopped parsley.
Stir in the peas and season with salt and pepper. Pour in 1/4 l of white wine, cover the pot, and pre-cook the peas over low heat for about 15 minutes. Then stir in 2 tbsp of tomato paste.

Briefly rinse the tuna steaks under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels. Lightly season both sides with salt, then place on top of the peas. Close the lid again and cook the fish for 10 minutes. Turn the tuna steaks and finish cooking for about 5 more minutes.
Season to taste with salt and coarsely ground pepper, and garnish with small parsley leaves and basil.
Thunfisch mit Erbsen.tips: A similar preparation is also popular with shark steaks. Variation: lightly dust the fish with flour, briefly sear in olive oil, and then place on the peas.

Gegrillte Sardinen mit Fenchel.name: Grilled Sardines with Fennel
steps: Gut the sardines, wash them thoroughly, and pat dry with paper towels. Season inside and out with salt and place in a shallow dish. Finely chop 3 cloves of garlic and whisk together with 50 ml of olive oil, then stir in 1 tbsp of fennel seeds. Drizzle over the fish. Pour in 1/8 l of wine, cover, and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

Bring about 1 l of salted water to a boil in a pot. Trim the fennel bulbs and blanch for 5 minutes in the boiling water. Rinse under cold water and drain well. Cut crosswise into 2 cm thick slices. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Remove the fish from the marinade, let them drain, and pat dry slightly. Reserve the marinade liquid. Place the sardines and fennel slices on a grill rack and brush with some of the marinade.

Grill slowly over charcoal for 10–15 minutes (or cook in a grill pan for 10 minutes instead). Brush the fish and vegetables with marinade occasionally and turn once or twice. Before serving, drizzle with lemon juice and season with salt and pepper.

Gefüllte Sardinen.name: Stuffed Sardines
ingredients: 100g fresh sheep's milk cheese (such as ricotta salata or fresh pecorino)
2 large cans of peeled tomatoes (800g each)
steps: Gut the fresh sardines, cut off the heads, and remove the backbone along with the tail. Rinse the fish thoroughly under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels. Lightly salt the insides and drizzle with the juice of 1/2 lemon.
Cut the sheep's milk cheese into small cubes, then halve them. Finely chop 1/2 bunch of parsley. Fill each sardine with cheese cubes, a little parsley, and half an anchovy fillet.
Drain the canned tomatoes and roughly chop them. Reserve the juice for another use. Spread the tomatoes in a shallow ovenproof dish and season with salt and pepper. Preheat the oven to 225°C.

Dust the fish lightly all over with a total of 3 tbsp of flour. Heat 5 tbsp of olive oil in a pan. Fry the stuffed sardines for 2 minutes on each side. Arrange them side by side on top of the tomatoes.

Finely chop the remaining parsley and 3 cloves of garlic, then mix with 50g of grated cheese and 8 tbsp of breadcrumbs. Sprinkle over the stuffed sardines, drizzle everything with the remaining olive oil, and bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 4) for 10 minutes until golden. Serve with lemon wedges.
Gefüllte Sardinen.tips: Tomato-free variation: season the sardines with salt and pepper, lightly dust with flour, dip in beaten egg, and coat in the breadcrumb and cheese mixture. Fry in olive oil or place on a greased baking sheet, drizzle with oil and white wine, and bake in the oven. Wine suggestion: a young white wine, such as Nuragus di Cagliari.

Ochsenschwanzragout.name: Oxtail Ragout
steps: Have the butcher chop the oxtail into small pieces. Briefly rinse under cold running water. Bring 1 1/4 l of salted water to a boil, add the oxtail pieces, and simmer for 10 minutes. Drain and set aside about 1/8 l of the cooking liquid.
Cut the bacon into fine strips. Very finely chop 1 bunch of parsley and 2 cloves of garlic. Cut 2 carrots and 1 onion into tiny dice. Combine with the garlic and parsley in a small bowl.
Heat 2 tbsp of lard in a wide braising pot. Stir in the bacon strips and slowly render. Add the oxtail pieces in batches and brown well on all sides.
Add the prepared vegetable mixture and sauté. Pour in 1/8 l of white wine and let it reduce by half with the lid off. Add another 1/8 l of wine and season with salt and pepper.
Stir 3 tbsp of tomato paste into the reserved 1/8 l of cooking liquid until smooth, then pour over the meat. Close the pot and braise over low heat for about 2 1/2 hours.
After 2 hours, trim and wash the celery. Set the tender leafy tops aside. Slice the stalks into thin rounds, then add them to the meat after 2 1/2 hours and cook for a further 15 minutes.

Wash the celery leaves and 1 bunch of parsley and finely chop. Once the meat is done, season the sauce with salt, pepper, paprika powder, and 1 pinch of cinnamon. Scatter over the celery leaves and parsley, and serve the ragout in a warmed bowl.
Ochsenschwanzragout.tips: As an alternative to browning the meat in bacon and lard, there is also a version using olive oil: sear the oxtail in it over high heat, season with chopped garlic and crushed chili pepper, then stir in tomato purée and white wine. White bread makes a great side dish. Wine suggestion: a dry white wine, such as Frascati secco.

Milchlamm mit Knoblauchsauce.name: Milk-fed Lamb with Garlic Sauce
ingredients: 1 kg tender lamb shoulder, fresh or frozen (milk-fed lamb if available)
steps: First, prepare the sauce. Finely chop the rosemary. Rinse 3 anchovy fillets, pat dry with paper towels, and chop finely. Chop 3 cloves of garlic. Place everything in a mortar and pound into a paste. Gradually stir in 3–4 tbsp of wine vinegar to form a smooth cream.
Halve 2 cloves of garlic and rub the lamb all over vigorously. Then cut the meat into smaller pieces (about 50g each) and season with freshly ground pepper.
Heat 6 tbsp of olive oil in a large pan. Add the lamb pieces and brown well on all sides, turning frequently, for 5 minutes. Reduce the heat and continue to cook gently for about 5 more minutes.
Once the meat is done, stir in the garlic cream thoroughly. Let it heat through briefly, sprinkle with freshly ground pepper, add salt if needed, and serve on a warmed platter.
Milchlamm mit Knoblauchsauce.tips: Milk-fed lamb is mainly available at Easter! If using slightly less tender lamb, leave it in the pan a little longer.

Kalbsschnitzel mit Salbei.name: Veal Cutlets with Sage
ingredients: 8 thin slices of tender veal cutlet (500g)
8 slices of raw ham, sliced paper-thin (Parma ham is best)
steps: Gently pound the veal cutlets flat using the smooth side of a meat mallet.
Place 1 slice of ham and 1 sage leaf on each cutlet and secure to the meat with a toothpick.
Melt 2 tbsp butter in a pan. Add the cutlets and fry for 2–3 minutes per side. Season the meat only lightly with salt and pepper, then remove and keep warm, covered.

Deglaze the pan drippings with 1/8 l white wine and bring to a vigorous boil. Whisk in 1–2 tbsp butter to finish the sauce. Adjust seasoning, return the cutlets to the pan briefly to heat through. Arrange on warmed plates and serve drizzled with the sauce.
Kalbsschnitzel mit Salbei.tips: They literally jump in your mouth! A delicious accompaniment: crusty white bread and fresh peas. Wine suggestion: a full-bodied white wine, such as Est! Est!! Est!!! di Montefiascone.

Kalbsbraten mit Kapernsauce.name: Roast Veal with Caper Sauce
steps: Season the veal round all over with pepper and dust lightly with 1–2 tbsp flour. Finely chop 1 carrot, the white parts of 3 spring onions (set the green tops aside), 1/2 bunch of parsley, and 2 garlic cloves.

Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a braising pot. Add the veal and brown on all sides. Stir in the chopped vegetables, parsley, and garlic and sauté briefly. Season with salt and pepper, then pour in 1/8 l dry white wine and 1/8 l stock. Scrub the lemon half under hot water, zest it, then cut into thin slices and add to the pot.
Close the pot tightly and braise for about 50 minutes. After about 40 minutes, cut the second carrot into fine strips and slice the green tops of the 3 spring onions into thin rings.

After 50 minutes, remove the veal and discard the lemon slices. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve and return it to the pot. Add the veal back in, then stir the finely cut vegetables and 2 tbsp capers into the sauce. Cover and cook for a further 10–15 minutes.

Finely chop the remaining parsley. Remove the finished roast, slice very thinly, and arrange on a warmed serving platter. Stir the parsley into the sauce, season to taste with salt and pepper, and spoon over the meat slices.
Kalbsbraten mit Kapernsauce.tips: Capers are delicate, grey-green buds that develop their wonderfully piquant flavour only through salting and pickling. Just a few spoonfuls are enough to add extra refinement to a sauce, a ragù, or a stuffing. The smallest capers are also the finest.

Geflügelragout mit Tomaten.name: Chicken Ragù with Tomatoes
steps: Cut the chicken into 10 pieces and rub with salt and pepper. Cut the bacon into thin strips. Blanch the tomatoes briefly in boiling water, then peel and deseed them. Roughly chop the flesh.

Heat 2 tbsp lard in a wide braising pot or large pan and fry the bacon strips in it. Brown the chicken pieces well on all sides in the hot fat. Sprinkle with half the marjoram, stir in the tomato pieces, and press in 2 garlic cloves through a garlic press.
Braise over gentle heat for about 40 minutes, gradually adding 1/4 l wine. The sauce for the ragù should reduce to a fairly thick consistency.
Once the meat is falling off the bone, adjust the seasoning with salt and freshly ground pepper. Serve sprinkled with fresh marjoram.
Geflügelragout mit Tomaten.tips: If you can only find dried marjoram, scatter some fresh parsley or chives over the dish before serving.

Wildschweinragout mit Pflaumen.name: Wild Boar Ragù with Prunes
ingredients: For the marinade: 1/2 l robust red wine
steps: For the marinade, bring 1/2 l red wine and 1/8 l red wine vinegar to a boil in a saucepan. Clean and roughly chop the onion, 1 carrot, and 1 stalk of celery, then add to the pot along with all the listed spices. Simmer vigorously, covered, for 5 minutes, then leave to cool.
Cut the wild boar into 2–3 cm cubes and place in a bowl. Pour the cold marinade over the meat, cover, and leave to marinate overnight in the refrigerator.
Remove the meat cubes from the marinade and pat dry with paper towels. Strain the marinade through a fine sieve and set aside.
Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a braising pot. Brown the meat in batches over medium heat on all sides. Season with salt and pepper, pour in half the marinade, and braise in the covered pot for 1.5 hours.
Soak the dried prunes in the remaining marinade. Soak the 30g raisins in water.

After about 1 hour of braising, cut 30g candied orange peel into tiny cubes. Drain the prunes and raisins, reserving the marinade. Cut the prunes into thin strips. Finely chop 1 onion and 1 tsp rosemary.

Melt 2 tbsp butter in a pan. Sauté the onion until translucent. Stir in the prunes, candied orange peel, raisins, and rosemary. Mix everything thoroughly and season with freshly ground pepper. Pour in the reserved marinade and cook vigorously for a few minutes.

Add the fruit mixture to the wild boar ragù and braise for a further 10 minutes. Once the meat is tender and falling apart, season the sauce with salt and pepper. Sprinkle with 2 tbsp pine nuts if desired and serve piping hot.
Wildschweinragout mit Pflaumen.tips: Going by the combination of ingredients, this is undoubtedly a Roman recipe — though the sight of roaming, semi-wild boar is far more a part of everyday village life in Sardinia.

Rebhuhn mit Linsen.name: Partridge with Lentils
steps: Cover the 200g lentils with water and soak overnight. Place in the soaking water, bring to a boil, and par-cook for 30 minutes. Meanwhile, remove any remaining pin feathers from the partridges. Drain the lentils, reserving the cooking water.
Cut the bacon into small cubes. Place in a pot with 1 tbsp lard, heat, and fry gently.
Season the partridges with salt and pepper. Brown on all sides in the hot bacon-lard mixture. Chop 1 onion, stir it in, and sauté over reduced heat until translucent.

Add the lentils, pour in about 1/4 l of the reserved cooking water, and stir in 1 tbsp tomato purée. Season generously with salt and pepper, then braise gently, half-covered, for about 50 minutes. Most of the liquid should evaporate.
Roughly chop the parsley. Adjust the seasoning of the lentils, halve the partridges if desired, and serve sprinkled with parsley.
Rebhuhn mit Linsen.tips: Wine suggestion: a spicy red wine, such as Monica di Sardegna.

Erbsen mit Schinken.name: Peas with Ham
ingredients: 1 kg fresh, tender peas (or 600g frozen as an alternative)
1/8 l good beef or veal stock
steps: Shell the peas. Cut the 2 small onions into thin slices. Trim the fatty part from the raw ham and cut it into strips. Heat 1/8 l stock in a small saucepan and keep ready.

Melt 1.5 tbsp butter and the fatty ham strips in a pan. Sauté the onion slices, stirring, until translucent. Stir in the peas and season with 1 pinch of sugar, salt, and pepper. Pour in 1/8 l hot stock and simmer for 10–20 minutes depending on the type of peas.

Meanwhile, cut the lean part of the ham into very thin strips. Toast thin slices of white bread in the toaster. Stir the ham strips and 1/2 tbsp butter into the just-tender peas, adjust seasoning with salt and pepper, and serve alongside the toast.
Erbsen mit Schinken.tips: A tasty little vegetable dish that also works well as a light supper (use the same quantity for 4 people in that case). Without the toast, it makes an ideal side dish for pan-fried liver or lean meat.

Artischocken auf jüdische Art.name: Artichokes Jewish-Style
steps: Fill a bowl with ice-cold water and mix in the juice of 1–2 lemons.

Cut off the woody stems of the artichokes. Remove the tough outer leaves, then use kitchen scissors to trim just the tips of the remaining leaves straight across. Place the artichokes immediately in the cold lemon water.
Remove the artichokes from the water, drain, and dry well. Loosen the leaves so they open up slightly like a flower. Season with salt and pepper.

Place the artichokes in a wide pot and pour in enough olive oil to half-cover them. Heat slowly. Fry the artichokes over medium heat for about 10 minutes, then turn and fry for another 10 minutes. The oil must not get too hot, or the outer leaves will burn before the artichoke hearts are fully cooked through.
Shortly before the end of cooking, carefully sprinkle the artichokes with a few drops of cold water. Lift out the crispy fried artichokes and drain well on paper towels. Serve with lemon wedges.
Artischocken auf jüdische Art.tips: A famous recipe from the Jewish ghetto of ancient Rome. Serve with bread!

Spinat mit Rosinen.name: Spinach with Raisins
steps: Soak 50g raisins in water. Wash the spinach and place in a pot while still wet. Heat, and allow to wilt briefly. Drain the spinach leaves well.
Heat 3–4 tbsp olive oil in a large pan and melt 2 tbsp butter in it. Halve 2 garlic cloves and fry over gentle heat, stirring, then remove from the pan.

Add the spinach to the pan and toss in the hot fat. Drain the raisins and mix them into the spinach. Cook gently for 10 minutes, stirring frequently. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Finish by scattering over 2 tbsp pine nuts and, if desired, the fried garlic.

Grüne Bohnen mit Thunfisch.name: Green Beans with Tuna
steps: Trim the green beans and remove any strings if needed. Wash and drain. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the beans and cook for 10–12 minutes until tender-crisp.

Meanwhile, wash the tomatoes, dice them, and remove the stem ends. Trim and wash the spring onions; finely chop the white parts and slice the green parts into thin rings. Drain the tuna and flake it finely with a fork.

Finely chop the parsley. In a bowl, whisk together 3 tbsp wine vinegar with salt, pepper, and 5 tbsp olive oil. Stir in the chopped white onion and half the parsley. Drain the cooked beans, rinse under cold water, and drain well. Add to the bowl and toss with the dressing.

Gently fold in the diced tomatoes, spring onion rings, and finally the tuna. Taste and adjust seasoning with vinegar, salt, and pepper if needed. Sprinkle with the remaining parsley and serve while still warm.

Kirschkuchen.name: Cherry Tart
ingredients: 1 unwaxed lemon (zest for the compote, juice for the pastry)
300 g flour, plus extra for rolling and the pan
100 g soft butter, plus 1 tbsp for the pan
steps: For the filling, start by making the cherry compote. Pit the sour cherries. Scrub the lemon thoroughly and cut the zest off in a spiral (reserve the juice for the pastry). Place the cherries and lemon zest in a saucepan, add 150 g sugar, 2 cloves, and 1 cinnamon stick. Cook uncovered over medium-high heat for about 15 minutes, stirring, until all the liquid has evaporated. If desired, stir in a splash of red wine or a spirit. Let cool, then remove the lemon zest, cloves, and cinnamon stick.

For the pastry, mix 300 g flour with 150 g sugar. Quickly work in 100 g soft butter cut into small pieces, 50 g lard, 1 egg, 1 egg yolk, and the juice of 1 lemon until a smooth dough forms. If it becomes too stiff, add a few tablespoons of milk while kneading. Place the dough in a bowl, cover, and refrigerate for 1 hour.

Grease a 26 cm springform pan with 1 tbsp butter and dust lightly with flour. Remove the dough from the refrigerator and roll out two-thirds of it on a floured surface into a thin circle. Line the base of the pan with the dough, pressing it up the sides to form a rim of about 5 cm.
Preheat the oven to 175°C. Spread the prepared cherry compote evenly over the pastry base and smooth it out. Fold the overhanging edges inward over the filling.

Roll out the remaining dough thinly and use a pastry wheel to cut strips about 1 cm wide. Arrange the strips in a lattice pattern over the tart. Whisk 1 egg yolk with 1 tbsp milk and brush the lattice with it. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for 30–40 minutes. Remove and let cool completely. Best served the next day — dust lightly with powdered sugar if desired.

Ricotta-Eis mit Rum.name: Ricotta Ice Cream with Rum
ingredients: 500 g fresh ricotta (or a 1:1 mix of quark and cream cheese)
steps: Beat 4 egg yolks with 100 g sugar in a bowl until light and fluffy. Gradually drizzle in 4 cl rum, stirring vigorously.

Press the ricotta through a fine-mesh sieve, then fold it into the egg mixture a spoonful at a time. Season with the finely grated zest of half a lemon. Whip the cream to stiff peaks, then fold it gently into the ricotta mixture in portions.
Line a shallow loaf pan with parchment paper. Pour in the mixture and smooth the top. Cover with parchment paper. Freeze in the freezer for 2–3 hours.
Remove from the freezer 30 minutes before serving and let soften slightly. Turn out onto a decorative platter. Slice and serve.
Ricotta-Eis mit Rum.tips: Best garnished with fresh fruit and berries — for example, melon balls, apricot slices, and blackberries.

Mandeltorte.name: Almond Cake
ingredients: 1/2 unwaxed lemon (zest + a dash of juice)
50 g flour, plus 1 tbsp for the pan
Butter or oil for the pan
steps: For the batter, chop the blanched almonds as finely as possible with a large knife, or grind them coarsely in a nut grinder.
Separate the 4 eggs. Beat the 4 egg yolks with 100 g sugar and 50 g vanilla sugar in a bowl with a whisk until light, pale, and airy.
Stir in the chopped almonds, 50 g flour, and 1 tsp baking powder. Finely grate the zest of half a lemon and add it to the batter. Preheat the oven to 175°C.
Beat the 4 egg whites with a dash of lemon juice until stiff peaks form. Fold them into the batter in 2–3 portions, keeping the mixture light.

Grease a round 28 cm cake pan and dust lightly with flour. Pour in the batter and smooth the top. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 40 minutes. Remove from the pan and let cool on a wire rack.

For the glaze, stir 150 g powdered sugar together with 2 tbsp lemon juice until smooth. Stir in 2 tbsp almond liqueur if desired. Roughly chop the almonds for decoration and toast them in a dry pan, stirring, until lightly golden. Spread the lemon glaze evenly over the cooled cake using a wide knife. Scatter the toasted almonds over the top before the glaze sets completely.
Mandeltorte.tips: Wine pairing: a dessert wine, such as a Malvasia or Moscato from Sardinia.

Käsetörtchen.name: Little Cheese Pastries
ingredients: 200 g flour, plus extra for rolling
400 g fresh cheese (such as ricotta or cream cheese)
steps: For the pastry, mix 200 g flour with 200 g semolina. Knead with just under 250 ml lukewarm water to form a smooth dough. Season with a pinch of salt, add 50 g soft butter cut into small pieces, and knead in firmly. Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest while you prepare the filling.

For the filling, beat 1 egg with 60 g sugar in a bowl until frothy. Stir in the saffron powder until dissolved and season with a pinch of salt. Gradually add the fresh cheese, mixing thoroughly. Wash and dry the lemon and orange, then finely grate their zests into the cheese mixture and stir to combine.

Preheat the oven to 175°C. Grease a baking sheet with oil. Roll the dough out on a floured surface until 2 mm thin. Cut out circles 8–10 cm in diameter using a glass. Re-roll the scraps and cut out more circles.

Separate 1 egg and lightly beat the egg white. Place 1 tbsp of cheese filling in the center of half the dough circles. Lightly brush the edges with egg white, top each with a second dough circle, and press the edges together firmly with a fork.

Place the filled pastries on the prepared baking sheet. Stir the egg yolk until smooth and brush it over the tops. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 25 minutes. Let cool and serve dusted with powdered sugar.
Käsetörtchen.tips: Finely chopped nuts also work very well in the filling.

Geröstetes Knoblauchbrot.name: Toasted Garlic Bread
ingredients: 4 large slices rustic country bread (sourdough is best)
steps: Blanch the tomatoes, peel, and remove the seeds. Roughly chop the flesh and let it drain.
Halve the 4 bread slices and toast them on both sides in a toaster or under the broiler (alternatively, fry them in a little olive oil in a pan until crispy).
Peel the 4 garlic cloves and halve them. Rub the freshly toasted bread vigorously with the cut garlic, then drizzle 1 tbsp olive oil over each slice.
Spread the tomato flesh on top and crush it slightly with a fork. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper. Serve immediately.
Geröstetes Knoblauchbrot.tips: Hearty country bread with a firm crumb and olive oil that truly delivers on flavor are what make this dish.

Kartoffelpizza.name: Potato Pizza
steps: Wash the potatoes and cook them in their skins. Peel while still hot and press through a potato ricer. Mix in 1 tsp salt and 2 tbsp olive oil. Let cool.
Briefly blanch the tomatoes in boiling water. Rinse in cold water, peel, and remove the seeds. Roughly chop the flesh, sprinkle with salt, and let drain well in a colander.

Rinse the anchovy fillets briefly, pat dry with paper towels, and halve if desired. Press 2 garlic cloves through a garlic press and mix into the well-drained tomatoes. Cut the mozzarella into small cubes.

Preheat the oven to 200°C. Grease a round 28 cm baking pan with 1 tbsp olive oil. Knead the riced potatoes with 50 g flour, spread the dough in the pan, pressing it out evenly, and form a raised edge all around.

Spread the tomatoes over the potato base and season generously with freshly ground pepper. Distribute the mozzarella on top, then lay the anchovies and olives over it. Bake until cooked through and golden.

Zwiebelpizza (Apulien).name: Onion Pizza (Apulia)
ingredients: 200 g flour, plus extra for rolling
2 tbsp olive oil, plus oil for the pan
steps: Dissolve 10 g yeast with a pinch of sugar in 2 tbsp lukewarm water, then stir in 2 tbsp flour.
Place the remaining flour in a bowl and make a well in the center. Pour the yeast mixture into the well, dust lightly with flour, cover, and leave to rise in a warm place for 30 minutes.

Add 1/2 tsp salt and 2 tbsp olive oil to the flour, then gradually knead in 125 ml lukewarm water to form a smooth dough. Knead vigorously on a board or work surface, then return the dough to the bowl. Cover with a cloth and leave to rise in a warm place for 1 hour, until the dough has doubled in volume.
For the topping, slice the onions into very thin rings (or finely chop them). Preheat the oven to 225°C. Grease a round, flat 26 cm pan with oil.
Knead the dough vigorously on a lightly floured surface. Place it in the pan and press it out evenly with your hands. Brush the surface of the dough with 1 tbsp olive oil.

Spread the onions over the dough and sprinkle with freshly grated Pecorino and oregano leaves if using. Drizzle the remaining 3 tbsp olive oil over the top. Slide the pizza into the well-preheated oven (gas mark 4) and bake for 25 minutes.
Season the freshly baked, crispy pizza with a little salt and plenty of freshly ground pepper, and serve immediately.
Zwiebelpizza (Apulien).tips: There's no need to pile on elaborate toppings — the best pizza is made with the simplest ingredients and a short list of components.

Öhrchennudeln mit Broccoli (Apulien).name: Orecchiette with Broccoli (Apulia)
ingredients: 200g all-purpose flour, plus extra for rolling
1 dried or pickled peperoncino (or chili pepper)
steps: The day before, mix 100g semolina with 200g flour in a bowl. Add 2 tbsp olive oil and 1 tsp salt, then knead with just under 250ml lukewarm water into a smooth dough. Shape into a loaf, cover with a damp cloth, and let rest for 20 minutes.

Cut off small portions of dough one at a time and roll on a floured surface into finger-thick logs (about 2 cm in diameter). Cut the logs into slices about ½ cm thick, then press each slice with your thumb to create a small indentation in the center. Cover with a kitchen towel and leave to dry overnight.

The next day, clean the broccoli and break into florets. Peel the thicker stalks if necessary. Bring 3 liters of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Add the broccoli stalks, then after 5 minutes add the florets and blanch for another 5 minutes.

Remove the vegetables with a slotted spoon, refresh in cold water, and drain. Bring the cooking water back to a boil. Add the prepared pasta to the boiling water and cook for about 10 minutes until al dente.

Meanwhile, cut the broccoli stalks into small pieces. Finely chop 3 garlic cloves. Slice the peperoncino into thin rings. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet. Sauté the broccoli and garlic, then stir in the peperoncino. Season with salt and pepper. Pour in a few tablespoons of the vegetable cooking water.
Once the orecchiette are cooked, drain well. Add them directly to the skillet and toss thoroughly with the vegetables. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper and serve.
Öhrchennudeln mit Broccoli (Apulien).tips: Traditionally, these sauce-friendly durum wheat pasta are served with young, tender rapini (turnip greens). A hearty lamb ragù pairs just as well, as does a simple tomato sauce with freshly grated pecorino.

Artischockenreis (Apulien).name: Artichoke Rice (Apulia)
ingredients: A few leaves of fresh mint
steps: Trim the artichokes, remove the outer leaves, and cut off the tough tips of the remaining leaves. Fill a bowl with cold water and the juice of 1 lemon, and place the cleaned artichokes in it immediately.
Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F). Finely chop the parsley and 2 garlic cloves, and slice a few mint leaves into thin strips. Mix everything together with the grated zest of 1 lemon.

Pour 250g rice into a wide, oven-safe pot. Drain the artichokes and place them side by side on top of the rice. Sprinkle with the herb mixture, season with salt and pepper, and drizzle with 4 tbsp olive oil.
Pour in 750ml hot chicken broth, cover the pot, and place in the preheated oven (gas mark 3). Cook for about 25 minutes until the rice is al dente. Add more broth or hot water as needed.
Sprinkle the finished dish with 30g freshly grated pecorino and serve immediately.

Öhrchennudeln mit Rucola (Apulien).name: Orecchiette with Arugula (Apulia)
ingredients: 350g arugula (or young dandelion leaves, watercress, or tender baby spinach as substitutes)
300g orecchiette (homemade per recipe on page 216, or store-bought)
steps: Clean and wash the arugula. Bring 2 liters of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Blanch the arugula in it for 1 minute, refresh in cold water, and drain. Using the same pot, bring another 3 liters of salted water to a boil for the pasta.
Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed. Finely chop the flesh with a large knife. Place in a saucepan, stir in 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper, bring to heat, and let simmer gently.
Add 300g orecchiette to the boiling salted water and cook for 10–15 minutes until al dente.

Meanwhile, cut the ham into thin strips. Finely chop 1 onion and 2 garlic cloves. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet. Stir in the onion and ham and sauté gently. Add the arugula and garlic, pour in a few tablespoons of the hot pasta cooking water, and season with salt and pepper.

Gemüseeintopf.name: Vegetable Stew
steps: Clean and wash the vegetables: slice the fennel bulb crosswise into thin rounds, reserving the fennel fronds. Slice the chicory, carrots, celery, and leek into rounds; cut the leaves of ½ white cabbage into thin strips.
Heat plenty of salted water in a large pot. Blanch all the vegetables in batches for 2 minutes each. Remove with a slotted spoon, refresh in cold water, and drain well. Rinse and dry the pot.

Cut the bacon into thin strips. Coat the large pot with 2 tbsp olive oil and heat. Fry the bacon strips until crispy, then press in 2 garlic cloves through a garlic press and stir together. Remove the bacon strips and set aside.

Heat 2 liters of beef broth in a separate pot. Meanwhile, layer the vegetables in the large pot. Sprinkle each layer with the garlic-bacon mixture and a little grated cheese, drizzle with olive oil, and season generously with freshly ground pepper.
Pour the hot beef broth over the layered vegetables and sprinkle with the remaining cheese. Cover the pot and let the stew simmer gently for about 45 minutes.
Gemüseeintopf.tips: It is also common to cook the layered vegetable soup in the oven — or simply to finish it by sprinkling cheese on top at the end and broiling until golden and crispy.

Bandnudeln mit Safransauce.name: Fettuccine with Saffron Sauce
ingredients: 4 tbsp freshly grated pecorino or parmesan
steps: Separate the zucchini blossoms from their stems. Slice 4 of the blossoms, the stems, and the basil into thin strips. Finely chop the parsley and 1 onion.
Bring 3 liters of salted water to a boil in a large pot for the fettuccine.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan. Sauté the onion. Dissolve the saffron in 2 tbsp lukewarm water and stir it in. Add the sliced zucchini blossoms, stems, and herbs, pour in 125ml beef broth, and let simmer gently. Season with salt and pepper.

Add the fettuccine to the boiling salted water and cook for 8–10 minutes until al dente. If needed, thin the sauce with a few tablespoons of pasta cooking water in between. Place the remaining 4 zucchini blossoms on top of the sauce, cover the pot, and let the blossoms steam briefly.

Drain the al dente pasta. Toss immediately with the sauce and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Garnish with the whole, briefly steamed zucchini blossoms. Serve the 4 tbsp grated pecorino on the side.

Nudeln mit Linsen.name: Pasta with Lentils
steps: Cover 200g lentils with water and soak overnight.
The next day, dice the bacon and 1 onion into small cubes. Wash 2 celery stalks and slice into rounds; set aside the remaining 2 stalks with their leaves for later.

In a large pot, gently fry the bacon with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the onion and celery and sauté until translucent. Add the lentils along with their soaking water. Press in 2 garlic cloves through a garlic press.
Let the lentils simmer gently for 30–40 minutes, adding 125ml white wine and a little water as needed — the lentils should have a soupy consistency.

Season the lentils generously with salt and pepper. Slice the remaining 2 celery stalks into thin rounds. Break 200g spaghetti into 3 cm pieces. Add both to the lentils and cook for 8–10 minutes. Finely chop the tender celery leaves. Adjust the seasoning of the lentils once more. Drizzle with 3 tbsp olive oil and scatter the celery leaves on top.

Spaghetti mit Knoblauch, Öl und Pfeffers.name: Spaghetti with Garlic, Oil, and Chili
ingredients: 1 peperoncino (dried hot red chili pepper), or fresh chili as a substitute
steps: Bring 4 liters of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Add the spaghetti and cook for 8–10 minutes until al dente.
Meanwhile, heat 50ml olive oil in a large skillet. Peel and halve 3 garlic cloves, then add them to the hot oil along with the dried peperoncino and sauté gently, stirring.

Finely chop the parsley. Drain the al dente spaghetti and let it drip dry. Remove the peperoncino and garlic from the skillet, add the spaghetti and parsley to the hot oil, and toss everything thoroughly. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper. Place the peperoncino back on top of the finished spaghetti as garnish and serve immediately.
Spaghetti mit Knoblauch, Öl und Pfeffers.tips: Only leave the peperoncino in the oil until the heat reaches your personal preference. Those who like it spicier can leave the chili in the skillet right to the end.

Gefüllte Muscheln.name: Stuffed Mussels
ingredients: 3 tbsp freshly grated pecorino or parmesan
4 tbsp coarse breadcrumbs (freshly made if possible)
steps: Scrub the mussels thoroughly under running water, debeard, and scrub clean. Discard any mussels that are already open.

Place the mussels in a large pot, pour in 250ml white wine, bring to heat, and cook for about 5 minutes until the mussels open. Discard any mussels that remain closed. Remove the mussels and let them cool slightly.

Pour the mussel cooking liquid through a fine sieve and set aside. Very finely chop 1 onion and 1 garlic clove. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in the pot and sauté the onion and garlic. Roughly break up 500g crushed tomatoes and stir in. Pour in the mussel liquid, season with salt and pepper, and reduce to a thick sauce. Pour the tomato sauce into a large, shallow, ovenproof dish. Preheat the oven to 225°C (435°F).
Gefüllte Muscheln.tips: Important: the mussels must be absolutely fresh — discard any shells that are open before cooking. Wine suggestion: Locorotondo.

Muscheln mit Kräuterpaste überbacken.name: Baked Mussels with Herb Paste
steps: Carefully break the cooled mussels apart at the hinge, discarding the empty shell halves. Arrange the shells with the mussel meat side by side.

For the filling, beat 2 eggs vigorously with a fork. Finely chop the parsley and basil, mince 2 garlic cloves, and mix with the eggs. Stir in 4 tbsp breadcrumbs and season the paste with salt, pepper, and 1 pinch of cayenne pepper.

Top each mussel half with 1 tsp of herb paste, then carefully place them into the tomato sauce (the sauce should not cover the mussels). Sprinkle with 3 tbsp freshly grated Pecorino and dot with 2 tbsp butter cut into small pieces.
Place the mussels in a preheated oven (gas mark 4) and bake for 10 minutes (or broil under the grill for about 5 minutes until golden). Serve with crusty white bread.

Rotbarben in Papierhülle.name: Red Mullet Baked in Parchment
steps: Scale the red mullet, wash them, and place in a bowl. Press 2 garlic cloves and mix with the juice of 1/2 lemon and 4 tbsp olive oil. Crumble 3 bay leaves finely, crush 1/2 tsp peppercorns in a mortar, and stir both into the marinade.
Drizzle the marinade over the red mullet. Leave to marinate in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, turning the fish once.
Preheat the oven to 200°C. Brush 4 large sheets of thick parchment paper (or alternatively aluminium foil or roasting bags) each with 1 tbsp olive oil.

Remove the red mullet from the marinade and place on the oiled parchment sheets. Season with salt and pepper and drizzle with the marinade. Pit and roughly chop 50g olives and distribute among the four portions. Wrap the fish in the parchment sheets, fold over the edges, and seal the parcels tightly.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 8 minutes. Serve the red mullet in their parchment parcels and open them only at the table.

Überbackene Goldbrasse.name: Baked Sea Bream
steps: Scale and gut the sea bream, rinse thoroughly, and pat dry with kitchen paper. Bring 1 litre of salted water to the boil in a pot. Wash, peel, and slice the potatoes into 1/2 cm thick rounds. Blanch in portions in the boiling salted water for 5 minutes, then drain.

Very finely chop the parsley and 3 garlic cloves (or grind in a mortar). Stir together with 8 tbsp olive oil to form a paste. Preheat the oven to 225°C. Brush a large ovenproof dish with 2 tbsp olive oil.
Layer half the potato slices in the dish. Season generously with salt and pepper, drizzle with some of the herb oil, and sprinkle with half the grated cheese.

Place the prepared fish on top of the potatoes, season with salt and pepper, and brush with some of the herb oil. Cover with the remaining potato slices and season again with salt, pepper, herb oil, and grated cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 4) for 30 minutes. Carve the sea bream at the table and serve piping hot.

Fischsuppe mit Garnelen.name: Fish Soup with Prawns
ingredients: 1 kg mixed fish fillets (redfish, haddock, hake, cod)
375ml dry white wine (or light fish stock)
steps: Cut the fish fillets into serving portions, sprinkle lightly with salt, cover, and refrigerate.

Wash 4 celery stalks and slice into 1 cm pieces. Set aside the tender leaves for later. Coarsely chop 2 onions. Finely chop 4 garlic cloves and crush in a mortar with 1/2 tsp peppercorns. Stir in 2 tbsp olive oil. Drain the canned tomatoes, chop them, and reserve the juice.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a large pot. Stir in the chopped onions and celery and sauté briefly. Add the tomatoes, pour in 375ml white wine, and bring to a vigorous boil. Stir in the garlic mixture and season with salt. Gradually add the reserved tomato juice and simmer briskly for 5 minutes.

Add the fish pieces to the pot. Cover and cook for 10 minutes, stirring in the prawns after 5 minutes and cooking until done. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Roughly chop the parsley and celery leaves and scatter over the soup.
Fischsuppe mit Garnelen.tips: Wine suggestion: a full-bodied white wine or a rosé, such as Castel del Monte bianco or rosato.

Lammbraten mit Oliven.name: Roast Lamb with Olives
ingredients: 1 small bunch of fresh oregano (or 1 tbsp dried)
steps: Dust the lamb thinly with 1 tbsp flour. Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a casserole dish, add the meat, and brown all over. Season with salt and pepper, pour in the juice of 1 lemon and 125ml stock. Cover and braise over low heat for 30 minutes.

Pit 100g olives, deseed the dried chilli, and finely chop both. Add to the lamb after 30 minutes of cooking time. Scatter in half the oregano leaves and pour in another 125ml stock. Cover and continue to braise for about 1½ hours until the meat is lovely and tender. Stir in 50g whole olives.

Season the sauce with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Carve the meat into thin slices and arrange on a warmed serving platter. Pour the sauce over the top, sprinkle with fresh oregano, and serve piping hot.
Lammbraten mit Oliven.tips: If the sauce is still too thin at the end, stir in a little cream and reduce to thicken. A refined addition: add a few fresh wild mushrooms to the sauce (about 30 minutes before the end of the cooking time).

Lammgulasch mit Zitronensauce.name: Lamb Stew with Lemon Sauce
steps: Trim the lamb of skin and sinew, cut into 2 cm cubes, and dust lightly with 1–2 tbsp flour. Dice 50g bacon and 1 onion. Heat 2 tbsp lard in a casserole dish. Add the bacon and fry gently. Add the meat in batches and brown well, stirring. Add the onion and cook until translucent.
Deglaze with 125ml white wine and season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of freshly grated nutmeg. Continue to cook, stirring, until the wine has evaporated.
Add a little more wine and stock, close the pot, and braise the lamb on the lowest heat for about 1 hour. Add more wine and stock as needed.
Once the meat is tender, lift the pieces out with a slotted spoon and keep warm on a preheated platter, covered.

In a small bowl, whisk 3–4 tbsp lemon juice with 3 egg yolks and 1 pressed garlic clove, then stir into the hot sauce and heat briefly (do not boil). Pour the sauce over the meat, sprinkle with lemon zest, and serve piping hot.

Lammbraten mit Kartoffeln.name: Roast Lamb with Potatoes
steps: Rub the leg of lamb all over with the juice of 1 lemon (keep the lemon halves). Wash, peel, and slice the potatoes into 1/2 cm thick rounds. Blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed them.

Brush a large roasting tin with 2 tbsp olive oil. Layer in the potato slices, season with salt and pepper, and dot with 2 tbsp lard cut into small pieces. Distribute the tomatoes among the potatoes. Preheat the oven to 175°C.

Very finely chop the parsley and 4 garlic cloves, and mix with 4 tbsp breadcrumbs and the finely grated zest of the lemon. Stir two-thirds of this mixture with 4 tbsp olive oil into a paste. Mix the remainder with 50g freshly grated Pecorino and set aside for later.

Season the leg of lamb with salt and pepper. Coat it all over with the parsley paste. Place on top of the potato slices in the roasting tin and roast in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 1½ hours.
15 minutes before the end of the cooking time, increase the oven temperature to 225°C (gas mark 4).
Scatter the parsley and cheese mixture over the lamb and potatoes, and dot with 1 tbsp lard cut into small pieces. Bake until golden and crispy.
Lammbraten mit Kartoffeln.tips: In Italy this dish is also popular made with tender kid goat. It is a traditional Easter recipe from the Abruzzo region. Wine suggestion: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo or a Rosso di Cerignola.

Gefüllte Paprikaschoten.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers
steps: Wash and dry the bell peppers. Cut a lid off the stem end of each pepper and set aside. Remove the white ribs and seeds from the inside.
For the filling: remove the crusts from the 2 bread rolls and use only the soft interior. Tear the crumb into tiny pieces, place in a small bowl, and soak with 2–3 tbsp olive oil.
Finely chop the parsley. Briefly rinse 4 anchovy fillets, pat dry, and finely chop. Press or finely mince 2 garlic cloves.
Add the chopped parsley, anchovies, and garlic to the soaked breadcrumbs along with 2 tbsp grated cheese and 2 tbsp capers. Mix everything together thoroughly. Preheat the oven to 175°C.
Season to taste with salt (go easy, given the anchovies!) and pepper. Gradually stir in about 4–5 tbsp olive oil so the mixture binds well. Brush an ovenproof dish with 1 tbsp oil.
Divide the filling among the prepared peppers and sprinkle 1/2 tbsp Pecorino over each one. Replace the cut-off lids and brush the outside of the stuffed peppers with oil.
Stand the peppers upright in the dish and bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for 20 minutes. Serve hot, either as a side dish with a meat course or cold as a starter.
Gefüllte Paprikaschoten.tips: In other parts of Italy also known as involtini di peperoni — made with various fillings. Red or green bell peppers can also be used.

Kartoffel-Pilzauflauf.name: Potato and Mushroom Bake
ingredients: 500 g fresh, large button mushrooms, cremini mushrooms, or fresh wild mushrooms
50 g breadcrumbs (preferably freshly grated coarse white bread crumbs)
steps: Wash, peel, and slice the potatoes into ½ cm thick rounds. Clean the mushrooms and trim the stem ends. Slice the mushrooms thickly and immediately drizzle with the juice of 1 lemon.
Cut the parsley leaves into fine strips and mix with 50 g breadcrumbs and 50 g grated cheese.

Preheat the oven to 175°C. Grease an ovenproof dish with 2 tbsp oil. Layer the potato and mushroom slices alternately in several layers. Season each layer with salt and pepper, sprinkle with the herb-cheese-breadcrumb mixture, and drizzle generously with olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 1 hour, until the potatoes are tender. Serve immediately.
Kartoffel-Pilzauflauf.tips: A delicious side dish for braised meat.

Überbackene Kartoffeln.name: Potato Gratin with Tomatoes and Onions
steps: Wash, peel, and slice the potatoes into ½ cm thick rounds. Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel, and slice. Slice the 3 onions thinly as well.
Preheat the oven to 175°C. Grease a large ovenproof dish with 2 tbsp olive oil.

Layer the potatoes, tomatoes, and onions in the dish in several overlapping, shingle-style layers. Season each layer with salt and pepper, sprinkle with oregano leaves and grated cheese, and drizzle with wine and a little oil (set aside 2 tbsp of the cheese to use right at the end).

Top the final layer with 2 tbsp of butter in small flakes. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 1 hour. Once the potatoes are tender (test with the tip of a knife), sprinkle with the remaining 2 tbsp of cheese and return to the oven for 2 more minutes. Serve hot, garnished with fresh oregano.

Ausgebackene Zwiebeln.name: Deep-Fried Onions in Herb Batter
ingredients: 500 g pearl onions or small onions
steps: First, prepare the batter: whisk 2 egg yolks with salt and pepper in a bowl. Add 100 g flour and 100 ml milk a tablespoon at a time, stirring until smooth. Stir in 3 tbsp grated cheese and press in 2 garlic cloves through a garlic press. Leave the batter to rest for 30 minutes.

Peel the onions. Bring 2 litres of salted water to a boil in a pot and blanch the onions in the rapidly boiling water for 2 minutes. Rinse under cold water, drain well, and pat dry with a kitchen towel.
Finely chop the parsley and stir into the batter. Beat 2 egg whites to stiff peaks and gently fold in.

Heat plenty of oil in a pot (or deep-fryer). Dip the onions into the batter using a fork, then place them in the hot oil and fry until golden and crispy. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain well on paper towels. Serve as hot as possible, immediately.
Ausgebackene Zwiebeln.tips: Lampasciuoli — the name for the bulbs of a hyacinth species considered a specialty in Apulia. They have a slightly bitter flavour and are prepared using the recipe above, or pickled with spices, grilled, or baked au gratin.

Schokoladenkuchen.name: Chocolate Cake
ingredients: 80 g butter + 1 tbsp for the tin
steps: Finely grind 100 g almonds (or buy ready-ground almonds if needed).
Separate the 5 eggs. Beat the 5 egg yolks with 100 g sugar in a bowl until pale and fluffy. Meanwhile, melt 80 g butter in a small saucepan.

Mix 100 g flour with 2 tbsp cornstarch. Fold into the egg mixture a spoonful at a time, alternating with the ground almonds. Remove the melted butter from the heat, let it cool slightly, then stir it into the batter as well.
Preheat the oven to 200°C. Grease a springform tin (28 cm diameter) with 1 tbsp of soft butter.

Beat the 5 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold thoroughly and evenly into the batter. Pour into the prepared tin and smooth the surface. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for 25 minutes. Then cover the surface of the cake with aluminium foil and bake for a further 10 minutes. Remove from the oven, release from the tin, and leave to cool (about 45 minutes).
Melt 200 g dark chocolate couverture and coat the cake evenly. Draw fine grooves as a pattern using a fork. Allow the couverture to set, then cut the cake into portions and serve.
Schokoladenkuchen.tips: Pane rozzo — originally the term for a simple, rustic country loaf. The Parozzo chocolate cake, which was originally shaped like a loaf of bread, owes its name to it, but bears no resemblance to it in flavour.

Mandel-Feigen-Nougat.name: Almond and Fig Nougat
steps: Place a bowl over a hot water bath, add 200 g honey, and let it simmer over low heat, stirring frequently. To test for the right consistency (after about 1 hour): drop a little honey into a glass of cold water — if it immediately forms a small ball, the honey is ready.

Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 225°C. Spread the hazelnuts on a baking tray and toast briefly (gas mark 4). Remove and rub off the skins. Coarsely chop the hazelnuts and blanched almonds, and cut the dried figs into small cubes.

Break the dark chocolate into small pieces. In a saucepan, slowly heat 5 tbsp sugar with 5 tbsp water, stirring until the sugar has dissolved. Add the chocolate and melt it in the sugar syrup, stirring frequently.
In a second saucepan, cook the remaining sugar with 3 tbsp water into a thick syrup. Beat 3 egg whites to stiff peaks.
Once the honey has reached the right consistency, reduce the temperature of the water bath slightly and carefully fold the beaten egg whites into the honey.
Stir in the melted chocolate, sugar syrup, chopped nuts and almonds, and the fig cubes one after another.

Line a (marble) board with half the wafer sheets. Spread the nougat mixture 2–3 cm thick over them and cover with the remaining wafer sheets. Leave to cool completely. Then cut into small rectangles using a sharp knife.
Mandel-Feigen-Nougat.tips: While cooling, cover the top layer of wafers with a cloth and weigh it down with a newspaper to prevent it from curling upward. Variation: instead of spreading the nougat on wafers, use a spoon to portion it into small decorative chocolate moulds. In Sicily, torrone is often refined with toasted sesame seeds.

Pizza mit Mozzarella und Basilikum.name: Pizza with Mozzarella and Basil
steps: For the pizza dough, dissolve 10 g yeast with 1 pinch of sugar and 2 tbsp lukewarm water in a small bowl. Stir in 2 tbsp flour, cover, and leave the starter to rise in a warm place for 30 minutes.

Heap the remaining flour onto a pastry board and make a well in the centre. Add the starter from the small bowl, ¼ tsp salt, and knead everything with about 125 ml lukewarm water into a smooth dough. Knead vigorously, occasionally slapping the dough against the work surface. Shape into a round loaf, place in a bowl, cover with a cloth, and leave to rise in a warm place for at least 1 hour, until the dough has doubled in volume.

Preheat the oven to 225°C. Grease a round baking tray (28 cm diameter) with 1 tbsp olive oil. Knead the dough once more vigorously on a floured surface. Roll out lightly with a rolling pin, then transfer to the tray and press out evenly by hand, leaving the edge slightly thicker.

Drain the canned tomatoes, reserving the juice for another use. Slice the tomatoes, distribute over the pizza base, and crush with a fork, leaving a 2 cm border free around the edge. Slice the mozzarella thinly and place over the tomatoes. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper.

Bake the pizza on the lower rack in the preheated oven (gas mark 4) for 15 minutes. Remove, sprinkle with 2 tbsp grated Pecorino and basil leaves, and carefully drizzle the basil with oil. Bake on the middle rack for a further 10 minutes and serve hot.
Pizza mit Mozzarella und Basilikum.tips: Knead the dough very thoroughly, let it rise slowly, and bake it at a high temperature — ideally in a wood-fired oven. Serve the pizza immediately after baking, always fresh and piping hot.

Gefüllte Pizzataschen.name: Stuffed Pizza Turnovers
ingredients: 100 g raw ham, sliced (or Italian salami)
150 g ricotta (Italian fresh cheese; well-drained quark as a substitute)
steps: Mix 10 g yeast with 1 pinch of sugar and 2 tbsp lukewarm water until smooth, then stir in 2 tbsp flour. Cover and leave to stand for 30 minutes.

Heap the remaining flour on a pastry board and make a well in the centre. Add the starter, 2 tbsp olive oil, and ¼ tsp salt. Knead with about 125 ml lukewarm water into a supple dough. Knead vigorously, then shape into a round loaf, cover, and leave to rise in a warm place for at least 1 hour until doubled in volume.

Cut the ham or salami into fine strips and the mozzarella into small cubes. Press the ricotta through a sieve into a bowl. Stir in 2 eggs until smooth, then mix in the ham strips, mozzarella cubes, and 2 tbsp grated Parmesan. Season with salt (carefully!), pepper, and oregano.

Preheat the oven to 225°C and grease a baking tray with 1 tbsp oil. Knead the well-risen dough vigorously once more on a floured surface, divide in half, and roll each half into a circle about ½ cm thick. Brush each circle with 1 tbsp oil.

Spread the filling evenly over the dough circles, leaving the edges free. Fold each circle in half, pressing the edges firmly to seal. Brush the outside of each turnover with 1 tbsp olive oil and egg yolk, then place on the greased tray. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 4) for 20 minutes.
Gefüllte Pizzataschen.tips: Variation: before baking, brush the turnovers with a spicy tomato sauce and sprinkle with freshly grated Pecorino or Parmesan and oregano leaves. Always serve calzone very hot.

Fritierte Minipizzen.name: Deep-Fried Mini Pizzas
ingredients: 2 tbsp olive oil + oil for deep-frying
steps: Prepare the dough for the mini pizzas following the calzone recipe (page 248).

For the sauce: drain the tomatoes (reserving the juice) and chop them roughly. Finely chop 3 garlic cloves and 1 tbsp oregano. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil and sauté the garlic. Add the oregano and tomatoes, season with salt and pepper. Gradually pour in the reserved tomato juice and simmer over low heat until reduced.
Knead the well-risen dough, then roll out on a floured surface to about ½ cm thickness. Cut out circles approximately 8 cm in diameter.
Heat plenty of oil for deep-frying. Fry the dough rounds in batches until golden. Drain on paper towels and keep warm until all are cooked.
Cut half of the basil into fine strips and stir into the sauce, then adjust the seasoning. Serve alongside the pizzette and garnish with the remaining basil.

Ausgebackene Käsebrote.name: Fried Cheese Sandwiches
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh oregano (or ½ tbsp dried)
steps: Cut the mozzarella into 8 thin slices. Remove the crusts from the bread slices. Place 2 slices of cheese on each of 4 bread slices, leaving the edges clear. Season with salt, plenty of pepper, and oregano. Cover the topped slices with the remaining 4 bread slices, briefly dip the edges in cold water, and press firmly to seal.

Whisk the 2 eggs with 2 tbsp milk, salt, and pepper. Lightly dust both sides of the sandwiches with 2 tbsp flour and place them in a deep dish. Pour the egg mixture over them and leave to soak until all the liquid is absorbed, turning the sandwiches once halfway through.
Heat a generous amount of olive oil in a frying pan. Add the sandwiches and fry on both sides until golden and crispy. Drain well on paper towels and serve immediately.
Ausgebackene Käsebrote.tips: A similar specialty from Roman cuisine is Pandorato (recipe on page 178).

Geröstetes Brot mit Sardellen.name: Toasted Bread with Anchovies
ingredients: 2 tbsp oil for the baking sheet
steps: Wash the tomatoes, slice them crosswise, then cut into strips, removing the stem end. Cut the mozzarella into 8 slices. Briefly rinse the anchovy fillets, pat dry with paper towels, and halve lengthwise.
Preheat the oven to 175°C and grease a baking sheet with 2 tbsp oil. Halve the 4 bread slices and spread with 50g butter.

Top each piece of bread with 1 slice of mozzarella, a few tomato strips, and 1–2 anchovy strips. Sprinkle with 1 tbsp oregano, season with pepper, and add a little salt if needed (depending on how salty the anchovies are). Place on the greased baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for 10 minutes. Serve hot.
Geröstetes Brot mit Sardellen.tips: An ideal accompaniment for aperitif hour — such as a dry martini on the rocks, Campari soda, or Aperol with lemon.

Mozzarella mit Tomaten.name: Mozzarella with Tomatoes
steps: Wash the tomatoes, slice them crosswise, and remove the stem end from each slice. Slice the mozzarella balls as well.

Arrange the tomato and mozzarella slices alternately, overlapping like roof tiles, on two large plates. Season lightly with salt and coarsely ground pepper, then drizzle with 8 tbsp olive oil. Serve scattered with fresh basil leaves.
Mozzarella mit Tomaten.tips: Together they are irresistible: mozzarella and tomato, the two round-shaped stars of Campanian cuisine.

Makkaroni mit neapolitanischer Fleischsa.name: Macaroni with Neapolitan Meat Sauce
ingredients: 1 kg fattened ox or braising beef
steps: Tie the beef into shape with kitchen twine. Heat 60g lard in a braising pot, add the meat, and brown it on all sides.

Thinly slice 1 carrot, 2 celery stalks, and 2 onions; finely chop 2 cloves of garlic. Add everything to the pot one after the other, stirring and sautéing briefly. Season the vegetables and meat generously with salt and pepper.

Gradually pour in ½ litre of white wine in small amounts, letting it reduce by half each time before adding more. Stir in 4 tbsp tomato paste and add about ½ litre of water (the meat should be just barely covered).

Place the lid half on and let the meat braise over the lowest heat for at least 3 hours, allowing the liquid to come to a brief simmer only occasionally. Toward the end of the cooking time, bring 3 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Break 500g of macaroni into roughly 5 cm pieces, add to the boiling water, and cook until al dente (about 6 minutes). Grease a large ovenproof dish with 1 tbsp oil and preheat the oven to 200°C.
Slice the caciocavallo or mozzarella. Pick off the basil leaves, tearing any large ones. Drain the macaroni and toss with 2 tbsp oil. Spread a layer of pasta in the prepared dish.

Remove the finished roast from the sauce, keep it warm, and serve as a main course if desired. Bring the sauce to a vigorous boil and adjust the seasoning — it should be richly dark, thick, and well seasoned. If needed, crush the vegetables in the sauce with a fork.

Spread a generous layer of meat sauce over the pasta, then top with cheese slices and basil. Add another layer of macaroni, then cover with sauce, cheese, and basil. Continue layering in this order until all the ingredients are used up, setting aside a few basil leaves.

Finish with a layer of meat sauce, dot with 2 tbsp butter, and place the dish in the preheated oven (gas mark 3). Bake for about 10 minutes, scatter with the reserved fresh basil, and serve piping hot.
Makkaroni mit neapolitanischer Fleischsa.tips: The roast can be served afterwards as a warm main course alongside some of the vegetables. Alternatively, let the tender, melt-in-the-mouth meat cool, cut it into fine strips, and toss it with bold ingredients such as capers, onions, bell peppers, and anchovies to make a hearty salad.

Nudeln mit Tomatensauce (Kampanien).name: Pasta with Tomato Sauce (Campania)
ingredients: 100g ricotta (Italian fresh cheese; cottage cheese or quark as a substitute)
steps: Cut the bacon into small cubes. Very finely chop 1 celery stalk, 1 carrot, 1 onion, and 1 clove of garlic. Combine everything in a pot, heat gently, and sauté over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Pour in ⅛ litre of white wine and bring to a boil. Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed them. Chop the flesh and add to the pot. Season with salt and pepper and simmer into a thick sauce.
Meanwhile, bring 3 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Add 300g fusilli and cook until al dente, 8–10 minutes. Crumble the ricotta.

Drain the cooked fusilli and immediately mix with the sauce, adding a few tablespoons of pasta water if needed. Stir in 1 tbsp grated pecorino, the ricotta, and the oregano leaves. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve the remaining pecorino on the side.

Spaghetti mit Muscheln (Kampanien).name: Spaghetti with Clams (Campania)
ingredients: 1 kg fresh clams (or 400g canned clam meat)
steps: Scrub the fresh clams thoroughly under running water and discard any that are already open. Place the closed ones in a large pot, pour in ¼ litre of white wine, and bring to a boil.

Cook for about 5 minutes until the shells open. Discard any that remain closed, remove the rest from the pot, and let them cool slightly. Strain the cooking liquid through a fine sieve and reserve it (for canned clams, use the liquid from the can).

Very finely chop 1 bunch of parsley, 1 onion, and 3 cloves of garlic. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a pot and sauté the mixture, stirring. Reduce the clam liquid by half (for canned clams, add the can liquid and ⅛ litre of wine).
Bring 4 litres of salted water to a boil, add 400g spaghetti, and cook until al dente (8–10 minutes).

Remove the clam meat from the shells and warm it through in the sauce without letting it boil again. Stir in 2 tbsp butter in small pieces and season with about 2 tbsp lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Finely chop the remaining parsley. Crush 8 peppercorns in a mortar.
Drain the spaghetti, toss with 2 tbsp olive oil and the clam sauce. Divide among warmed plates, sprinkle with parsley and crushed pepper, and serve.
Spaghetti mit Muscheln (Kampanien).tips: For a more striking presentation (and less effort), serve the clams in their shells.

Spaghetti mit Oliven-Kapern-Sauce (Kampa.name: Spaghetti with Olive and Caper Sauce (Campania)
ingredients: 500g ripe tomatoes (or canned / carton crushed tomatoes)
steps: Briefly blanch the tomatoes in boiling water, refresh in cold water, and peel. Deseed the flesh and roughly chop.

Finely chop 100g pitted olives and 3 tbsp capers. Rinse the anchovy fillets, pat dry, chop finely, and mash to a paste with a fork. Slit open the chilli, deseed it, and cut into thin rings (wash your hands thoroughly immediately afterwards). Finely chop 3 cloves of garlic.
Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a pot. Sauté the garlic and chilli, stirring. Stir in the tomato flesh, tomato paste, and mashed anchovies. Simmer uncovered over low heat for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring 4 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Add 400g spaghetti and cook until al dente, 8–10 minutes. Very finely chop ½ bunch of parsley.

Stir the capers and olives into the sauce and season with salt and pepper. Drain the spaghetti and immediately toss with the sauce. Scatter over the parsley and serve with freshly grated Parmesan on the side if desired.
Spaghetti mit Oliven-Kapern-Sauce (Kampa.tips: Use salt sparingly, as the anchovies, capers, olives, and tomato paste are all already quite salty.

Ausgebackene Fische und Krustentiere.name: Fried Mixed Seafood
ingredients: 500g fresh anchovies or very small sardines (or frozen)
steps: Thoroughly wash and clean the squid: peel off the skin, pull the tentacles and innards out of the body and cut them off just behind the head (keep the tentacles, discard the innards and heads). For larger squid, cut the body into strips.
Scale the red mullet and cut off the heads and fins. Rinse the fish briefly under running water, pat dry with paper towels, and sprinkle with salt.

Lightly scale and gut the anchovies or small sardines, cutting off all fins except the tail fin (for very small anchovies, the head can be left on). Wash the fish thoroughly and pat dry with paper towels. Sprinkle with salt.

Prepare the prawns with their shells and tails on, but remove the heads and pull out the digestive tract (alternatively, peel the prawns completely and dust with flour before frying). Preheat the oven to 150°C.

Heat a generous amount of olive oil in a deep-fryer or heavy pot and add 2 peeled garlic cloves. To test the temperature, dip a wooden skewer into the oil — when small bubbles rise around it, the oil is ready. Remove the garlic before frying.
Dust the squid very lightly with flour, add to the hot oil in batches, and fry until crispy. Drain on paper towels and keep warm in the oven (gas mark 1).

Lightly dust the red mullet and anchovies or sardines with flour as well, then fry in the hot oil one batch at a time until crispy (mullet 3–4 minutes, anchovies 2 minutes). Lift out the fried fish with a slotted spoon, drain on paper towels, and keep warm alongside the squid. Add the unpeeled prawns directly to the hot oil and fry until crispy (or dust the peeled prawns with flour and fry). Drain well on paper towels.
Line a large platter with firm white napkins. Arrange all the fried, hot seafood decoratively on top. Sprinkle with salt, add lemon wedges, and serve immediately.
Ausgebackene Fische und Krustentiere.tips: Especially important with seafood: start with the best, freshest ingredients you can find, prepare them carefully, and serve them piping hot!

Tintenfisch in Tomatensauce.name: Octopus in Tomato Sauce
steps: Thoroughly wash fresh octopus and peel off the outer skin. Pull the tentacles and innards out of the body, cut off just behind the head (keep the tentacles, discard the rest). Turn the body pouch inside out, rinse, and cut into strips. Pat everything dry with paper towels. If using frozen octopus, thaw in advance, cut into pieces, rinse briefly, and pat dry with paper towels.
Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water, peel, and halve. Remove the seeds with a spoon and roughly chop the flesh.

Finely chop 2 onions and 4 garlic cloves. Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot. Add the octopus pieces and sauté over high heat, stirring. Season with salt and pepper, stir in the onions and cook until translucent. Drizzle with about 2 tbsp lemon juice.
Stir in the chopped garlic and tomatoes. Bring briefly to a boil, then pour in ⅛ l white wine. Cover and braise over the lowest possible heat for 45 minutes.

Finely chop 50g green olives and the parsley. Once the octopus is cooked and tender (test with a skewer!), uncover the pot and reduce the sauce briefly over higher heat if desired. Stir in the chopped olives and parsley, and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Tintenfisch in Tomatensauce.tips: When braised with a whole chili pepper, this dish is also known as Polpi alla luciana. Octopus with its sturdy tentacles is most commonly used here — it needs to braise a little longer than the more delicate squid varieties such as cuttlefish or calamari.

Marinierte Kräutermakrelen.name: Marinated Herb Mackerel
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C. Scale, gut, and thoroughly wash the mackerel. Place in an ovenproof dish, season with salt, and scatter over ½ tsp peppercorns and 2 bay leaves. Drizzle with 3–4 tbsp lemon juice and add enough water to just cover the fish.

Cook in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for 10–15 minutes. Remove the fish from the cooking liquid and let drain. While still warm, cut along both sides lengthways and peel off the skin. Allow to cool slightly, then lift off the fillets. Prepare a shallow, attractive serving dish.
Cut the mint into thin strips; finely chop the parsley and 2 garlic cloves. Mix the herbs and garlic with ⅛ l white wine vinegar, then stir in 4 tbsp olive oil. Season well with salt and pepper.

Lay some mackerel fillets side by side in the dish and brush with some of the herb marinade. Arrange the remaining fillets on top and drizzle with the rest of the marinade. Cover the dish and leave the mackerel to marinate in the refrigerator for at least 1 day.
Before serving, garnish with fresh herb leaves.
Marinierte Kräutermakrelen.tips: An affordable fish with a bold, distinctive flavour. The juicy mackerel fillets can easily break apart when lifted from the bones — especially while the fish is still warm. This minor imperfection does nothing to detract from the delicious taste.

Geschmorte Rindersteaks (Kampanien).name: Braised Beef Steaks (Campania)
steps: Season the steaks with freshly ground pepper on both sides, drizzle with 2 tbsp lemon juice, cover, and set aside.
Cut the ham into thin strips. Clean the mushrooms and slice thinly. Finely chop ½ bunch of parsley. Preheat the oven to 200°C.

Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan, add the ham, and sauté for 2 minutes. Stir in the mushrooms and parsley and continue to cook gently for 2 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and 1–2 tbsp lemon juice. Transfer to a wide, ovenproof earthenware dish.

Lay the marinated steaks side by side on top of the mushroom mixture. Braise in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 20 minutes. After 10 minutes, turn the meat, season with salt and pepper, and drizzle with 2 tbsp oil and 1 tbsp lemon juice.
To serve, place the steaks on plates and top with the mushrooms and ham. Scatter with fresh parsley leaves.

Gefülltes Hähnchen (Basilicata).name: Stuffed Chicken (Basilicata)
steps: Cut both the streaky and fatty bacon into small cubes. Place in a frying pan, heat, and render out the fat, frying the cracklings until crispy, then remove. Add the chicken livers to the hot bacon fat and briefly sear on both sides. Season with salt and pepper, remove from the pan, and leave to cool slightly. Dice the livers and mix in a bowl with the cracklings. Reserve the bacon fat in the pan.

Beat 2 eggs and pour over the liver mixture. Finely chop the parsley and stir in. Add 50g freshly grated Pecorino and mix everything thoroughly. Season with salt (carefully, given the saltiness of the bacon and cheese) and pepper.

Preheat the oven to 225°C. Rinse the chicken inside and out, then pat dry with paper towels. Season with salt and pepper, fill with the liver mixture, and close the opening (sew with kitchen twine or secure with toothpicks). Truss the wings and legs close to the body and tie the rosemary sprig on the outside (if using dried rosemary, add it to the stuffing instead).

Reheat the bacon fat in the pan, take out 1 tbsp of it and use it to grease a large roasting pan. Place the stuffed chicken inside and brush all over with the remaining bacon fat. Roast in the preheated oven (gas mark 4) for about 1 hour 15 minutes until crispy. Turn once or twice during roasting, basting repeatedly with the released juices and fat.
Gefülltes Hähnchen (Basilicata).tips: Sautéed potato slices with onions and mushrooms make a great accompaniment — or a punchy, refreshing cauliflower salad.

Schweinekoteletts mit Tomaten und Knobla.name: Pork Chops with Tomatoes and Garlic (Campania)
steps: Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed. Roughly chop the flesh. Thinly slice 3–4 garlic cloves.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan with a lid. Add the chops and sear well on both sides. Remove the meat, season with salt and pepper, and set aside covered on a plate.

Pour off most of the cooking fat, leaving just a thin film. Add 1 tbsp fresh olive oil and heat. Gently sauté the garlic slices, scatter in half the oregano, and stir in the chopped tomatoes. Pour in ⅛ l red wine, bring to a vigorous boil, and stir until the sauce thickens slightly. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

Return the chops to the sauce, cover, and braise over the lowest heat for about 15 minutes, turning the meat once. Remove the chops and arrange on warmed plates. Spoon the tomato sauce over the top, sprinkle with oregano, and serve.
Schweinekoteletts mit Tomaten und Knobla.tips: Alla pizzaiola refers to the method of cooking meat or fish in a tomato sauce. Instead of pork chops, you can just as easily use escalopes, beef steaks, or fish fillets. While the chops rest after searing, flavourful meat juices will collect on the plate — be sure to add these to the tomato sauce.

Auberginenauflauf (Kampanien).name: Eggplant Bake (Campania)
ingredients: Olive oil for frying and greasing the dish
steps: Wash the eggplants, remove the stems, and slice lengthways into ½ cm thick slices. Sprinkle with salt, place on a plate, cover with a second plate, and weigh down. Leave for at least 1 hour to draw out the bitter juices.

For the sauce: blanch the tomatoes, peel, and deseed. Finely chop the flesh and heat in a saucepan. Cut one-third of the basil into strips and press the garlic; add both to the pan. Simmer into a thick sauce, seasoning with salt and pepper. Stir occasionally.

Meanwhile, briefly rinse the eggplant slices under cold running water, drain, and pat thoroughly dry with a kitchen towel. Heat a generous amount of olive oil in a frying pan, add the slices in batches, and fry until golden. Drain well on paper towels.
Grease an ovenproof dish. Cut the remaining basil into strips; slice the mozzarella and the 2 hard-boiled eggs. Preheat the oven to 175°C.

Line the dish with a layer of eggplant slices. Sprinkle with some Parmesan. Add a few egg and mozzarella slices, cover with tomato sauce, and scatter over some basil. Cover with eggplant slices and continue layering all the ingredients in this order (reserving 2 tbsp Parmesan for the top). Finish with a layer of tomato sauce.

Place the eggplant bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) and bake for about 40 minutes. Scatter the remaining Parmesan over the top 10 minutes before the end of cooking time. Do not serve piping hot straight from the oven — lukewarm is best (it is also delicious cold).
Auberginenauflauf (Kampanien).tips: If you cannot find fresh Parmesan at a cheese counter, use Pecorino or another strong, well-aged hard cheese instead. Around Naples, this dish is sometimes given an intriguing edge with a little dark chocolate.

Blumenkohlsalat.name: Cauliflower Salad
steps: Trim and wash the cauliflower, then break into florets. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the florets for 5 minutes. Drain in a colander, refresh under cold water, and leave to drain.
