Wash the bell pepper, halve it, and remove the seeds and membranes. Cut the halves into very thin strips or small dice. Halve the 50g each of green and black pitted olives.
Rinse the 4 anchovy fillets well, pat dry, and cut into thin strips. Deseed the 4 pepperoncini and cut into rings or strips.

Place the cooled cauliflower in a bowl and mix with the prepared ingredients and 1 tbsp capers. Season with salt and pepper, pour over 3–4 tbsp wine vinegar and 6 tbsp olive oil. Toss everything together thoroughly and adjust seasoning.
Blumenkohlsalat.tips: A pinch of chili powder or cayenne pepper gives the florets an extra kick.

Eingelegte Zucchinischeiben.name: Marinated Zucchini Slices
steps: Wash and trim the zucchini. Cut into ½ cm thick slices, sprinkle with salt, and leave briefly to release moisture. Pat thoroughly dry.

Heat a generous amount of olive oil in a pot. Add the zucchini slices in batches and fry until lightly golden. Remove and drain well on paper towels. Spread the zucchini slices out on a large platter and season with salt and freshly ground pepper.

Cut ½ bunch each of mint and basil into thin strips. Finely chop ½ bunch of parsley and 3 garlic cloves. Mix the herbs and garlic together; stir half of the mixture into 100ml wine vinegar and drizzle over the zucchini slices. Scatter the remaining herbs on top, cover, and leave to marinate in the refrigerator for 3–4 hours.
Eingelegte Zucchinischeiben.tips: Serve the marinated zucchini slices cool but not too cold — ideally take them out of the refrigerator about 15 minutes before serving.

Kartoffel-Auberginen-Topf.name: Potato and Aubergine Stew
steps: Trim and wash the aubergines, then cut into 2cm cubes. Sprinkle with salt and leave to stand for about 30 minutes.

Meanwhile, trim the bell peppers and cut into small pieces. Pour boiling water over the tomatoes, then peel and deseed them. Roughly chop the flesh. Wash, peel and dice the potatoes. Finely chop the 2 onions and 3 garlic cloves.
Briefly rinse the aubergines and pat dry with kitchen paper. Preheat the oven to 200°C. Heat 6 tbsp olive oil in a large oven-safe pot.

Stir in the potato pieces and brown them all over. Add the chopped onions, then gradually stir in the aubergine and pepper pieces, frying while stirring. Season generously with 1 tbsp oregano, the chopped garlic, salt and pepper. Add the tomato flesh to the pot, mix everything thoroughly and cover with a lid.
Cook in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for 45 minutes. Finely chop the parsley and stir into the vegetables just before serving. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Kartoffel-Auberginen-Topf.tips: Ideal not only as a side dish with lamb, pork or beef, but also as a hearty meat-free main course (3–4 portions). Reminiscent of French ratatouille. Instead of using the oven, you can also braise the vegetables on the stovetop. Feel free to add green olives, courgette or celery, and scatter fresh basil leaves over the dish before serving.

Süßer Weizen.name: Sweet Wheat
steps: Soak 100g wheat berries in water overnight. The next day, drain, cover with fresh water and simmer for about 30 minutes until tender. Drain, transfer to a bowl and leave to cool slightly.
Finely chop 2 tbsp walnuts, toast briefly in a dry frying pan and stir into the wheat.

Stir 125ml white wine and 2 tbsp honey into the wheat until you have a smooth, creamy mixture. Halve the pomegranate crosswise (caution: the juice contains a stubborn dye). Use a spoon to scoop out the seeds and carefully fold them into the wheat. Finely grate the zest of 1/2 lemon and stir it in.
Spoon into small bowls and refrigerate. Just before serving, garnish with 50g roughly chopped dark chocolate.
Süßer Weizen.tips: An unusual and wonderfully flavoursome dessert whose Arabic origins are immediately apparent. A similar dessert made from cooked wheat, honey, nuts, chocolate and cinnamon is also prepared in Sicily (Cuccia) and Apulia (Grane cuotte), traditionally served during the Christmas season.

Gefrorene Kaffeecreme.name: Frozen Coffee Cream
steps: Dissolve 4 tbsp sugar in 250ml freshly brewed hot coffee, then leave to cool. In a small saucepan, gently warm 250ml milk. Split the vanilla pod lengthways, scrape out the seeds and add both the seeds and pod to the milk. Leave to infuse without boiling.
Place a round-sided bowl over a warm bain-marie. Add 4 egg yolks and 4 tbsp sugar and whisk until pale and foamy (or use a double boiler).

Remove the vanilla pod from the warm milk. Gradually pour first the cooled coffee, then the milk into the egg foam, stirring vigorously throughout. Remove the creamy mixture from the heat, set over a cold bain-marie and stir until cooled.

Whip 250g cream to stiff peaks. Fold three-quarters of it into the coffee cream. Cover the remaining cream and refrigerate for later use as a garnish. Pour the coffee cream into metal cups and freeze for approximately 3–4 hours until firm.
Remove the coffee cream from the freezer 20 minutes before serving. Fill the remaining cream into a piping bag and pipe small rosettes onto each cream. Garnish with coffee beans.
Gefrorene Kaffeecreme.tips: Variation: Granita di caffè – a refreshing, ice-cold speciality made from coffee and sugar. It is crushed or blended before serving and topped with a dollop of whipped cream.

Biskuit mit Vanillecreme.name: Sponge Cake with Vanilla Cream
ingredients: 1 ready-made flat sponge cake base (see recipe on page 160)
500g ricotta (Italian fresh cheese; well-drained quark may be used as a substitute)
steps: In a saucepan, slowly heat 125ml milk with 50g sugar, without bringing it to a boil. Split the vanilla pod lengthways, scrape out the seeds and add both seeds and pod to the milk. Leave to infuse for 10 minutes, then remove from the heat and leave to cool slightly.

In a bowl, beat 2 eggs with 1 egg yolk and 100g sugar until pale and fluffy. Remove the vanilla pod from the cooled milk, then gradually pour the milk into the egg mixture, stirring to form a smooth cream.
Press the ricotta through a fine sieve and fold it in. Grate 50g chocolate and stir it into the cream. Flavour with 2 cl Amaretto.

Cut the sponge into strips about 5cm wide. Line the base of a rectangular ovenproof dish with one third of the sponge strips. Soak with 2 cl rum, then spread one third of the cream over the top. Layer the remaining ingredients in the same way, creating three layers in total.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the flavours to meld.

Shortly before serving, preheat the oven to 250°C. Beat the remaining egg white to stiff peaks. Spoon into a piping bag fitted with a plain nozzle and pipe the meringue over the surface of the dessert. Place in the very hot oven (gas mark 5) for just a few minutes until the peaks turn light golden brown. Serve immediately.
Biskuit mit Vanillecreme.tips: The version from Emilia-Romagna is not finished with meringue. Instead, slices of Bundt cake are soaked in liqueur and layered alternately with vanilla cream and chocolate cream in a bowl.

Fritierte Reiskugeln.name: Fried Rice Balls
ingredients: 3 tbsp olive oil, plus extra olive oil (or clarified butter) for frying
Bay leaves (fresh if possible) to garnish
steps: Finely chop 1 onion and 1/2 bunch of parsley. Cut the turkey breast and 1 celery stalk into very small cubes. Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a pot and melt 1 tbsp butter in it. Gently fry the onion until translucent. Add the turkey and minced beef in batches and brown well. Stir in the celery and parsley and fry briefly. Season with salt, pepper and a pinch of chilli powder.
Mix 2 tbsp tomato paste with 200ml hot beef stock and pour it in. Add 50g peas, cover the pot and braise the ragù over the lowest heat.

Bring 500ml salted water to a boil in a pot. Add 300g rice and cook over low heat, stirring frequently, until the liquid is absorbed. Add a little more water if needed. Near the end of the cooking time, dissolve the contents of 1 saffron sachet in 2 tbsp hot water and stir it in. The finished rice should be nicely separate and not too soft. Stir 2 tbsp butter and 50g freshly grated Pecorino into the hot rice. Leave to cool slightly, then mix in 1 egg.

Chop the remaining parsley, and cut 1/2 bunch of basil into fine strips. If necessary, let the ragù reduce a little in the uncovered pot. Remove from the heat, stir in the herbs and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Fritierte Reiskugeln.tips: Make double the amount of meat ragù and serve whatever is left over from the filling as a hearty sauce alongside the crispy rice balls.

Gebratene Kräuter-Oliven.name: Pan-Fried Herb Olives
ingredients: 1 sprig fresh oregano (or 1–2 tsp dried oregano leaves)
steps: Briefly rinse the olives, drain and pat dry. Slice 3 garlic cloves thinly.
In a frying pan, gently heat 3 tbsp olive oil with the garlic slices. Stir in the olives and fry gently, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes. Scatter in half of the oregano leaves.
Pour in 6 tbsp white wine vinegar and 2–3 tbsp water, bring to a vigorous boil and let the liquid reduce by half.
Stir in the remaining oregano and season with freshly ground black pepper. Serve the olives hot straight from the pan.
Gebratene Kräuter-Oliven.tips: Serve with crusty white bread to mop up the delicious sauce. There are many recipes from Calabria for home-marinated olives.

Orangensalat.name: Orange Salad
steps: Thickly peel the 2 oranges all the way round, removing the white pith as well. Catch the juice in a small bowl. Cut the oranges into 1/2 cm thick slices, removing any seeds. Arrange the orange slices on a large platter.
Trim the fennel bulb and remove the tough outer layers. Set aside the feathery fennel fronds for garnish. Cut the tender fennel heart into small cubes. Slice 2 onions into very thin rings.
Whisk the collected orange juice with 1 tbsp white wine vinegar and 4 tbsp olive oil. Finely chop 1 tsp rosemary and stir it in. Season with salt and pepper.

Toss the fennel cubes thoroughly in the dressing, then lift them out and distribute over the orange slices. Scatter the onion rings on top and drizzle the remaining dressing over the salad. Add a few generous grinds of black pepper and garnish with the fennel fronds.
Orangensalat.tips: You can omit the fennel and instead use more mild onion rings over the oranges, garnishing with black olives if you like.

Meeresfrüchte-Salat.name: Seafood Salad
steps: Prepare the squid: wash thoroughly under cold running water and peel off the thin skin. Pull the head and innards outward and cut them off.

Bring 1 litre of water to a boil in a pot with the juice of 1/2 lemon and 1/2 tsp salt. Add the cleaned squid, cover and simmer for about 30 minutes until tender (test with a skewer). Drain and leave to cool. Chop the tentacles roughly and cut the squid body into thin rings. Combine with the prawns in a bowl.

For the marinade, deseed the chilli peppers and cut into thin rings. Chop 2 garlic cloves. Whisk 2–3 tbsp lemon juice vigorously with 5 tbsp olive oil. Stir in the chilli rings and garlic, and season with salt, pepper and oregano.
Pour the marinade over the squid and prawns, mix well and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to marinate.

Remove from the refrigerator about 15 minutes before serving. Very finely chop the parsley and stir it in. Taste again and adjust seasoning with salt, pepper and lemon juice if needed. Garnish with lemon wedges.
Meeresfrüchte-Salat.tips: Another way to make squid salad: fry cleaned, sliced squid in good olive oil, season with garlic, oregano, chilli powder, salt and pepper, then pour in white wine and braise until tender.

Gefüllte Nudelröllchen.name: Stuffed Pasta Rolls
ingredients: 150g all-purpose flour, plus extra for rolling
500g braised beef with a rich sauce (e.g. Brasato alla milanese)
steps: Pasta dough: Mix 150g flour with 150g semolina, mound it on a work surface and make a well in the centre. Add 2 eggs and 1/2 tsp salt, then knead with about 100ml lukewarm water into a smooth pasta dough. Cover with a cloth and leave to rest for 20 minutes.

Roll out the pasta dough on a floured surface to about 2mm thickness. Cut into squares of roughly 10cm per side. Bring 3 litres of salted water with 2–3 tbsp oil to a boil in a large pot. Add the pasta sheets in batches and par-cook for 5 minutes. Lift out with a slotted spoon, briefly dip in cold water, drain well and lay on a clean kitchen towel.

Pass the braised beef through a meat grinder or cut into very small cubes. Combine with half the braising sauce in a saucepan and simmer vigorously until you have a thick, hearty ragù. Season well with salt, pepper and freshly grated nutmeg.
Gefüllte Nudelröllchen.tips: For a quicker version, use ready-made dried cannelloni from a packet. The filling can also be made with ground meat, ricotta and fresh spinach, or with tomato sauce and diced mozzarella. The filled pasta rolls are often spread with a generous layer of béchamel sauce before being baked.

Makkaroni mit Schinken.name: Macaroni with Ham
steps: Blanch, peel and deseed the tomatoes. Chop the flesh. Cut the ham into strips, finely chop 1 onion and 2–3 garlic cloves.

Heat 2 tbsp olive oil and 1 tbsp lard. Sauté the onion and chilli until softened. Stir in the garlic and ham, briefly fry, then mix in the tomatoes. Simmer the sauce for about 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Bring 3 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Break 400g macaroni into pieces and cook until al dente, about 8 minutes.

Grate the Caciocavallo, finely chop the parsley, and cut 1/2 bunch of basil into strips. Season the tomato sauce with salt and pepper, remove the chilli. Stir in 3/4 of the parsley. Crush 1/2 tsp peppercorns in a mortar.
Drain the macaroni and toss with 2 tbsp lard and the crushed pepper. Mix in the cheese and tomato sauce. Scatter the finely cut herbs on top.

Nudeln mit Sardinen und Fenchel.name: Pasta with Sardines and Fennel
ingredients: 1 fennel bulb (about 200g) with plenty of fronds
steps: Gut, bone and behead the sardines. Rinse thoroughly and pat dry. Season lightly with salt, drizzle with the juice of 1 lemon and refrigerate.

Soak 50g sultanas in water. Trim the fennel, removing any tough parts. Set the tender fronds aside. Bring 1 litre of salted water to a boil, cook the fennel for 10 minutes. Drain, reserving the cooking water. Dice the fennel.

Very finely chop 1/2 bunch of parsley. Rinse 4 anchovy fillets, pat dry and finely chop. Mince 3 garlic cloves. Grind these 3 ingredients in a mortar with 4 tbsp olive oil, the zest of 1/2 lemon and 1 tsp fennel seeds to form a paste.

Finely chop the onion. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a pan and gently sauté the onion until translucent. Stir in the fennel and briefly fry. Lower the heat and mix in the anchovy paste, the sultanas with their soaking liquid, a few tablespoons of the fennel cooking water and 2 tbsp pine nuts. Simmer gently.
Top up the fennel water to 3 litres, salt and bring to a boil. Cook 400g pasta until al dente, about 10 minutes. Drain well and toss with 2 tbsp olive oil.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a second pan. Pat the sardines dry with a kitchen towel and fry for 2 minutes on each side; season with salt and pepper. Roughly chop the remaining 1/2 bunch of parsley and the fennel fronds.

Layer the pasta into a warmed dish, seasoning with salt and pepper. Spoon some of the anchovy sauce over each layer and scatter a few fried sardines on top. Sprinkle with parsley and fennel fronds, then drizzle with 2–3 tbsp olive oil.

Nudeln mit Gemüse und Kapern.name: Pasta with Vegetables and Capers
steps: Cut the aubergine into 1cm cubes. Place in a colander, sprinkle with salt and leave to draw out the moisture.

Halve the bell peppers and remove the seeds and membranes. Roast the pepper halves under the grill or in a hot oven (225°C / Gas Mark 4) until the skin blisters and darkens. Remove from the heat, cover with a damp kitchen towel, then peel off the skin. Leave to cool slightly and cut into small pieces.
Blanch, peel and deseed the tomatoes; cut the flesh into small cubes. Rinse 4 anchovy fillets, pat dry and finely chop. Chop 50g pitted olives. Thinly slice 2 garlic cloves.

Briefly rinse the aubergine cubes and dry them thoroughly. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a wide pan. Fry the aubergine and garlic, stirring, until lightly browned. Mix in the chopped anchovies and tomatoes, season with a little salt and pepper. Cover and simmer for about 15 minutes. Grate 50g Caciocavallo.
Bring 3–4 litres of salted water to a boil in a large pot. Cook 400g fusilli until al dente, about 8–10 minutes.
Meanwhile, stir the pepper pieces, olives and capers into the sauce and cook uncovered until the fusilli are ready. Drain the pasta and toss with 2 tbsp olive oil.
Adjust the sauce seasoning with salt and pepper. Toss with the hot fusilli in a warmed bowl. Scatter over basil leaves and serve the grated cheese alongside.
Nudeln mit Gemüse und Kapern.tips: Fusilli is a spiral-shaped pasta also popular in Calabria. In Sicily this dish is made with wild mountain fennel, and it is sometimes mixed with tomatoes and briefly baked in the oven.

Couscous mit Fisch.name: Couscous with Fish
ingredients: 1kg mixed fish (e.g. sea bream, mullet, sardines, haddock)
steps: For the fish broth, gut, wash and fillet all the fish, or cut into portions; scale first where necessary (e.g. sea bream and haddock; sardines need only light scaling). Refrigerate the fillets for later. Use only the fish trimmings (heads, fins, bones) for the broth.

Blanch, peel and deseed the tomatoes; finely chop the flesh. Finely chop 2 celery stalks, 1 onion, 3 garlic cloves and 1/2 bunch of parsley. Heat 5 tbsp olive oil in a large pot and gently sauté the celery, onion, garlic and parsley.

Stir in the fish trimmings, tomatoes and 1 bay leaf; season generously with salt and pepper. Pour in 1.5 litres of water, cover and simmer for 30 minutes. Strain the broth and return about 1 litre to the pot. Pour the remaining broth into a deep frying pan for cooking the fish fillets.

Dissolve the saffron powder in a little broth and use it to moisten the 300g couscous. Bring the broth in the large pot to a boil. Tip the couscous into a sieve, suspend it over the boiling fish broth and close the pot tightly. Steam for about 10 minutes, fluffing with a fork from time to time.
Couscous mit Fisch.tips: Keep loosening the couscous with a fork throughout cooking; at the end, crumble it finely with your fingers. Oriental tales season this chapter of Sicilian culinary art — the traces of an Arab past taste of cinnamon and nutmeg, of cloves and saffron.

Seehecht mit Rosmarin.name: Hake with Rosemary
steps: Ask your fishmonger to prepare the hake oven-ready (scaled, gutted and gills removed). Before cooking, rinse the fish briefly under cold running water and pat dry with kitchen paper. Season inside and out with salt and pepper.
Grease a shallow ovenproof dish with 2 tbsp olive oil and place 2 rosemary sprigs in it. Preheat the oven to 175°C.

Rinse 6 anchovy fillets thoroughly, pat dry and finely chop. Gently warm them in a small saucepan with 2–3 tbsp olive oil, mash with a fork and stir into a smooth cream. Finely chop 2 garlic cloves and mix in. Season to taste with 2–3 tbsp lemon juice.

Spread the inside of the hake with some of the anchovy cream. Place the fish in the dish on top of the rosemary sprigs. Drizzle the remaining cream over the top. Scatter over 4 tbsp breadcrumbs and drizzle with the remaining olive oil. Roast in the preheated oven (Gas Mark 2) for just under 40 minutes. Serve garnished with lemon wedges.

Geschmorter Thunfisch.name: Braised Tuna
ingredients: 1kg ripe tomatoes (or 1 large can peeled tomatoes, 800g)
steps: Skin the tuna steaks, rinse briefly and pat dry with kitchen paper. Press 2 garlic cloves, rub over the 4 fish steaks and season with salt and pepper. Preheat the oven to 175°C.

Blanch, peel and deseed the fresh tomatoes (if using canned, drain well and reserve the liquid for another use). Finely chop the tomato flesh. Finely chop 50g pitted black olives. Finely chop 2 tbsp capers and 1 onion; cut the basil and mint into strips.

Grease an ovenproof dish with 4 tbsp olive oil. Arrange the tuna steaks side by side in the dish and drizzle with 125ml white wine. Scatter the chopped tomatoes, onion, olives, capers and half the herbs over the top; drizzle with the remaining 2 tbsp olive oil. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Bake on the middle shelf of the preheated oven (Gas Mark 2) for 20–30 minutes. Scatter over fresh herbs and serve.

Gegrillte Schwertfischrouladen.name: Grilled Swordfish Rolls
ingredients: For the sauce: 2 lemons (7–8 tbsp juice)
steps: Skin the swordfish slices, rinse briefly and pat dry. Sprinkle lightly with salt and refrigerate.

Finely chop 1 onion, 2 garlic cloves, 1 tbsp capers, 1/2 bunch parsley and 1 tsp thyme. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a frying pan and gently sauté the onion and garlic. Mix in 3 tbsp breadcrumbs and toast briefly, stirring. Remove the pan from the heat and stir in the herbs and capers. Season the mixture with salt, pepper and 1 pinch of cayenne pepper.
Thinly slice 50g Provolone. Spread the swordfish slices evenly with the herb breadcrumb mixture, lay the cheese slices on top and roll up. Secure the rolls firmly with cocktail sticks.
Lightly oil a grill pan with 1 tbsp olive oil and heat. Place the fish rolls in the pan, drizzle with the remaining oil and cook over a moderate heat, turning, until cooked through (7–8 minutes).

Meanwhile, prepare the sauce. Juice 2 lemons. Finely chop 1/2 bunch of parsley and 1 tsp oregano. Dissolve 1/4 tsp salt in 100ml warm water. Pour 125ml olive oil into a small bowl and set it in a warm water bath. Gradually whisk in the salt water and 7–8 tbsp lemon juice, beating vigorously into the oil. Stir in the herbs and serve alongside the grilled swordfish. Season the fish with freshly ground pepper.
Gegrillte Schwertfischrouladen.tips: Also excellent made with shark steaks.

Gefüllter Rollbraten.name: Stuffed Rolled Roast
ingredients: 1 large beef slice for braising, about 3 cm thick (about 600 g, e.g. cut from the shoulder)
100 g young Provolone cheese (or Mozzarella as a substitute)
steps: Cut the beef horizontally through the middle without cutting all the way through. Open it out, press flat, and pound into a large slice about 1 cm thick.

Tear the crumb of 1 bread roll into tiny pieces. Whisk 1 raw egg with 2 egg yolks and 4 tbsp milk, then soak the breadcrumbs in the mixture. Finely chop the parsley, 2 garlic cloves, and 1 tsp rosemary.

For the filling, place the ground beef and veal forcemeat in a bowl. Add the egg-and-bread mixture, 3 tbsp grated Pecorino, parsley, garlic, rosemary, and 1 tsp thyme. Mix everything thoroughly and season generously with salt and pepper.
Lay the beef slice flat on the work surface. Using dampened hands, spread the filling evenly over it and smooth it out. Leave about 2 cm around the edges clear.
Gefüllter Rollbraten.tips: Sliced thinly, this flavorful meat is also delicious served cold or slightly warm, and makes an excellent small intermediate course or appetizer.

Kalbsschnitzel in Marsala.name: Veal Escalopes in Marsala
steps: Cut each veal escalope in half crosswise and gently pound thinner using the flat side of a meat mallet. Rub both sides of the escalopes lightly with pepper.

Peel 1 garlic clove. In a large, heavy skillet, slowly heat 2 tbsp clarified butter. Add the garlic clove and sauté over low heat for about 2 minutes, stirring. Remove the garlic and increase the heat.
Place the escalopes in the hot fat and fry for about 5 minutes on each side. Remove, place on a warmed serving platter, season lightly with salt, and keep covered and warm.

Pour the fat out of the pan, then deglaze the pan juices with 150 ml Marsala and stir vigorously to loosen any browned bits. Stir in 2 tbsp ice-cold butter, cut into small pieces, and let it melt into the sauce. Season with salt and pepper. Return the escalopes to the finished sauce and heat through completely before serving.
Kalbsschnitzel in Marsala.tips: Garlic lovers can sauté several cloves, let them soften in the sauce, and serve them alongside the meat. Potatoes and vegetables — such as fresh broccoli — make a great side dish.

Fleischpastete (Kalabrien).name: Meat Pie (Calabria)
ingredients: 250 g flour, plus extra for rolling
Olive oil for greasing (for the tin and the dough)
steps: For the dough, dissolve 10 g yeast in 2 tbsp lukewarm water and mix with 2 tbsp flour to make a pre-ferment (poolish). Place the remaining flour in a bowl, make a well in the centre, add salt and the pre-ferment. Cover with a cloth and leave to rest in a warm place for 30 minutes. Then knead with about 125 ml lukewarm water into a smooth, elastic dough. Cover again and leave to rise for about 1 hour, until the dough has roughly doubled in volume.
Meanwhile, prepare the ragù filling: cut the pork neck and liver into 1 cm cubes. Finely chop 1 onion and 2 garlic cloves.
Heat 2 tbsp lard in a wide pot. Briefly brown the liver pieces, then remove them. Add the onion, garlic, and peperoncino to the hot lard and sauté, stirring.

Increase the heat and brown the pork neck cubes. Season with 1 tsp oregano, salt, and pepper. Stir 4 tbsp tomato purée with 4 tbsp water until smooth and pour in. Cover and cook for about 10 minutes. Stir in the liver pieces and braise for a further 10 minutes. Then let the ragù reduce briefly in the uncovered pot. Remove the peperoncino and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.

Preheat the oven to 175°C. Grease a springform tin (26 cm diameter) with olive oil. Roll out two-thirds of the yeast dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of 1/2 cm. Line the tin with the dough sheet, letting the edges overlap the sides. Spoon in the meat ragù and fold the overhanging dough edges inward over the filling.

Roll out the remaining dough into a round sheet and use it to cover the pie, pressing the edges firmly all around to seal. Prick the dough lid several times with a skewer, brush with olive oil, and sprinkle with oregano if desired. Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 50 minutes.
Fleischpastete (Kalabrien).tips: Savory meat pies are also popular in Sicily — for example, pasticcio di carne, made with a pastry of flour, lard, and eggs, and filled with pork, almonds, cinnamon, and pistachios.

Huhn mit Thunfischsauce (Sizilien).name: Chicken with Tuna Sauce (Sicily)
ingredients: 1 can tuna in water, drained (150 g)
steps: Bring 3 litres of water with 1 tsp salt to a boil. Roughly chop 1 carrot, 2 celery stalks, and 1 onion and add them to the water. Pour in the juice of 1 lemon. Rub the chicken or poularde all over with pepper, place it in the boiling water, then reduce to the lowest heat and poach gently until cooked through (1–2 hours depending on the quality of the bird).
Purée the tuna with 1 tbsp capers and 2 tbsp olive oil. Very finely chop the parsley and 1/2 bunch of basil.

In a tall bowl, whisk 1 egg yolk with a pinch of salt and 2 tbsp lemon juice. With the mixer running on a hand-held electric beater, pour in the remaining olive oil in a thin, steady stream to make a mayonnaise.
Stir in the herbs, the tuna mixture, and a few spoonfuls of the poaching broth. Season well with salt, pepper, and more lemon juice if desired.
Remove the chicken from the broth and let it cool slightly. Divide into portions and remove the skin. Arrange on a serving platter and serve the tuna sauce alongside.

Gefüllte Tomaten (Sizilien).name: Stuffed Tomatoes (Sicily)
steps: Wash the tomatoes, slice a lid off the top of each one, and scoop out the seeds with a spoon. Remove the flesh and chop finely. Lightly salt the tomatoes on the inside and place them upside down on a rack to drain.

Drain the anchovies and cut into small pieces. Sprinkle with about 2 tbsp lemon juice. Grate the bread roll into crumbs. Remove the flesh of the 50 g olives from the pits. Finely chop 2 onions, 2 garlic cloves, and 1/2 bunch of parsley.

Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet. Sauté the onions until translucent. Stir in the garlic and parsley and continue cooking gently. Add the chopped anchovies and tomato flesh, bring to a vigorous boil, stirring, and cook until all the liquid has evaporated. Stir in the olive pieces and 2 tbsp capers and cook briefly. Season with pepper and salt if needed, then remove the pan from the heat.
Fill the tomatoes with the mixture, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and grated Pecorino, drizzle with the remaining olive oil, and bake in a preheated oven until golden.
Gefüllte Tomaten (Sizilien).tips: Calabrian cuisine also features its own spicy variations on stuffed tomatoes — traditionally filled with pasta and herbs, or with finely chopped beef, grated Pecorino, breadcrumbs, and parsley. Stuffed tomatoes are a wonderful side dish for grilled meat or fish, but can also be served as a starter or light snack with crusty white bread.

Gefüllte Tomaten.name: Stuffed Tomatoes
steps: Preheat the oven to 175°C and grease a baking dish with 1 tbsp olive oil.
Place the well-drained tomatoes in the dish, opening facing up.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a small skillet and toast 4 tbsp breadcrumbs in it, stirring, until golden.
Mix 3 tbsp grated Pecorino and half of the toasted breadcrumbs with the prepared filling and season well.
Fill the hollowed-out tomatoes with the mixture.
Sprinkle the stuffed tomatoes with the remaining breadcrumbs and 1 tbsp Pecorino, and drizzle with the remaining 2 tbsp olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 30 minutes.
Chop 1/2 bunch of parsley, scatter over the tomatoes, and serve hot.

Paprikagemüse.name: Braised Peppers
steps: Halve the bell peppers, remove the seeds and membranes. Wash the halves and cut each into four pieces.
Briefly blanch the tomatoes, peel, and quarter them. Remove the seeds with a spoon and roughly chop the flesh.
Halve 2 onions and slice thinly. Cut 2 garlic cloves into thin slices.
Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot. Sauté the onions until translucent, then stir in the pepper pieces and garlic and fry briefly.
Drizzle the vegetables with 4 tbsp wine vinegar and bring to a vigorous boil. Season with salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper.
Stir in the peeled tomatoes, reduce the heat, cover the pot, and braise the peppers for about 20 minutes.
The vegetables should be nicely tender but on no account mushy. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Serve hot, warm, or cold as a side dish with meat.
Paprikagemüse.tips: Serve hot, warm, or cold as a side dish with meat.

Bratkartoffeln mit Marsala.name: Pan-Fried Potatoes with Marsala
ingredients: 750 g small potatoes, as uniform in size as possible (waxy variety, new potatoes if available)
steps: Wash and peel the potatoes. Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet and melt 1 tbsp butter in it. Arrange the potatoes in a single layer and fry over medium-low heat for 10 minutes.
Turn the potatoes in the pan, dot with 1 tbsp butter cut into small pieces, and continue frying for about 5 minutes.
Deglaze with 125 ml dry Marsala and bring to a vigorous boil. Then reduce the heat, add another 1 tbsp butter to the pan, and season the potatoes with salt.
Finish cooking covered for 20–25 minutes (depending on the size of the potatoes), turning occasionally.
Scatter fresh basil leaves over the crispy fried potatoes, season with freshly ground pepper, and serve immediately.
Bratkartoffeln mit Marsala.tips: Use only dry Marsala — not sweet varieties. Dry sherry works well as a substitute. The same preparation method works beautifully with other vegetables, such as carrots (carote al marsala).

Gemischter Salat.name: Mixed Salad
steps: Trim the fennel bulb and cut away the tough parts. Slice the fennel heart paper-thin. Set the tender fronds aside.
Halve the bell pepper, remove the seeds and membranes. Wash the pepper halves and cut into thin strips.
Peel the 150 g carrots; wash the 2 zucchini and 4 celery stalks. Cut everything into thin rounds or very fine matchsticks.
Arrange all the salad ingredients in a large bowl. Add the tender celery leaves and fennel fronds.
For the dressing, vigorously whisk together 7 tbsp olive oil, 2–3 tbsp white wine vinegar, salt, and freshly ground pepper; taste and adjust seasoning. Drizzle over the salad and serve immediately.
Gemischter Salat.tips: Use aromatic, first-press olive oil and whisk the vinegar and seasonings together very thoroughly with a whisk. To taste, you can increase the amount of vinegar or add a splash of lemon juice.

Süß-saures Auberginengemüse.name: Sweet and Sour Eggplant
steps: Cut the eggplant into 1 cm cubes and place in a colander. Sprinkle with salt and leave for about 1 hour to draw out the bitterness.

Trim the 3 celery stalks and cut into 3 cm pieces; set the tender leaves aside. Coarsely chop the 200 g onions. Drain the tomatoes and chop them; reserve the tomato juice for another use (e.g. soup). Halve the 50 g olives.

Heat 4 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot. Sauté the onions until translucent. Add the celery pieces and fry for a few minutes. Sprinkle with ½ tsp sugar, season with salt and pepper. Stir in the tomatoes, olives, and 2 tbsp capers. Cook uncovered over the lowest heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Meanwhile, briefly rinse the eggplant cubes and pat dry with paper towels. Heat the remaining olive oil in a frying pan until very hot. Fry the eggplant in batches until crispy. Drain on paper towels.

Add the fried eggplant cubes to the vegetable pot. Pour in 5 tbsp white wine vinegar and let everything simmer gently for another 15 minutes. Season to taste with salt, pepper, and more vinegar if desired.
Serve sprinkled with fresh basil leaves and chopped celery greens.
Süß-saures Auberginengemüse.tips: Tastes especially good served warm. Serve as an appetizer with bread or rice, as a side dish with pan-fried meat, or as a main course garnished with hard-boiled eggs or small squid.

Auberginenröllchen mit Tomatensauce.name: Eggplant Rolls with Tomato Sauce
ingredients: 1 peperoncino (hot chili pepper), or substitute a chili
steps: Wash and trim the eggplants. Slice lengthwise into ½ cm thick slices. Sprinkle the slices with salt on both sides, lay them out side by side on a cloth, and leave for about 1 hour to draw out the bitterness.

Prepare the tomato sauce: Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water, peel, deseed, and finely chop the flesh. Finely chop 1 onion and 2 garlic cloves. Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot, sauté the onion and garlic. Stir in the peperoncino, ⅛ l red wine, and the chopped tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper and let simmer over low heat.

Briefly rinse the eggplant slices and dry them thoroughly. Very finely chop the parsley, basil, and 2 garlic cloves. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil and toast the 3 tbsp breadcrumbs in it, then mix thoroughly with the herbs, garlic, and 4 tbsp grated pecorino. Stir in 2 tbsp olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Cut the mozzarella into thin small slices.
Spread the herb paste evenly over the eggplant slices, top with mozzarella slices, and roll up. Secure with toothpicks.
Brush a grill pan with 3 tbsp olive oil and heat it. Place the eggplant rolls in the pan and fry until crispy all over. Add more oil as needed. Season with freshly ground pepper.
Season the reduced tomato sauce with salt and pepper; add cayenne pepper for extra heat if desired. Remove the peperoncino. Serve the sauce alongside the grilled rolls.
Auberginenröllchen mit Tomatensauce.tips: Great as a side dish with grilled meat or fish. The eggplant rolls can also be served warm as a small appetizer with bread.

Festtagstorte.name: Celebration Cake
steps: Sponge: Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F). Beat 4 egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff. Fold in 120 g sugar, the zest of ½ lemon, 4 egg yolks, and 150 g flour. Line a springform pan (24 cm diameter) with parchment paper. Pour in the batter and smooth the top.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 2) for about 45 minutes. Remove the sponge, turn out onto a wire rack, and let cool completely. Then slice horizontally into 3 layers.

Filling: Beat the ricotta in a bowl until creamy. In a small saucepan, stir 300 g sugar into about ⅛ l water, heat and let the sugar dissolve, simmering into a light syrup. Remove from the heat and let cool slightly. Stir into the ricotta along with 3 cl maraschino liqueur.
Coarsely chop 150 g dark chocolate and 50 g pistachios; dice 150 g candied fruit into small cubes. Fold all ingredients into the ricotta cream.

Place one sponge layer in the springform pan and soak with 1 cl maraschino liqueur. Spread half the ricotta cream over it, smooth the top, and cover with the second sponge layer. Soak with liqueur, add the remaining cream, top with the last sponge layer, and drizzle with the remaining liqueur.
Refrigerate the cake for at least 3 hours until firm. Then carefully release from the pan and transfer to a cake plate.

Whip the cream with 1 tsp vanilla sugar until very stiff. Spread about ¾ of the cream over the entire cake; fill the remainder into a piping bag and pipe a pattern on top. Decoratively arrange 250 g candied fruit and use to garnish the cake. Serve as soon as possible.
Festtagstorte.tips: Ricotta can be substituted with well-drained low-fat quark or curd cheese. Decoration variations: the cake can also be covered with chocolate glaze or simply dusted lightly with powdered sugar.

Fritierte Honigstäbchen.name: Fried Honey Sticks
ingredients: 400 g flour, plus extra for rolling
steps: Finely chop the almonds and toast briefly in a dry pan.
Mix 400 g flour with the spices in a bowl and make a well in the center. Dissolve 20 g yeast in 2 tbsp lukewarm water, then add to the well along with the almonds and 100 g powdered sugar.

Mix everything together, gradually adding about 100 ml lukewarm water until a smooth dough forms. Shape into a ball, cover with a cloth, and leave to rise in a warm place for 1 hour until roughly doubled in size. Keep refrigerated until ready to use.

Knead the dough well on a floured surface, then shape into sticks about 1 cm thick and 3–4 cm long. Heat oil in a pot or deep fryer. Fry the sticks in batches until golden brown. Drain on paper towels, transfer to a plate, drizzle lightly with honey, and serve immediately.
Fritierte Honigstäbchen.tips: In the original recipe, the crispy hot sticks are fully dipped in honey — this is a slightly lighter version.

Gefüllte Pfirsiche.name: Stuffed Peaches
ingredients: 80 g amaretti cookies (small almond macaroons, store-bought or homemade per recipe on page 120)
steps: Blanch the peaches briefly, peel, halve, and remove the pits. Lightly hollow out the fruit halves, place the scooped-out flesh in a bowl, and mash it. Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).

Crumble the amaretti and soak with 2–3 tbsp Marsala. Combine with 1–2 tbsp sugar, 2 tsp lemon juice, and 1 egg yolk in the bowl and mix thoroughly. Fill the mixture into the peach halves and garnish each with one almond.
Place the fruit in a buttered, ovenproof dish, dot with small pieces of butter, and pour in the remaining Marsala.
Bake in the preheated oven (gas mark 3) for about 15 minutes. Dust lightly with powdered sugar.
Gefüllte Pfirsiche.tips: Marsala, the delicious dessert wine, is a product of this island — and here it is not just sipped but enthusiastically added to both sweet and savory dishes. The dry ('secco') version of the wine works best for cooking.

Ricottapudding.name: Ricotta Pudding
ingredients: 400 g ricotta (Italian fresh cheese; well-drained quark can be substituted)
30 g each candied lemon peel and candied orange peel
Butter and flour for the mold
steps: Soak 2 tbsp golden raisins in 2 cl rum; very finely chop 30 g each of candied lemon peel and candied orange peel.
Heat 200 ml milk, sprinkle in 3 tbsp semolina, and stir well. Leave to swell briefly.
Remove from the heat, turn the semolina out onto a cold rinsed plate, and let cool. Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F).
Meanwhile, thoroughly mix the ricotta with 4 tbsp powdered sugar, 1 whole egg, 3 egg yolks, the chopped candied peels, the soaked raisins, ¼ tsp cinnamon, and the zest of 1 lemon.
Beat the remaining 3 egg whites to stiff peaks. Stir the semolina into the ricotta mixture, then gently fold in the egg whites.

Grease a pudding mold (1.5 l capacity) well and dust with flour. Fill in the mixture and smooth the top. Cook the pudding in a warm water bath in the oven (gas mark 2) for about 1 hour. Turn out of the mold, dust with powdered sugar, and serve. Garnish with candied fruit if desired.

Meloneneis.name: Melon Sorbet
steps: Slowly heat 6 tbsp sugar with 6–8 tbsp water, stirring, until the sugar has dissolved. Simmer for a few minutes over gentle heat, then let cool.
Peel and deseed the melon, cut the flesh into pieces, and purée in a blender. Stir in 4–5 tbsp lemon juice.
Mix in the cooled sugar syrup and pour the purée into a freezer-safe container.
Freeze until semi-solid (about 1 hour). Remove and stir vigorously, then return to the freezer and leave until fully firm.
Remove from the freezer about 30 minutes before serving and let soften slightly. Stir vigorously once more, then spoon into individual bowls or decorative glasses.
Meloneneis.tips: A refreshing dessert prepared in many different ways across Sicily: sometimes with watermelon instead of honeydew, sometimes enriched with pine nuts, raisins, candied lemon peel, and cinnamon.
